CampusOS | Design Hub ERD v11 Architecture Functions v11

CampusOS Entity-Relationship Design — Version 11.0 (Build Baseline)

840 Tables · 38 Modules · 76 ADRs · 5 Delivery Waves · March 2026

STATUS: BUILD BASELINE — Implementation-ready. Peer-reviewed. Includes business platform schema. This is the authoritative schema reference for all engineering, architecture, and implementation work. 840 tables in one document — no amendments to cross-reference.

v11 build baseline. Merges v10 frozen ERD (803 tenant-schema tables) with business platform schema (+37 platform tables). No amendments to cross-reference — everything is in this document.

v11 additions over v10: +37 tables (803→840). +2 modules (M90 Customer & Subscriptions, M91 Internal Operations). +6 ADRs (071–076). New platform domains: CRM (crm_ 9 tables), Internal Operations (ops_ 5 tables), Pricing (platform_pricing_ 3 tables), Community Exchange (platform_marketplace_ + platform_community_ + platform_asset_ + platform_search_ 20 tables).

Unchanged from v10: All 803 tenant-schema tables. All 36 original modules. All 70 original ADRs. Module boundaries, identity model, IAM architecture, data lifecycle, and event contracts are preserved exactly. The v11 additions are exclusively in the platform schema.

CampusOS

Entity-Relationship Design

Version 11.0 — Unified Baseline: Journey-Driven Expansion, Module Restructuring, Procurement Forward

March 2026 | 840 Tables | 38 Modules | 76 ADRs | 5 Delivery Waves

84038576
TablesModulesWavesADRs
About this document CampusOS is a cloud-native multi-tenant School Operating System. This document is the complete Entity-Relationship Design — the authoritative schema reference for all engineering, architecture, and implementation work. 840 tables across 38 modules, 76 Architectural Decision Records, and 5 delivery waves are documented here. Every table includes its full schema, indexes, constraints, partitioning strategy, operational rules, Kafka event contracts, and ADR cross-references. The system is greenfield. Nothing described here is a migration from a legacy system. All design decisions — module boundaries, identity model, IAM architecture, data lifecycle, event contracts — are made here for the first time and are intended to be correct as built. Design history: The ERD was developed iteratively from an initial 354-table baseline across 24 modules. Key additions include: IAM subsystem (ADR-036, +21 tables), advanced scheduling (ADR-053, +18 sch_ tables), DPO Compliance Suite (ADR-052, +12 dpo_ tables), M27 Student Services, M103 Groups & Communities, Data Import pipeline, Transportation expansion, Extracurricular, Food Service, Facilities Management, Platform Event Model (ADR-057), universal identity FK contract (ADR-055), Scheduling Engine Contract (ADR-056), M83 Finance minimal GL backbone (ADR-058), Platform Invariants Specification, pre-build architectural fixes (ADR-059), and open question resolutions: multi-type identity, hybrid solver, district analytics (ADR-060). Current total: 840 tables, 38 modules, 76 ADRs. v10 adds +145 tables over v9.2 via the user journey design process (20 personas, 366 sections). Module restructuring: M64 Extracurricular split into M64 Clubs & Student Life + M66 Athletics. M81 Enrollment & Payments split into M81 Enrolment + M84 Payments & Billing. 3 modules renamed (M23, M60, M62). 4 new modules (M66 Athletics, M67 School Store, M84 Payments, M86 Procurement). 10 new ADRs (061–070). Basic Procurement moved from Wave 5 to Wave 2. Identity model: iam_person is the universal human identity record. platform_users is the authentication account. All domain tables reference iam_person for human identity and platform_users only for auth/session/audit context. This contract is locked by ADR-055. Delivery waves: Wave 1 covers the operational core (SIS, Classroom, HR, Communications, Enrollment & Payments, Service Requests, Task Management, Approval Workflows, Calendar). Wave 2 adds operational extensions. Wave 3 adds community and engagement modules. Wave 4 adds Finance & Accounting (M83). Basic Procurement (M86) is now Wave 2 per ADR-061. Full procurement scope (three-way matching, blanket POs) remains post-M83. 76 ADRs (001–076) govern all significant design decisions. Groups: 001–031 core architecture; 032–040 module scope and boundaries; 041–050 scalability; 051–058 compliance, finance backbone, scheduling engine, event model, and identity unification; ADR-059 pre-build architectural fixes; ADR-060 district analytics Wave 3 scope.

Module Numbering Scheme (v11)

Modules are assigned to range-based groups. Each group has a 10–20 slot buffer for future modules. M0 is permanently reserved for Platform Core. Table prefixes (sis_, cls_, etc.) are unchanged from v5.0.

RangeGroupModules AssignedBuffer
M0Platform Core (fixed)M0 only — permanent—
M1–M19Core BackendM1, M2, M316 free
M20–M39Core AcademicM20, M21, M22, M23, M24, M25, M26, M2712 free
M40–M59CommunicationsM40, M41, M4217 free
M60–M79Campus OperationsM60, M61, M62, M63, M64, M65, M66, M6712 free
M80–M89AdministrationM80, M81, M82, M83, M84, M85, M86, M872 free
M90–M99Safety & ComplianceM90, M918 free
M100–M109Community & EngagementM100, M101, M102, M1036 free
M110–M119Data & ReportingM1109 free

Architectural Decision Records — Complete Register (v11)

Complete register: 76 ADRs (001–076). Groups: 001–031 core architecture; 032–040 module scope and boundaries; 041–050 scalability; 051–058 compliance, finance backbone, scheduling engine, event model, and identity unification; 059 pre-build architectural fixes; 060 district analytics; 061–070 v10 expansion (procurement, school store, officials marketplace, vendor accounts, credential vault, device selection, course selection, streaming, cross-school athletics, achievements).

ADRDecisionStatus
001Multi-tenancy: schema-per-tenant PostgreSQL. Max 80 active schemas per cluster (+20 migration buffer). TenantRoutingDataSource reads platform_tenant_routing per-request.ACCEPTED
002Modular monolith → selective microservices. Video Processing, AI Gateway, Notification Service extracted from day one. All other modules co-deployed until justified by scale.ACCEPTED
003Stripe Connect for payments. Card numbers never stored in CampusOS database.ACCEPTED
004AI Gateway abstraction layer. Domain services never call AI provider APIs directly. /summarize-lesson, /grade-submission, /generate-student-summary are the only endpoints.ACCEPTED
005UUIDv7 mandatory for all primary keys. generateId() utility in libs/shared/src/uuid.ts. Never use UUIDv4 on high-volume tables.ACCEPTED
006Redis SET NX idempotency for notification fan-out. No DB UNIQUE constraint on msg_notification_queue.idempotency_key — causes deadlocks at scale.ACCEPTED
007sis_attendance_records: RANGE(school_year DATE) → HASH(class_id) 8 buckets. school_year replaces academic_year_id UUID — UUID bounds cannot be pre-provisioned deterministically.ACCEPTED
008Cluster tiering: operate at 80 active schemas per cluster, provision 20-schema migration buffer. pg_class contention degrades autovacuum beyond 100 schemas.ACCEPTED
009No running_balance on pay_ledger_entries. Compute SELECT SUM on demand, cache in Redis 30s TTL. Route reads to primary for 500ms after any write.ACCEPTED
010cls_gradebook_snapshots: async Kafka consumer with 30s debounce per (class_id, student_id). Never update synchronously inside a grade write transaction.ACCEPTED
011Task Worker is the sole writer to tsk_tasks. No domain module writes tasks directly. Auto-task idempotency via UNIQUE(owner_id, source, source_ref_id) + Redis SET NX.ACCEPTED
012Approval Workflow Engine (WorkflowEngineService) is sole writer to wsk_approval_requests and wsk_approval_steps. Source modules submit via REST POST /api/v1/approvals and listen on approval.request.resolved Kafka event.ACCEPTED
013cal_personal_calendar_items: delta-update pattern only. calendar.event.changed → worker applies targeted row updates. Full rebuild of all user calendar rows is forbidden.ACCEPTED
014Substitute profiles in platform schema. Never copied into tenant schemas. Cross-school visibility via direct platform schema read.ACCEPTED
015Safeguarding check: store third-party reference ID + binary PASSED/FLAGGED result only. Registry data is transient — never persisted.ACCEPTED
016inc_incident_timeline: immutable append-only. No UPDATE or DELETE ever. RLS enforced at DB level.ACCEPTED
017Timetable import: all-or-nothing transactional. Validate all rows for conflicts before committing any row. No partial import state.ACCEPTED
018cur_delivery_gaps: materialised nightly by background job. Never computed on demand.ACCEPTED
019Alumni self-maintained contact data. Alumni portal allows self-service profile updates post-graduation.ACCEPTED
020platform_entity_cache cross-schema FK pattern. Tenant schemas cannot FK-reference platform schema entities at DB level. Application checks cache before any cross-schema reference.ACCEPTED
020-Aplatform_entity_cache: synchronous pre-population at entity provisioning. Cache is complete before first tenant request is served.ACCEPTED
020-BCache operational contract: 60s reconciliation SLA. CacheRepairJob idempotent re-fetch. 409 ENTITY_NOT_SYNCED on miss. BulkEntitySyncJob for >10,000-entity operations (1,000-entity batches, Kafka bypassed). Global reference schema permanently rejected. RLS-as-primary-isolation permanently rejected.ACCEPTED
021— Parent Engagement as standalone module (eng_ prefix). PTC tables extracted from Meetings.ACCEPTED
022— Academic Scheduling as standalone module (sch_ prefix). Timetable tables extracted from Calendar.ACCEPTED
022-Ahr_lesson_observations: class_id FK removed. HR has no compile-time dependency on Classroom schema. observed_class_label is a denormalised snapshot captured at observation time.ACCEPTED
023Platform-seeded curriculum and accreditation standards (CCSS, NGSS, AdvancED, IB) live in platform schema. Per-tenant copies eliminated. One migration updates all schools.ACCEPTED
024pg_partman manages all append-heavy tables. All managed tables monitored in partition_mgmt_health. Alert on retention_drift_days outside ±2.ACCEPTED
025tsk_auto_task_rules.trigger_event_type is ENUM, not raw Kafka topic string. Task Worker owns the ENUM→topic mapping. Kafka renames need no data migration.ACCEPTED
026Four-tier data lifecycle: T1 Hot (PostgreSQL active) / T2 Warm (PostgreSQL cold-schema) / T3 Cold (S3 Parquet + Athena) / T4 Legal Hold (S3 Object Lock WORM). Athena-first for T3. platform_archive_catalog is tier routing authority. Mandatory cost controls on all Athena queries.ACCEPTED
027v1 scope: rotation calendars, constraint solver, exam scheduling, co-teaching, district staff excluded. Two-layer import model (sch_timetable_slots + sch_import_slot_metadata). 7-year FERPA retention.ACCEPTED
028Cross-module reference taxonomy: HARD FK (DB-enforced) / SOFT INTEGRITY (app-layer, registered in platform_reference_health) / DISPLAY-ONLY (no enforcement, column comment required). Platform infrastructure exception: designated workers may do read-only PK lookups across modules.ACCEPTED
029sub.assignment.completed Kafka event schema. HR module never reads sub_* tables directly.ACCEPTED
030sis_student_active_accommodations read model. Classroom module never reads hlth_* tables directly. Health → SIS Kafka consumer maintains the read model.ACCEPTED
031Tenant cluster rebalance: tiered migration (pg_dump for Small/Medium; logical replication for Large/Enterprise). is_frozen write-gate on platform_tenant_routing. All layers check is_frozen. Automation threshold: 200 rebalances/year triggers ClusterPlacementService investment.ACCEPTED
032Volunteer identity as platform-level profile (platform_volunteer_profiles), parallel to ADR-014 (substitutes). VOLUNTEER added to platform_entity_cache entity_type ENUM. Volunteer physical sign-in handled by M90 Visitor Management. Domain modules own their own volunteer assignment tables.ACCEPTED
033M60 Helpdesk (renamed from Service Requests) scope clarification: shared reactive ticketing engine for IT, facilities, and HR support. Not a facilities-specific tool. M65 Facilities Management links to Helpdesk tickets via DISPLAY-ONLY reference for reactive-originated work orders. M60 and M65 are distinct modules with distinct ownership.ACCEPTED
034M65 Facilities Management v1 scope: buildings & spaces, facilities assets, preventive maintenance, work orders, custodial zone assignments, supply inventory, compliance inspections. OUT OF SCOPE: custodian shift scheduling (owned by M80 HR), district-level space sharing, automated maintenance AI scheduling, classroom scheduling (M22), technology assets (M62). HR owns when custodians work; Facilities owns where they are assigned.ACCEPTED
035M42 Publications approval routing: student and parent-created publications route through M2 Approval Workflows (wsk_approval_requests) rather than custom approval logic. Staff-created publications bypass approval. pub_publications.approval_request_id is SOFT INTEGRITY → wsk_approval_requests, NULL for staff-authored publications.ACCEPTED
036IAM subsystem Wave 1: person/account separation (iam_person + platform_users restructured), scoped time-bound role assignments (iam_role_assignment), federated identity (iam_identity_provider + iam_federated_identity), relationship-based access rules (iam_relationship_access_rule), effective access cache (iam_effective_access_cache), machine identity (iam_service_principal + iam_client_credential + iam_api_key), and IAM-specific audit events (iam_access_change_event + iam_auth_event). Authentication delegated to external IdP — CampusOS authorises, IdP authenticates. Guardian access always derived from relationship rules, never implicit. iam_role_assignment is the sole access grant mechanism. M0 IAM subsystem: 62 tables.ACCEPTED
037Long-term FK anchor contract: three FK categories — (1) FK(iam_person) for human identity; (2) FK(platform_users) for auth/session/audit; (3) domain projection IDs for role context. Superseded and completed by ADR-055 which locked the final state across all modules.ACCEPTED
038Finance & Accounting boundary: M81 owns AR (invoices, payments, family accounts). M83 owns GL, budgets, and AP. No shared tables. M81 emits Kafka events consumed by M83 for GL posting. fin_suppliers pulled forward to Wave 2 per ADR-051.ACCEPTED
039Master data governance: identity survivorship rules, canonical entity ownership, cross-module data stewardship, domain projection lifecycle.ACCEPTED
040Platform Data Import subsystem (6 tables): transactional import pipeline for SIS/HR/enrollment data from external systems. Import is all-or-nothing per ADR-017.ACCEPTED
041platform schema HASH partitioning: iam_person HASH 32 buckets, platform_students HASH 32 buckets, platform_users HASH 32 buckets (email domain). Online migration via pg_partman 5-step procedure.ACCEPTED
042Tenant routing Redis cache: 500ms TTL on platform_tenant_routing reads. TenantRoutingDataSource reads cache first; falls back to DB on miss.ACCEPTED
043IAM cache pre-warming: IAMCachePrewarmWorker pre-warms iam_effective_access_cache on LOGIN and ROLE_CHANGE events. Target: p95 < 200ms, cache hit rate > 0.99 after pre-warm.ACCEPTED
044Per-tenant connection pooling: PgBouncer in TRANSACTION mode. platform_connection_pool_config governs pool size per cluster. platform_connection_pool_health monitors utilisation.ACCEPTED
045Noisy-neighbor detection: platform_tenant_usage_metrics tracks hourly API/DB/Kafka metrics. NoisyNeighborDetector flags tenants at 2x rolling median. platform_quota_breach_events triggers throttle/isolate actions.ACCEPTED
046Kafka consumer isolation by tier: SMALL/MEDIUM tenants share consumer groups. LARGE/ENTERPRISE tenants get dedicated consumer groups per topic. Configured via platform_tenant_quotas.kafka_consumer_isolation.ACCEPTED
047Messaging partition scale-up: msg_threads HASH partition increased from 16 to 64 buckets. msg_messages RANGE monthly. Online migration via pg_partman.ACCEPTED
048Financial ledger archival: pay_ledger_entries RANGE partition annual via pg_partman. 7-year T1 retention, then T3 S3 Parquet. Ledger balance checkpoints prevent full-table scans.ACCEPTED
049Analytics incremental snapshots: rpt_rebuild_snapshots enables FROM_SNAPSHOT rebuilds. Prevents full Kafka replay on analytics read model corruption. SLA: < 10 minutes for FROM_SNAPSHOT rebuild.ACCEPTED
050Read replica routing: platform_read_replica_routing governs which modules use replicas. Analytics and reporting route to replica. Write path always uses primary. 500ms primary-sticky window after writes (ADR-009).ACCEPTED
051fin_suppliers and fin_supplier_contacts pulled forward from Wave 4 to Wave 2. Finance Administrators need supplier address book before GL is built. default_gl_account_code is a TEXT placeholder until Wave 4 FK migration.ACCEPTED
052DPO Compliance Suite (12 tables): dpo_ prefix. SAR lifecycle (30/45-day deadlines), erasure requests, processing restrictions, portability requests, breach records (72-hour notification), ROPA, retention policies, privacy notices, consent records, processor register, DPAs, DPIAs.ACCEPTED
053M22 Advanced Scheduling scope expansion: rotation calendars, automated constraint-solver scheduling, exam scheduling, co-teaching, pull-out interventions, cross-school staff, complex cover. Each item previously excluded by ADR-027 v1 scope is now formally in scope.ACCEPTED
054Wave 5 Procurement & Purchasing added to roadmap. SUPERSEDED by ADR-061: basic procurement moved to Wave 2 as M86 (prefix prc_). Full scope remains post-M83.SUPERSEDED
055Universal human identity FK contract: iam_person is the canonical FK target for all domain human identity references. platform_users reserved for auth/session/audit only. hr_employees, sis_students, sis_guardians are domain projections — role-context FKs to these tables are correct and not subject to replacement. See ADR-055 for complete table list.ACCEPTED
056Scheduling Engine Contract: solver is a stateless external service. Hard constraints enforced by DB (EXCLUSION CONSTRAINTs). Soft constraints scored and minimised. Promotion lifecycle: DRAFT → REVIEWED → APPROVED → ACTIVE. Rebuild triggers defined. Conflict resolution: three options per clash. Cross-school constraints coordinated by SchedulingWorker before solver invocation.ACCEPTED
057Platform Event Model: canonical event envelope (event_id, event_type, event_version, occurred_at, published_at, tenant_id, source_module, correlation_id, payload). Topic naming: {env}.{domain}.{entity}.{verb}. Versioning: minor = backward-compatible; major = new topic. Idempotency via per-consumer event_id table. DLQ on three failures. Event ownership: one module per event type.ACCEPTED
058M83 Finance minimal GL backbone (7 tables): fin_chart_of_accounts, fin_funds, fin_accounting_periods, fin_journal_batches, fin_gl_entries, fin_budgets, fin_budget_lines. M81 → M83 integration is event-driven (pay.invoice.created etc. consumed by M83 journal posting worker). All fin_gl_entries rows IMMUTABLE once batch is POSTED.ACCEPTED
059Pre-build architectural fixes (8 fixes): fin_gl_entries partition key, fin_journal_batches.is_balanced removal, platform_event_consumer_idempotency, iam_scope_membership removal, GDPR erasure for immutable tables, person.identity.updated event, fac.space events, platform_families. See ADR-059 scope box for details.ACCEPTED
060District Analytics: DistrictAnalyticsWorker nightly aggregation of school-level rpt_ snapshots into rpt_district_summary and rpt_district_school_comparison. Hybrid scheduling solver: CP-SAT ≤300 sections, heuristic >300 sections.ACCEPTED
061Basic Procurement Scope (Wave 2): M86 created with prefix prc_. Supersedes ADR-054 Wave 5 placement. Basic scope: requisitions, POs, receiving, distribution, vendor management. Full scope (three-way matching, blanket POs, encumbrance) deferred to post-M83.ACCEPTED
062School Store Module: M67 created with prefix str_. STUDENT store (parent approval, family account billing) and PUBLIC store (external customers, shipping). Inventory linked to prc_ for replenishment. Backorder: payment deferred until received.ACCEPTED
063Officials Marketplace: platform_official_profiles/credentials/availability in M0. ath_official_assignments/ratings in M66. Follows substitute marketplace pattern (ADR-029). Cross-school sharing via platform entity cache (ADR-020).ACCEPTED
064Vendor Account Identity Model: platform_vendor_accounts, platform_vendor_account_members, iam_vendor_access_grants. Scoped, time-limited access. No student data. Auto-revoke on contract expiry. Full audit.ACCEPTED
065Credential Vault: tech_credential_vault with AES-256 (separate key from student data). Per-credential access audit via tech_credential_access_log. Tiered access: STANDARD, ELEVATED, CRITICAL. Rotation alerts at 60/30/14/3 days.ACCEPTED
066Device Selection Workflow: tech_device_options + tech_device_selections with parent/supervisor approval. School-configurable via feature flags. Integrates with enrolment and HR onboarding.ACCEPTED
067Course Selection Lifecycle: sch_course_catalogue_entries, sch_student_course_selections (alternates, parent approval, counsellor review), sch_teacher_recommendations, sch_add_drop_requests. Extends ADR-053.ACCEPTED
068Athletic Event Streaming: ath_game_streams via Video Processing Service (ADR-002). Access per event: PUBLIC/SCHOOL_ONLY/BOTH_SCHOOLS/COACHES_ONLY. ath_highlight_clips for portfolio. Athletic events = public performances, photo privacy flag N/A.ACCEPTED
069CampusOS-to-CampusOS Athletic Integration: ath_game_proposals for cross-school scheduling. Roster sharing, official sharing, away-school ticketing, shared results. Cross-tenant via platform entity cache (ADR-020).ACCEPTED
070Achievements System: cls_achievement_definitions, cls_student_achievements, cls_student_points (append-only), cls_student_streaks, cls_achievement_rewards, cls_reward_redemptions. School-optional via feature flags. No academic impact.ACCEPTED

Module Summary — All 38 Modules (v11)

5 delivery waves. v10 restructuring preserved: M64 split, M81 split, 3 renames, 4 new modules, procurement Wave 2. v11 adds M90 Customer & Subscriptions + M91 Internal Operations.

| Foundation |

0Platform Coreplatform_ + iam_81Foundation — 74 tables: platform_(38) + iam_(20, scope_membership removed per ADR-059) + infrastructure(16 incl. platform_event_consumer_idempotency, platform_families, platform_family_members): organisations, schools, roles, permissions, role_permissions, audit_log, grading_scales, school_feature_flags, school_notification_policies, custom_field_definitions, custom_field_values, partition_mgmt_health, compliance_ferpa_requests

TOTAL658
M0Platform Coreprefix: platform_ + iam_81 tablesFoundation

The platform schema is shared across all tenants and accessible from every tenant schema via search_path. Platform tables hold cross-tenant identity, configuration, and infrastructure. They are never duplicated into tenant schemas except through explicit read model or cache patterns (ADR-020).

### Identity & Tenancy

TableDefinition
organisationsid UUID PK(v7), name TEXT NOT NULL, country_code CHAR(2), org_type ENUM(DISTRICT,MAT,INDEPENDENT_GROUP), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Represents districts or multi-academy trusts that own multiple schools. Organisation-level aggregation uses rpt_district_summary — never cross-schema JOINs.
schoolsid UUID PK(v7), organisation_id UUID FK(organisations), name TEXT NOT NULL, subdomain TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, country_code CHAR(2) NOT NULL, timezone TEXT NOT NULL, plan_tier ENUM(SMALL,MEDIUM,LARGE,ENTERPRISE) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SMALL', schema_name TEXT NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Primary tenant entity. INDEX(subdomain) for tenant resolution at request time.
platform_usersid UUID PK(v7), email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, display_name TEXT, avatar_url TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, mfa_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Hash-partitioned: HASH(split_part(email,'@',2)) 32 BUCKETS (ADR-041 — increased from 16; large enterprise customers create hot partitions at 16 buckets; online migration via pg_partman). Redis permission cache (5-min TTL) populated on login — never hit DB for permissions on every API request.
platform_tenant_routingid UUID PK(v7), tenant_id UUID FK(schools) NOT NULL UNIQUE, cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL, schema_name TEXT NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, is_frozen BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, is_migrating BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, migrated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, max_connections_pool INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 10 (ADR-044 — constrains PgBouncer pool size per tenant; ENTERPRISE tier up to 50; ConfigSyncWorker regenerates pgbouncer.ini on change). ADR-031. Read per-request by TenantRoutingDataSource — never cached in-process. is_frozen=true: API returns 503 WRITE_FROZEN for writes; workers re-queue with 5s delay; Kafka consumers pause that tenant's messages only; scheduled jobs skip and reschedule 60s later.
platform_studentsid UUID PK(v7), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL UNIQUE (links this student record to the universal iam_person identity — platform_students retains its own table for student portability across school transfers, but all PII fields are authoritative in iam_person; first_name/last_name/date_of_birth are kept here as a denormalised snapshot for zero-latency student lookup without joining to iam_person), first_name TEXT NOT NULL, last_name TEXT NOT NULL, date_of_birth DATE, national_id_hash TEXT (HMAC blind index), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, data_subject_is_self BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (set to true when student turns 18 — at this point the student holds their own data subject rights under GDPR/FERPA and the platform transfers control from parent/guardian account to the student account; DataSubjectTransferWorker sets this flag and emits dpo.data_subject.transferred Kafka event triggering: guardian portal_access revoked unless student explicitly grants it, compliance_ferpa_requests going forward require student authorisation not parent, SAR responses sent to student not guardian), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Student identity projection — carries denormalised PII snapshot for zero-latency lookup. sis_students.platform_student_id FK(platform_students) references this record. Authoritative human identity is iam_person; platform_students.person_id FK(iam_person) is the link. Portable across school transfers — this record persists when a student moves schools; sis_students records are school-scoped. PARTITION BY HASH(id) 32 BUCKETS (ADR-041). SYNC: when iam_person is updated, the platform emits a person.identity.updated Kafka event (ADR-059); IdentityProjectionSyncWorker consumes it and updates first_name, last_name, date_of_birth on this record to keep the snapshot current.
platform_entity_cacheUNIQUE(entity_type, platform_entity_id). id UUID PK(v7), entity_type ENUM(STUDENT,SUBSTITUTE,VOLUNTEER) NOT NULL [VOLUNTEER added ADR-032], platform_entity_id UUID NOT NULL, cached_name TEXT NOT NULL, version_hash TEXT NOT NULL, cached_at TIMESTAMPTZ. ADR-020/020-A/020-B. Per-tenant cache for cross-schema FK resolution. Maintained by Kafka consumers for platform.student.updated, platform.substitute.updated, and platform.volunteer.updated. On miss → 409 ENTITY_NOT_SYNCED.

### Volunteer Identity (ADR-032)

TableDefinition
platform_volunteer_profilesid UUID PK(v7), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL UNIQUE (the volunteer's human identity), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (may not have a platform login account — volunteers sometimes managed without portal access), phone TEXT, background_check_status ENUM(PENDING,PASSED,FLAGGED,EXPIRED), background_check_ref TEXT (third-party reference ID only — registry data is transient, never persisted — ADR-015 pattern), background_check_date DATE, certifications JSONB (array of {type, issued_by, expiry_date}), availability_notes TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Never copied into tenant schemas. Cross-school visibility via direct platform schema read. Follows ADR-014 (substitute) pattern exactly.

### Substitute Identity (Platform-Level — ADR-014)

TableDefinition
platform_familiesCross-tenant family identity record — the platform-level anchor for a family unit that may have members at multiple schools within a district or MAT (ADR-059). Parallel to platform_students. id UUID PK(v7), family_display_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Johnson-Okafor Family' — editable by primary guardian or admin), organisation_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → organisations.id nullable — the district or MAT this family belongs to; null for standalone school families), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. PARTITION BY HASH(id) 16 BUCKETS. INDEX(organisation_id) WHERE organisation_id IS NOT NULL. This record is created once at platform level and referenced by sis_families.platform_family_id in each tenant schema. It enables cross-school sibling detection and district-level family communications without cross-schema JOINs.
platform_family_membersLinks iam_person records to a platform_families record — the cross-tenant membership list (ADR-059). id UUID PK(v7), family_id UUID FK(platform_families) NOT NULL, person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL, member_role ENUM(STUDENT,GUARDIAN,PARENT,STEP_PARENT,GRANDPARENT,FOSTER_PARENT,OTHER_ADULT) NOT NULL, is_primary_contact BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (one per family for district communications), joined_at DATE nullable, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. UNIQUE(family_id, person_id). INDEX(person_id, is_active). INDEX(family_id, member_role). To find all siblings across schools: SELECT pfm.person_id FROM platform_family_members pfm WHERE pfm.family_id = (SELECT family_id FROM platform_family_members WHERE person_id = $1) AND pfm.member_role = 'STUDENT' AND pfm.person_id != $1 AND pfm.is_active = true. Populated by the family provisioning workflow and kept in sync by the FamilyMemberSyncWorker on sis_family_members INSERT/UPDATE events.
platform_substitute_profilesid UUID PK(v7), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL UNIQUE (the substitute's human identity — iam_person is the canonical record for name, DOB; platform_users is their auth account), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL UNIQUE (their login account — separate from identity per ADR-055), first_name TEXT NOT NULL (DENORMALISED SNAPSHOT from iam_person — kept here for zero-latency directory search without joining to iam_person; iam_person is authoritative per ADR-055), last_name TEXT NOT NULL (same denormalisation note as first_name), phone TEXT, bio TEXT nullable (substitute-authored professional summary visible to schools in the directory), profile_photo_s3_key TEXT nullable (signed URL on access), preferred_subjects TEXT[] nullable (e.g. {'Mathematics','Science'} — used by matching engine to prioritise relevant postings), preferred_grade_levels TEXT[] nullable (e.g. {'Year 7','Year 8','Year 9'}), max_travel_distance_km INT nullable (matching engine filters postings beyond this radius if home coordinates are set), home_latitude NUMERIC(9,6) nullable, home_longitude NUMERIC(9,6) nullable, preferred_school_types TEXT[] nullable (e.g. {'Primary','Secondary'} — filters matching engine results), is_searchable BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (when false: substitute does not appear in school directory searches — useful when taking an extended break without deactivating), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Never copied into tenant schemas. Cross-school visibility via direct platform schema read. Note: avg_rating column REMOVED — computed on demand: SELECT AVG(overall_score) FROM sub_ratings WHERE ratee_sub_id=$1, cached in Redis TTL=1h.
platform_sub_credentialsid UUID PK(v7), substitute_id UUID FK(platform_substitute_profiles), credential_type TEXT, issued_by TEXT, expiry_date DATE, verification_status ENUM(PENDING,VERIFIED,EXPIRED,REVOKED). INDEX(substitute_id, credential_type, verification_status, expiry_date). Partial index WHERE verification_status='VERIFIED' for matching engine. Alert job: 60 days and 30 days before expiry.
platform_sub_preferencesSubstitute teacher preferences for specific schools — preferred or blocked. id UUID PK(v7), substitute_id UUID FK(platform_substitute_profiles) NOT NULL, school_id UUID NOT NULL (SOFT INTEGRITY to schools.id), preference_type ENUM(PREFERRED,BLOCKED) NOT NULL (PREFERRED: substitute wants to work here and should be prioritised in notifications; BLOCKED: substitute will not accept postings from this school — matching engine excludes them), reason TEXT nullable (private — visible to substitute only, not the school). UNIQUE(substitute_id, school_id). INDEX(substitute_id, preference_type).
platform_sub_availabilityid UUID PK(v7), substitute_id UUID FK, availability_type ENUM(RECURRING,SPECIFIC,BLOCKED), day_of_week SMALLINT, specific_date DATE, start_time TIME, end_time TIME. UNIQUE(substitute_id, availability_type, day_of_week, specific_date). BLOCKED type overrides RECURRING via NOT EXISTS subquery.

### Roles, Permissions & Memberships

These tables define the permission vocabulary. Governed by the IAM subsystem (ADR-036). iam_role_assignment is the sole mechanism for granting permissions — these are reference data, not runtime access controls. Hot-path permission checks use iam_effective_access_cache only.

ADR-055 FK CONTRACT NOTE: domain tables that FK to hr_employees, sis_students, or sis_guardians are using the ROLE-CONTEXT category (category 3 of ADR-055) — not a violation of the iam_person contract. For example, sch_timetable_slots.teacher_id FK(hr_employees) is correct because it references the teacher ROLE, not the human identity. iam_person.id is used when the reference is to a human being regardless of their role. These two patterns are mutually exclusive and both valid.

TableDefinition
rolesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID (null = system role), name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, is_system BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. UNIQUE(school_id, name). Platform system roles seeded at provisioning. Schools create custom roles (e.g. Department Head).
permissionsid UUID PK(v7), code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, resource TEXT NOT NULL, action TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT. Resource:action pairs. Examples: grade:write, student_record:read, leave_request:approve, lesson_observation:write. All @PreAuthorize annotations check permissions, not roles.
role_permissionsUNIQUE(role_id, permission_id). Junction table mapping roles to permission sets.

### Configuration & Operational Infrastructure

TableDefinition
platform_tenant_configsUNIQUE(school_id, config_key). config_value JSONB. Always served from Redis cache (5-min TTL). Never read from DB per-request. Keys include: ai_features_enabled, max_ai_tokens_per_day, sms_enabled, archive_monthly_budget_usd, mfa_required_for_teachers.
platform_push_tokensid UUID PK(v7), platform_user_id UUID FK, device_id TEXT, platform ENUM(IOS,ANDROID,WEB), token TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(platform_user_id, device_id). On new token: set old is_active=false, insert new row.
platform_dlq_messagestopic TEXT, partition INT, offset BIGINT, payload JSONB, error_message TEXT, retry_count INT, first_failed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_failed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Dead letter queue after 3 consumer retry failures.
platform_kafka_consumer_healthUNIQUE(consumer_group, topic, partition). RANGE partition on recorded_at monthly (pg_partman). Fields: consumer_group, topic, partition INT, committed_offset BIGINT, log_end_offset BIGINT, lag BIGINT. Alert: lag > 1,000 any partition. Tighter for msg.message.posted: lag > 500 = WARNING, > 2,000 sustained 2 min = PAGE.
audit_logid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, actor_id UUID, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id UUID, data_subject_id UUID nullable (the platform_users.id or platform_students.id of the person whose data was accessed — populated for all PII access events; enables cross-module DPO query: SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE data_subject_id=$1 ORDER BY created_at DESC), action_category ENUM(READ,WRITE,DELETE,EXPORT,SHARE,LOGIN,ADMIN) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'READ' (enables DPO to filter audit_log by category — e.g. all EXPORT events across all actors), metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. RLS enforced. RANGE partition by created_at monthly. Retention: T1 12 months → T2 7 years → T3 S3 Parquet (FERPA minimum). Records every access to student PII, health records, and financial data. INDEX(data_subject_id, created_at DESC) WHERE data_subject_id IS NOT NULL — cross-module DPO subject access query.
platform_signature_requestsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, document_type TEXT, document_ref_id UUID, signer_id UUID, status ENUM(PENDING,SIGNED,DECLINED,EXPIRED), signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, signature_provider_ref TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Reused across HR contracts, device agreements, telehealth consent, field trip consent, IEP sign-off.
grading_scalesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT, scale_type ENUM(LETTER,NUMERIC,PASS_FAIL), entries JSONB (array of {label, min_pct, max_pct, gpa_points}). School-configured; referenced by cls_assignments and cls_grades.
school_feature_flagsPer-school feature toggle table. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK(schools) NOT NULL, feature_key TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. STUDENT_TO_STUDENT_MESSAGING, WELLBEING_CHECKINS, PUBLICATIONS_MODULE, PORTFOLIO_MODULE, STUDENT_PORTAL_LOGIN, PARENT_ABSENCE_REPORTING, AI_LESSON_SUMMARIES, TELEHEALTH, ALUMNI_PORTAL), is_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, enabled_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, enabled_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, config_json JSONB nullable (optional feature-specific configuration), notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(school_id, feature_key). Platform migrations seed default rows for all known feature_key values for every school on provisioning. Schools cannot create new feature_key values. School admin can toggle is_enabled for keys within their permission set.
school_notification_policiesPer-school notification configuration. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK(schools) NOT NULL UNIQUE, quiet_hours_start TIME nullable, quiet_hours_end TIME nullable, quiet_hours_timezone TEXT nullable, emergency_escalation_channels TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{PUSH,SMS,EMAIL}' (always bypasses quiet hours), default_announcement_channels TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{IN_APP,EMAIL}', parent_messaging_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, student_messaging_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, digest_frequency ENUM(DAILY,WEEKLY,DISABLED) DEFAULT 'DAILY', updated_by UUID FK(platform_users), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Platform seeds defaults at provisioning.
custom_field_definitionsUNIQUE(school_id, entity_type, field_key). entity_type ENUM(STUDENT,EMPLOYEE,INCIDENT,...), field_type ENUM(TEXT,NUMBER,DATE,BOOLEAN,SELECT), label TEXT, is_required BOOLEAN.
custom_field_valuesUNIQUE(entity_id, field_definition_id). value JSONB. INDEX(entity_id, field_definition_id).
platform_user_preferencesUNIQUE(platform_user_id, pref_key). pref_value JSONB. Notification channels, language, theme. Served from Redis cache (5-min TTL).

### Archive & Reference Infrastructure (ADRs 026–028)

TableDefinition
platform_archive_catalogid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, table_name TEXT NOT NULL, tier ENUM(T1,T2,T3,T4) NOT NULL, data_from DATE NOT NULL, data_to DATE NOT NULL, storage_location TEXT (S3 URI prefix for T3/T4; null for T1/T2), partition_version INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 (incremented on any T3 write — included in Athena result cache key SHA-256), row_count_estimate BIGINT, is_under_legal_hold BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(school_id, table_name, tier, data_from). Archive API performs a single SELECT here before routing any T3 query. ADR-026.
platform_archive_query_logid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, requested_by UUID FK(platform_users), query_type ENUM(FERPA_AUDIT,COMPLIANCE_EXPORT,ANALYTICS_TREND,LEGAL_DISCOVERY), template_name TEXT, date_range DATERANGE, estimated_bytes_scanned BIGINT, actual_bytes_scanned BIGINT, estimated_cost_usd NUMERIC(8,4), cache_hit BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, execution_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, created_at DESC). Monthly school cost reports generated from this table.
platform_archive_query_templatesUNIQUE(template_name). sql_template TEXT NOT NULL, max_date_range_days INT NOT NULL, cost_tier_override ENUM(CONFIRM,DUAL_AUTH,BLOCK), description TEXT. All T3 queries must reference a registered template. No ad-hoc SQL. New templates require SRE + compliance team approval. ADR-026.
platform_legal_holdsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, entity_type TEXT (STUDENT,EMPLOYEE,INCIDENT,MESSAGE,TIMETABLE_SLOT,...), entity_id UUID, reason TEXT NOT NULL, requested_by UUID FK(platform_users), requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ (null=indefinite), released_by JSONB ({legal_contact_id, platform_admin_id} — two-party release required), released_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, entity_type, entity_id). PARTIAL INDEX WHERE released_at IS NULL. Archive pipeline checks this table before any partition detach or row migration. ADR-026.
platform_reference_healthUNIQUE(school_id, table_name, column_name). table_name TEXT, column_name TEXT, reference_type ENUM(SOFT_INTEGRITY), target_table TEXT (from platform_reference_registry), default_sample_fraction NUMERIC(4,3) DEFAULT 0.05, last_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_success_rate NUMERIC(5,4), unresolvable_count INT, checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Written nightly by SoftIntegrityVerificationWorker. Alerts: < 0.99 = WARNING; < 0.95 = ALERT; 0.0 for 24h = PAGE. ADR-028.
platform_reference_registryUNIQUE(table_name, schema_name). table_name TEXT, schema_name TEXT, pk_column TEXT DEFAULT 'id', pk_type TEXT DEFAULT 'UUID', owning_module TEXT NOT NULL, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_verified_accessible_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Populated by Flyway migration hook on table creation. CI rejects DROP TABLE or ALTER TABLE RENAME without corresponding UPDATE here in the same migration batch. ADR-028.

### Platform Standards (ADR-023)

TableDefinition
platform_event_consumer_idempotencyKafka event idempotency store for all event consumers (ADR-057, ADR-059). Prevents duplicate processing on Kafka retry or replay. id UUID PK(v7), consumer_name TEXT NOT NULL (the logical name of the consumer service — e.g. 'gl_posting_worker', 'iam_access_worker', 'notification_worker'), event_id UUID NOT NULL (the event_id from the ADR-057 event envelope — UUIDv7 globally unique per event), processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). UNIQUE(consumer_name, event_id) — the idempotency gate: INSERT succeeds → process the event; INSERT fails (duplicate) → skip and acknowledge. RANGE partition by processed_at MONTHLY via pg_partman, 90-day retention (authentication events retention pattern — consumers that need longer audit trails maintain their own domain-specific event logs). INDEX(consumer_name, event_id) — hot path lookup. NOTE: this table lives in the platform schema and is shared across all consumers. It is NOT tenant-scoped — event_id is globally unique per ADR-057 (UUIDv7) so consumer_name + event_id is sufficient without tenant_id. Consumer teams must use their service name consistently as consumer_name.
partition_mgmt_healthUNIQUE(schema_name, table_name). current_partition_count INT, oldest_partition DATE, newest_partition DATE, retention_months INT, retention_drift_days INT, last_maintenance_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Populated by pg_partman maintenance job. Alert: retention_drift_days outside ±2 days (SRE page).
acc_frameworks_platformPlatform-seeded accreditation frameworks (AdvancED, CIS, IB, etc.). id UUID PK(v7), name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, body TEXT, region TEXT, version TEXT. Updated by platform migrations only — never by tenant code. Schools reference via acc_school_framework_adoptions in tenant schema.
acc_standards_platformUNIQUE(framework_id, code). GIN index on to_tsvector(code││' '││description) for keyword search. Updated by platform migrations only.
cur_standards_frameworks_platformPlatform-seeded curriculum frameworks (Common Core, NGSS, national standards). A CCSS update now requires one migration against the platform schema, not 1,000 tenant migrations. Schools adopt via cur_school_framework_adoptions.
cur_standards_platformUNIQUE(framework_id, code). GIN(to_tsvector('english', code││' '││description)) for standards keyword search. Updated by platform migrations only.
Four-Tier Data Lifecycle (ADR-026) T1 Hot — PostgreSQL partitioned, active. All application UI. <5ms. T2 Warm — PostgreSQL cold-schema (detached partition, same cluster). Archive API endpoint, compliance queries. <50ms. T3 Cold — S3 Parquet queried via Athena SDK only. Compliance API, SRE only. Never application UI. 30–120s. Cost controls mandatory. T4 Legal Hold — S3 + S3 Object Lock (WORM). platform_legal_holds gates all deletes. Two-party release: school legal contact + CampusOS platform admin. Athena controls: mandatory date_range, registered templates only, 50GB MaxBytesScanned hard cap, dry-run tiers (<10GB proceed / 10–100GB confirm / 100–500GB dual-auth / >500GB blocked), Redis result cache keyed on SHA-256(params+partition_version), monthly budget per plan tier.
M1Task Managementprefix: tsk_6 tablesWave 1

Task Worker is the SOLE writer to tsk_tasks. No domain module writes tasks directly (ADR-011). Auto-task idempotency enforced via UNIQUE(owner_id, source, source_ref_id) + Redis SET NX.

TableDefinition
tsk_tasksRANGE(created_at) monthly via pg_partman. UNIQUE(owner_id, source, source_ref_id) WHERE source != 'MANUAL' — auto-task deduplication. INDEX(owner_id, status, due_at) on each active partition. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, owner_id UUID FK(platform_users), title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, source ENUM(MANUAL,AUTO,SYSTEM), source_ref_id UUID, priority ENUM(LOW,NORMAL,HIGH,URGENT), status ENUM(TODO,IN_PROGRESS,DONE,CANCELLED), due_at TIMESTAMPTZ, task_category ENUM(ACADEMIC,PERSONAL,ADMINISTRATIVE,ACKNOWLEDGEMENT) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PERSONAL' (drives To-Do screen grouping — ACADEMIC = assignment-related; PERSONAL = student/parent-created; ADMINISTRATIVE = forms, policies; ACKNOWLEDGEMENT = requires sign-off), acknowledgement_id UUID FK(tsk_acknowledgements) nullable (populated only when task_category = ACKNOWLEDGEMENT — points to the specific acknowledgement record the student must action), created_for_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (when set: the task was created BY another user FOR this account — e.g. a teacher creating a task for a specific student. The task appears in the created_for user's To-Do list; the creating user (owner_id) can see its status. INDEX(created_for_id, status) WHERE created_for_id IS NOT NULL.). Background archiver: moves status IN (DONE,CANCELLED) AND updated_at < now()-30 days to tsk_tasks_archive. Runs nightly.
tsk_task_tagsUNIQUE(task_id, tag). GIN index on tag for filter queries.
tsk_task_commentsid UUID PK(v7), task_id UUID FK, author_id UUID FK, body TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(task_id, created_at DESC).
tsk_tasks_archiveRANGE(created_at) annual partition. Same schema as tsk_tasks. No FK back to tsk_tasks. INDEX(owner_id, completed_at DESC) — serves completion-date queries across partitions. TaskHistoryRepository routes: <30 days → tsk_tasks (T1), 30d–3yr → tsk_tasks_archive (T2), >3yr → S3 Parquet via compliance endpoint (T3). ADR-026.
tsk_acknowledgementsLightweight certification record for student or parent sign-off. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, subject_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (the student or parent who must acknowledge), source_type ENUM(ANNOUNCEMENT,DISCIPLINE_RECORD,POLICY_DOCUMENT,SIGNED_FORM,CONSENT_REQUEST,CUSTOM) NOT NULL, source_ref_id UUID NOT NULL (SOFT INTEGRITY → the source entity: msg_announcements.id, sis_discipline_incidents.id, ext_consent_records.id, etc.), source_table TEXT NOT NULL (DISPLAY-ONLY — table name for human-readable audit — ADR-028), title TEXT NOT NULL (the acknowledgement prompt shown to the student/parent, e.g. 'I have read and understood the behaviour incident report dated 12 March'), body_s3_key TEXT nullable (optional full document to read before acknowledging — signed URL on read), requires_dispute_option BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: student can select 'I acknowledge receipt but dispute the content'), status ENUM(PENDING,ACKNOWLEDGED,ACKNOWLEDGED_WITH_DISPUTE,EXPIRED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, dispute_reason TEXT nullable (populated when student selects dispute), created_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (teacher or admin who required the acknowledgement), expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (after which status becomes EXPIRED and escalation task fires for teacher), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(subject_id, status). INDEX(source_type, source_ref_id). On INSERT: Task Worker creates corresponding tsk_tasks row with task_category=ACKNOWLEDGEMENT and acknowledgement_id pointing to this record. On status → ACKNOWLEDGED or ACKNOWLEDGED_WITH_DISPUTE: emit student.acknowledgement.completed Kafka event consumed by originating module.
tsk_auto_task_rulesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, trigger_event_type ENUM(GRADE_POSTED,LEAVE_APPROVED,STUDENT_ENROLLED,INCIDENT_DECLARED,ASSIGNMENT_OVERDUE,ASSIGNMENT_POSTED,GRADE_RETURNED,ANNOUNCEMENT_REQUIRES_ACKNOWLEDGEMENT,DISCIPLINE_PENDING_STUDENT_SIGN_OFF,CONSENT_REQUESTED,CHECKOUT_OVERDUE,ABSENCE_REQUEST_SUBMITTED,INFO_UPDATE_REQUESTED,BUS_NO_SHOW_DETECTED,...) NOT NULL (ADR-025). v6.7 additions: ASSIGNMENT_POSTED creates academic task for student when teacher publishes; GRADE_RETURNED creates review-feedback task; ANNOUNCEMENT_REQUIRES_ACKNOWLEDGEMENT creates acknowledgement task linked to tsk_acknowledgements; DISCIPLINE_PENDING_STUDENT_SIGN_OFF creates acknowledgement task with dispute option; CONSENT_REQUESTED creates acknowledgement task targeting guardian account; CHECKOUT_OVERDUE creates library return reminder. target_role TEXT, title_template TEXT, description_template TEXT, priority ENUM(LOW,NORMAL,HIGH,URGENT), due_offset_hours INT. Task Worker owns ENUM → Kafka topic string mapping. Seeded at provisioning. Schools can deactivate rules — cannot delete system rules.
M2Approval Workflowsprefix: wsk_7 tablesWave 1

WorkflowEngineService is the SOLE writer to wsk_approval_requests and wsk_approval_steps (ADR-012). Source modules submit via POST /api/v1/approvals and listen on approval.request.resolved Kafka event.

TableDefinition
wsk_workflow_templatesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, request_type TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, request_type).
wsk_workflow_stepsid UUID PK(v7), template_id UUID FK, step_order INT, approver_type ENUM(SPECIFIC_USER,ROLE,MANAGER,DEPARTMENT_HEAD), approver_ref TEXT, is_parallel BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, timeout_hours INT nullable (when set: if step not actioned within timeout_hours, escalation worker creates wsk_workflow_escalations row and notifies escalation_target), escalation_target_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (user who receives escalation notification and task if timeout_hours is breached). INDEX(template_id, step_order).
wsk_approval_requestsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, template_id UUID FK, requester_id UUID FK, request_type TEXT, reference_id UUID, reference_table TEXT, status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,CANCELLED,WITHDRAWN), submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX 1: (school_id, status, request_type) WHERE status NOT IN ('APPROVED','REJECTED','CANCELLED','WITHDRAWN') — admin dashboard. INDEX 2: (school_id, requester_id, created_at DESC) — requester's own history.
wsk_approval_stepsid UUID PK(v7), request_id UUID FK, step_order INT, approver_id UUID FK(platform_users), status ENUM(AWAITING,APPROVED,REJECTED,SKIPPED), actioned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, comments TEXT. INDEX(approver_id, status) WHERE status='AWAITING' — approver's pending queue.
wsk_approval_commentsid UUID PK(v7), request_id UUID FK, author_id UUID FK, body TEXT NOT NULL, is_requester_visible BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(request_id, created_at).
wsk_workflow_escalationsImmutable record of an approval step escalation due to timeout breach. id UUID PK(v7), request_id UUID FK(wsk_approval_requests) NOT NULL, step_id UUID FK(wsk_approval_steps) NOT NULL, original_approver_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, escalated_to_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, escalation_reason TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Approval step timed out', hours_overdue NUMERIC(5,1) NOT NULL, escalated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, resolved_by UUID FK nullable. IMMUTABLE. INDEX(escalated_to_id, resolved_at) WHERE resolved_at IS NULL. On INSERT: Task Worker creates URGENT task for escalated_to_id.
wsk_approval_attachmentsid UUID PK(v7), request_id UUID FK, uploaded_by UUID FK, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, filename TEXT, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
M3Calendarprefix: cal_7 tablesWave 1

Module 3 scope: school calendar events, personal calendar items, subscriptions only. Timetabling extracted to M22 (Academic Scheduling).

TableDefinition
cal_calendarsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, calendar_type ENUM(SCHOOL,CLASS,STAFF,PERSONAL,RESOURCE), is_primary BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, color TEXT.
cal_calendar_daysUNIQUE(calendar_id, date). day_type ENUM(SCHOOL_DAY,HOLIDAY,STAFF_DAY,EXAM,CLOSURE), notes TEXT.
cal_event_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, color TEXT, is_public BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
cal_eventsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, calendar_id UUID FK, event_type_id UUID FK, title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, start_at TIMESTAMPTZ, end_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_all_day BOOLEAN, recurrence_rule TEXT (RFC 5545 iCal format), location TEXT. INDEX(school_id, start_at).
cal_event_rsvpsUNIQUE(event_id, responder_id). response ENUM(ACCEPTED,DECLINED,TENTATIVE,AWAITING), responded_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
cal_personal_calendar_itemsid UUID PK(v7), platform_user_id UUID FK, school_id UUID, source_event_id UUID FK (SOFT INTEGRITY → cal_events.id), source_type ENUM(CLASS,ASSIGNMENT,MEETING,TIMETABLE_SLOT,SCHOOL_EVENT,TASK) NOT NULL (TASK added v6.7: when a tsk_tasks row with due_at is created for a student or parent, a corresponding calendar item is created so it surfaces on the planner; when the task is marked DONE or CANCELLED, the calendar item is removed), title TEXT, start_at TIMESTAMPTZ, end_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Delta-update pattern (ADR-013): never fully rebuilt. calendar.event.changed event → worker applies targeted row updates only.
cal_calendar_subscriptionsUNIQUE(platform_user_id, calendar_id). notify_on_change BOOLEAN, subscribed_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
M20SIS Coreprefix: sis_39 tablesWave 1
TableDefinition
sis_studentsid UUID PK(v7), platform_student_id UUID FK(platform_students) NOT NULL UNIQUE, school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_number TEXT, grade_level TEXT, homeroom_class_id UUID FK(sis_classes), enrollment_status ENUM(ENROLLED,TRANSFERRED,GRADUATED,WITHDRAWN) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ENROLLED', withdrawal_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to enr_withdrawal_requests.id nullable — populated when status=WITHDRAWN; links to the formal withdrawal record), re_enrollment_hold BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: school has placed a hold preventing this student from re-enrolling — e.g. outstanding fees, behaviour history; EO must clear the hold explicitly before a new application is accepted), re_enrollment_hold_reason TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Never duplicate PII fields from platform_students — resolve from platform schema when needed.
sis_guardiansid UUID PK(v7), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the guardian's human identity — name and contact details resolve from iam_person; school-scoped relationship details are in this record), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (their portal login account — null if guardian has no portal access), school_id UUID NOT NULL, relationship ENUM(PARENT,GUARDIAN,GRANDPARENT,FOSTER_PARENT,OTHER), preferred_contact_method ENUM(EMAIL,SMS,APP,PHONE), family_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to sis_families.id nullable — links this guardian record to the family unit they belong to at this school). NOTE: family membership and student-to-student relationships are managed via sis_family_members. sis_guardians is the school-scoped contact record; sis_families is the family entity.
sis_student_guardiansUNIQUE(student_id, guardian_id). has_custody BOOLEAN, is_emergency_contact BOOLEAN, receives_reports BOOLEAN, portal_access BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: requires sis_guardians.account_id IS NOT NULL — a guardian cannot have portal access without a platform_users account; application layer enforces this: setting portal_access=true for a guardian with no account_id is rejected with GUARDIAN_HAS_NO_ACCOUNT error), portal_access_scope ENUM(FULL,ACADEMIC_ONLY,COMMUNICATIONS_ONLY) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'FULL' (FULL = all portal features; ACADEMIC_ONLY = grades, attendance, schedule only — hides address, custody details, other guardian contacts; COMMUNICATIONS_ONLY = messaging and announcements only — used for restricted non-custodial access). Only applies when portal_access=true. School admin sets this per guardian per student — cannot be self-edited by the guardian.
sis_emergency_contactsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, relationship TEXT, phone TEXT, is_authorised_pickup BOOLEAN. INDEX(student_id).
sis_familiesThe family unit — a named household that may have children enrolled at one or more schools within an organisation. SCHEMA NOTE: sis_families lives in the tenant schema (each school can see families with links to their school via sis_family_school_links). For multi-school organisations, the same family_id appears in multiple tenant schemas linked via sis_family_school_links; the family record itself is replicated per tenant on provisioning using the organisation_id SOFT INTEGRITY reference. This is the standard ADR-020 pattern — cross-tenant identity via platform entity cache, not a shared cross-schema table. id UUID PK(v7), organisation_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to organisations.id nullable — for multi-school families within a MAT or district; null for standalone school), family_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'The Johnson-Okafor Family' — editable by admin or primary guardian), primary_address TEXT nullable, city TEXT nullable, postcode TEXT nullable, country_code CHAR(2) nullable, notes TEXT nullable (staff-only notes about the family — not visible to guardians), platform_family_id UUID FK(platform_families) nullable (SOFT INTEGRITY — links to the cross-tenant platform_families record when the family spans multiple schools in a district; null for standalone school families where cross-school membership is not needed), created_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (actor — who created this family record; auth context), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(organisation_id) WHERE organisation_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(platform_family_id) WHERE platform_family_id IS NOT NULL.
sis_family_membersLinks any person (student or adult) to a family with a typed relationship role. Replaces the need for separate sibling tables — all family relationships are expressed here. id UUID PK(v7), family_id UUID FK(sis_families) NOT NULL, person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the human being who is a member of this family — iam_person is the universal identity record per ADR-055), person_type ENUM(STUDENT,GUARDIAN,PARENT,STEP_PARENT,GRANDPARENT,FOSTER_PARENT,OTHER_ADULT) NOT NULL, relationship_to_family ENUM(CHILD,STEP_CHILD,FOSTER_CHILD,GRANDCHILD,OTHER) nullable (how this person relates to the core family unit — used for student members), is_primary_contact BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (the main communication recipient for the family — only one per family), joined_family_at DATE nullable, notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(family_id, person_id). INDEX(family_id, person_type). INDEX(person_id). To find siblings: SELECT fm.person_id FROM sis_family_members fm JOIN iam_person ip ON ip.id=fm.person_id WHERE fm.family_id = (SELECT family_id FROM sis_family_members WHERE person_id = $person_id) AND fm.person_type = 'STUDENT' AND fm.person_id != $person_id.
sis_family_member_relationshipsTyped bilateral relationship between two members of the same family — captures precise kinship including step and half relationships. id UUID PK(v7), family_id UUID FK(sis_families) NOT NULL, person_a_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL, person_b_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL, relationship_type ENUM(PARENT_CHILD,STEP_PARENT_CHILD,FOSTER_PARENT_CHILD,GRANDPARENT_GRANDCHILD,SIBLING,STEP_SIBLING,HALF_SIBLING,TWIN,GUARDIAN_WARD,OTHER) NOT NULL, relationship_label TEXT nullable (optional free-text override — e.g. 'step-brother', 'maternal grandmother' — displayed in the UI when set, otherwise derived from relationship_type), is_confirmed BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(family_id, person_a_id, person_b_id). CHECK: person_a_id != person_b_id. INDEX(family_id, person_a_id). INDEX(family_id, person_b_id). NOTE: relationships are bidirectional by convention — one row records the relationship; the application layer resolves the inverse (e.g. if A is sibling of B, B is sibling of A).
sis_family_school_linksSchool-scoped link declaring that a family has student(s) enrolled at this school. Enables the SIS to query 'all families with current enrolments' without scanning guardian records. id UUID PK(v7), family_id UUID FK(sis_families) NOT NULL, school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (false when student leaves the school), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(family_id, school_id, student_id). INDEX(school_id, family_id). INDEX(family_id, is_active).
sis_guardian_relationshipsAdult-to-adult relationship record within a family — used for custody and communication routing. id UUID PK(v7), family_id UUID FK(sis_families) NOT NULL, guardian_a_id UUID FK(sis_guardians) NOT NULL, guardian_b_id UUID FK(sis_guardians) NOT NULL, relationship_type ENUM(MARRIED,CIVIL_PARTNERSHIP,SEPARATED,DIVORCED,CO_PARENTING,REMARRIED,OTHER) NOT NULL, communicate_separately BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: these two adults should never share a communication thread about the children — used in high-conflict custody situations; school communications are duplicated independently to each), custody_order_s3_key TEXT nullable (optional — custody document for reference). UNIQUE(family_id, guardian_a_id, guardian_b_id). CHECK: guardian_a_id != guardian_b_id. INDEX(family_id).
sis_parent_info_update_requestsParent-initiated request to update student general information or their own contact details. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, submitted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (guardian account), target_type ENUM(STUDENT_INFO,GUARDIAN_INFO,EMERGENCY_CONTACT) NOT NULL (STUDENT_INFO: update to student general fields such as preferred name, home address, home language, doctor name; GUARDIAN_INFO: update to the submitting parent's own contact details — phone, email, address, preferred contact method; EMERGENCY_CONTACT: add, update or remove an emergency contact), target_id UUID NOT NULL (SOFT INTEGRITY: sis_students.id for STUDENT_INFO; sis_guardians.id for GUARDIAN_INFO; sis_emergency_contacts.id for EMERGENCY_CONTACT update, NULL for new contact add), proposed_changes JSONB NOT NULL (key-value map of field names to new values — only fields being changed are included), change_reason TEXT nullable, status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,AUTO_APPROVED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, reviewer_notes TEXT nullable, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(submitted_by, created_at DESC). INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'PENDING' — admin review queue. On APPROVED: application layer applies proposed_changes to the target table and sets applied_at. A full audit trail is maintained in audit_log — the parent request is the source of record for the change. Schools can configure auto-approval for low-risk fields (phone number, email) and require manual review for higher-risk fields (address, emergency contacts).
sis_academic_yearsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT, start_date DATE NOT NULL, end_date DATE NOT NULL, is_current BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. UNIQUE(school_id, start_date).
sis_termsid UUID PK(v7), academic_year_id UUID FK, name TEXT, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, term_type ENUM(SEMESTER,QUARTER,TRIMESTER,FULL_YEAR).
sis_departmentsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, head_employee_id UUID FK(hr_employees). UNIQUE(school_id, name).
sis_coursesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, department_id UUID FK, code TEXT, name TEXT NOT NULL, credit_hours NUMERIC(3,1), grade_level TEXT.
sis_course_prerequisitesPrerequisite relationship between courses. id UUID PK(v7), course_id UUID FK(sis_courses) NOT NULL (the course that has a prerequisite), prerequisite_course_id UUID FK(sis_courses) NOT NULL (the course that must be completed first), is_mandatory BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (false = recommended but not enforced), min_grade TEXT nullable (minimum grade required in the prerequisite — e.g. 'C', '60' — null means any passing grade), notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(course_id, prerequisite_course_id). CHECK: course_id != prerequisite_course_id — a course cannot be its own prerequisite. INDEX(course_id). INDEX(prerequisite_course_id) — find what courses depend on a given course.
sis_course_proposalsFormal Department Head proposal for a new course requiring admin approval. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, proposed_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL (the DH submitting the proposal), department_id UUID FK(sis_departments) NOT NULL, proposed_course_name TEXT NOT NULL, proposed_course_code TEXT nullable, rationale TEXT NOT NULL (why this course is needed), expected_enrollment INT nullable, resource_requirements TEXT nullable (staffing, materials, equipment needed), curriculum_framework_alignment TEXT nullable (which standards this course addresses), target_grade_level TEXT nullable, target_academic_year_id UUID FK(sis_academic_years) nullable (when the course would first be offered), status ENUM(DRAFT,SUBMITTED,UNDER_REVIEW,APPROVED,REJECTED,WITHDRAWN) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT', linked_approval_id UUID FK(wsk_approval_requests) nullable (populated on SUBMITTED), reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, reviewer_notes TEXT nullable, approved_course_id UUID FK(sis_courses) nullable (populated when APPROVED — links to the created sis_courses record), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, status). INDEX(proposed_by, created_at DESC).
sis_classesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, course_id UUID FK, academic_year_id UUID FK, term_id UUID FK, section_code TEXT, room TEXT, max_enrollment INT. INDEX(school_id, academic_year_id).
sis_class_teachersUNIQUE(class_id, teacher_employee_id). is_primary_teacher BOOLEAN.
sis_enrollmentsUNIQUE(student_id, class_id) WHERE status='ACTIVE'. id UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, class_id UUID FK, status ENUM(ACTIVE,DROPPED,TRANSFERRED), enrolled_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
sis_attendance_recordsCOMPOSITE PARTITION: RANGE(school_year DATE) → HASH(class_id) 8 buckets (ADR-007). school_year = sis_academic_years.start_date for the current year. Pre-populated at school day start with status=PRESENT, confirmation_status=PRE_POPULATED. Only CONFIRMED records count in official reports. BRIN index on date within each partition. evidence_source ENUM(BUS_SCAN,DOOR_SCAN,MANUAL_CHECKIN,PREVIOUS_PERIOD,SYSTEM_INFERRED). parent_explanation TEXT nullable (parent-provided reason for absence — submitted via portal or app, stored against the attendance record), parent_explained_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, absence_request_id UUID FK(sis_absence_requests) nullable (populated when this absence was pre-authorised via a parent request — links the confirmed attendance record back to the request that authorised it).
sis_attendance_evidenceid UUID PK(v7), record_id UUID FK(sis_attendance_records), evidence_type ENUM(BUS_SCAN,DOOR_SCAN,PHOTO,NOTE), source_ref_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
sis_absence_requestsParent-submitted absence request — both same-day reports and advance authorised absence requests. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, submitted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (guardian account), absence_date_from DATE NOT NULL, absence_date_to DATE NOT NULL (same as absence_date_from for single-day; range for planned absences), reason_category ENUM(ILLNESS,MEDICAL_APPOINTMENT,FAMILY_EMERGENCY,HOLIDAY,RELIGIOUS_OBSERVANCE,OTHER) NOT NULL, reason_text TEXT NOT NULL, supporting_document_s3_key TEXT nullable (optional upload — e.g. doctor note), request_type ENUM(SAME_DAY_REPORT,ADVANCE_REQUEST) NOT NULL (SAME_DAY_REPORT: parent notifying of absence today — auto-approved after admin review window; ADVANCE_REQUEST: future planned absence requiring admin approval), status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,AUTO_APPROVED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, reviewer_notes TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(student_id, absence_date_from). INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'PENDING' — admin review queue. On INSERT: Task Worker creates ABSENCE_REQUEST_SUBMITTED task for school admin. On APPROVED or AUTO_APPROVED: sis_attendance_records for those dates are updated with status=ABSENT and absence_request_id set.
sis_discipline_categoriesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, severity ENUM(LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL). UNIQUE(school_id, name).
sis_discipline_action_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, requires_parent_notification BOOLEAN.
sis_discipline_incidentsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, student_id UUID FK, reported_by UUID FK(hr_employees), category_id UUID FK, description TEXT, incident_date DATE, status ENUM(OPEN,UNDER_REVIEW,RESOLVED). INDEX(student_id, incident_date DESC).
sis_student_notesLightweight teacher-authored pastoral or academic observation about a student. Not a formal discipline incident or counselor referral — a quick note capturing context. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, author_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, note_type ENUM(PASTORAL,ACADEMIC,BEHAVIOURAL,WELLBEING,GENERAL) NOT NULL, note_text TEXT NOT NULL, is_shared_with_counselor BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: counselors with student_counseling_record:read permission can see this note — feeds the broader picture without requiring a formal referral), is_confidential BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: note visible only to author and school admin — not visible to other teachers or the student), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(student_id, created_at DESC). INDEX(author_id, created_at DESC). NOTE: this table is not a replacement for svc_referrals or sis_discipline_incidents — those remain the formal escalation paths. sis_student_notes is for informal observations that build context over time.
sis_discipline_actionsUNIQUE(incident_id, action_type_id). start_date DATE, end_date DATE (for suspensions).
sis_transfer_recordsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, from_school_id UUID, to_school_id UUID, transfer_date DATE, reason TEXT, transfer_type ENUM(INBOUND,OUTBOUND).
sis_staffid UUID PK(v7), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL UNIQUE (the staff member's human identity), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL UNIQUE (their login account), school_id UUID, employee_id UUID FK(hr_employees), staff_type ENUM(TEACHER,ADMINISTRATOR,SUPPORT,COUNSELLOR).
sis_student_active_accommodationsIEP/504 read model (ADR-030). Populated by Health module Kafka consumer on iep.accommodation.updated event. Classroom module reads this — never reads hlth_* tables directly. UNIQUE(student_id, accommodation_type, effective_from). id UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK(sis_students), accommodation_type TEXT (EXTENDED_TIME,ALTERNATIVE_ASSESSMENT,ASSISTIVE_TECH,READ_ALOUD,REDUCED_DISTRACTION...), applies_to ENUM(ALL_ASSESSMENTS,ALL_ASSIGNMENTS,SPECIFIC), specific_assignment_types TEXT[], effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE, plan_type ENUM(IEP,504). INDEX(student_id, effective_from, effective_to).
M21Classroomprefix: cls_33 tablesWave 1
TableDefinition
cls_lesson_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, icon TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
cls_lessonsid UUID PK(v7), class_id UUID FK nullable (NULL when is_template=true — lesson bank entries are not tied to a specific class), teacher_id UUID FK, lesson_type_id UUID FK, title TEXT NOT NULL, date DATE nullable (NULL for template lessons), duration_minutes INT, learning_objectives TEXT[], status ENUM(DRAFT,PUBLISHED,ARCHIVED,TEMPLATE) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT' (TEMPLATE: lesson bank entry — visible to teacher across all their classes, can be copied into any class as a DRAFT), is_template BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, bank_lesson_id UUID FK(cls_lessons) nullable (when a lesson is created by copying from the bank, this points to the source template — preserves provenance, does not create a live FK dependency). CHECK: (class_id IS NOT NULL AND is_template = false) OR (class_id IS NULL AND is_template = true) — template lessons have no class. INDEX(class_id, date DESC) WHERE class_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(teacher_id, is_template) WHERE is_template = true — teacher's lesson bank query. department_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → sis_departments.id nullable — when set on a template lesson, the lesson is visible to all teachers in the department via the department lesson bank, not just the authoring teacher; set explicitly by the teacher or DH; null means private to the authoring teacher only). INDEX(department_id, is_template) WHERE department_id IS NOT NULL AND is_template = true — department bank query.
cls_lesson_resourcesid UUID PK(v7), lesson_id UUID FK, resource_type ENUM(FILE,URL,VIDEO,DOCUMENT), url TEXT, s3_key TEXT, title TEXT, is_visible_to_students BOOLEAN. Signed S3 URLs (15–60 min expiry) — never expose public bucket URLs.
cls_video_jobsid UUID PK(v7), lesson_id UUID FK, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, status ENUM(PENDING,TRANSCRIBING,SUMMARISING,COMPLETE,FAILED), transcript_s3_key TEXT, duration_seconds INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. VIDEO PIPELINE: teacher uploads → S3 presigned URL (never through app server) → S3 event → Kafka video.uploaded → Video Worker → AWS Transcribe / Whisper → AI Gateway /summarize-lesson → stores in cls_lesson_ai_artifacts → lesson.summary.ready event.
cls_lesson_ai_artifactsid UUID PK(v7), lesson_id UUID FK UNIQUE, video_job_id UUID FK, summary_text TEXT, key_points JSONB, study_guide JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, model_version TEXT. Separate from cls_lesson_embeddings (vector storage).
cls_lesson_embeddingsid UUID PK(v7), lesson_id UUID FK, chunk_index INT, chunk_text TEXT, embedding VECTOR(1536). UNIQUE(lesson_id, chunk_index). VECTOR INDEX: CREATE INDEX USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (m=16, ef_construction=64). Cosine similarity for semantic search. HNSW rebuild required on dimension change — dimension change = ADR required.
cls_assignment_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT, weight_in_category NUMERIC(5,2), category ENUM(HOMEWORK,QUIZ,TEST,PROJECT,CLASSWORK). UNIQUE(school_id, name).
cls_assignmentsid UUID PK(v7), class_id UUID FK, lesson_id UUID FK, assignment_type_id UUID FK, title TEXT NOT NULL, instructions TEXT, due_date TIMESTAMPTZ, max_points NUMERIC(6,2), grading_scale_id UUID FK, is_ai_grading_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, is_extra_credit BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: assignment does not count against the student's overall grade average — extra points only; gradebook snapshot logic excludes is_extra_credit=true assignments from denominator when computing current_average). INDEX(class_id, due_date).
cls_assignment_categoriesid UUID PK(v7), class_id UUID FK, name TEXT, weight NUMERIC(5,2). Weights must sum to 100 per class (application-layer validation).
cls_assignment_questionsid UUID PK(v7), assignment_id UUID FK, question_text TEXT NOT NULL, question_type ENUM(MULTIPLE_CHOICE,SHORT_ANSWER,ESSAY,TRUE_FALSE,FILE_UPLOAD), points NUMERIC(5,2), sort_order INT. INDEX(assignment_id, sort_order).
cls_answer_key_entriesUNIQUE(question_id, option_index). correct_answer TEXT, explanation TEXT, is_correct BOOLEAN.
cls_submissionsUNIQUE(student_id, assignment_id). Partial index (assignment_id, submitted_at DESC) WHERE status='SUBMITTED'. id UUID PK(v7), status ENUM(NOT_STARTED,IN_PROGRESS,SUBMITTED,GRADED,RETURNED), submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
cls_ai_grading_jobsid UUID PK(v7), submission_id UUID FK, status ENUM(PENDING,RUNNING,COMPLETE,FAILED), ai_suggested_grade NUMERIC(6,2), ai_confidence NUMERIC(3,2), ai_reasoning TEXT, model_version TEXT, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. AI must never write to cls_grades. teacher_grade in cls_grades is only set by teacher action.
cls_submission_question_gradesUNIQUE(submission_id, question_id). ai_suggested_points NUMERIC(5,2), ai_confidence NUMERIC(3,2), teacher_awarded_points NUMERIC(5,2), feedback TEXT. AI suggestions are read-only inputs to teacher review UI.
cls_gradesUNIQUE(student_id, assignment_id). id UUID PK(v7), teacher_id UUID FK, grade_value NUMERIC(6,2), letter_grade TEXT, feedback TEXT, graded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_published BOOLEAN. AI must never write to this table. Only teacher action creates or updates a confirmed grade.
cls_gradebook_snapshotsUNIQUE(class_id, student_id, term_id). current_average NUMERIC(5,2), last_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Updated by async Kafka consumer with 30s debounce per (class_id, student_id). Never updated synchronously inside a grade write transaction (ADR-010).
cls_lesson_bank_sharesExplicit sharing of a template lesson to a department or individual teacher. Supplements the department_id column for targeted sharing outside the author's own department. id UUID PK(v7), lesson_id UUID FK(cls_lessons) NOT NULL (must be a template lesson — is_template=true), shared_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, shared_with_department_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → sis_departments.id nullable), shared_with_employee_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → hr_employees.id nullable), shared_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. CHECK: (shared_with_department_id IS NOT NULL) != (shared_with_employee_id IS NOT NULL) — share targets exactly one of department or individual. UNIQUE(lesson_id, shared_with_department_id) WHERE shared_with_department_id IS NOT NULL. UNIQUE(lesson_id, shared_with_employee_id) WHERE shared_with_employee_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(shared_with_department_id) WHERE shared_with_department_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(shared_with_employee_id) WHERE shared_with_employee_id IS NOT NULL.
cls_student_progress_notesTeacher-authored mid-term progress note for a specific student in a specific class. Distinct from formal report cards (cls_report_cards) — lighter weight, can be created at any point in the term. id UUID PK(v7), class_id UUID FK(sis_classes) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, author_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL (the teacher writing the note), term_id UUID FK(sis_terms) NOT NULL, note_text TEXT NOT NULL, overall_effort_rating ENUM(EXCELLENT,GOOD,SATISFACTORY,NEEDS_IMPROVEMENT) nullable, is_parent_visible BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (teacher explicitly publishes to parent portal — default private), is_student_visible BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (set when is_parent_visible or is_student_visible first set to true), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(class_id, student_id, term_id) — one progress note per student per class per term; teacher can update it before publishing. INDEX(class_id, term_id). INDEX(student_id, term_id).
