On is_parent_visible → true: emit cls.progress_note.published — Communications worker notifies parent.
cls_report_cardsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, class_id UUID FK, term_id UUID FK, status ENUM(DRAFT,PUBLISHED), published_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(student_id, class_id, term_id).
cls_report_card_entriesUNIQUE(report_card_id, subject). id UUID PK(v7), report_card_id UUID FK(cls_report_cards) NOT NULL, subject TEXT NOT NULL (INTENTIONAL FREE-TEXT — not a FK to sis_courses. Report cards frequently need to show subject labels that do not map 1:1 to a sis_courses record: cross-listed subjects, combined grades like 'Science — Year 5', curriculum areas that span courses, or imported historical records from external SIS. A snapshot of the subject label at reporting time is the correct design. If a sis_course FK is later required for linkage, an optional course_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → sis_courses.id) can be added without breaking the UNIQUE constraint), final_grade TEXT, grade_value NUMERIC(5,2), teacher_comments TEXT, effort_grade TEXT. INDEX(report_card_id).
cls_ai_usage_logid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, job_type ENUM(GRADING,SUMMARISATION,STUDENT_SUMMARY), tokens_used INT, cost_usd NUMERIC(8,4), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. RANGE partition by created_at monthly. Used for per-tenant AI quota enforcement.
AI Grading Safety Rules AI suggestions stored in cls_ai_grading_jobs and cls_submission_question_grades.ai_suggested_* columns only. cls_grades.grade_value is set exclusively by teacher action. Never by AI pipeline. Review queue UI shows AI-graded submissions sorted by low ai_confidence first. All AI inputs/outputs logged with PII masking (ADR-004). Per-tenant token quotas enforced via cls_ai_usage_log + Redis counters.
M22Academic Schedulingprefix: sch_31 tablesWave 2

Module 22 (ADR-022): extracted from Calendar module. sch_ prefix for all tables. ADR-027 defines v1 scope boundary.

M22 v1 Scope Boundary (ADR-027) — SUPERSEDED BY ADR-053 IN SCOPE: bell schedules (day-of-week), room inventory, timetable slot assignment with conflict detection, transactional import from external tools, substitution timetable auto-creation, Calendar delta-update events. HISTORICAL v1 SCOPE — ALL ITEMS BELOW NOW IN SCOPE PER ADR-053. This box is retained for traceability only. The following were explicitly out of scope in v1: rotation/A-B week calendars (now: sch_rotation_cycles + sch_rotation_calendar), automated constraint solver/schedule generation (now: sch_scheduling_requests + sch_scheduling_candidates + sch_scheduling_candidate_slots), shared district staff across schools (now: sch_cross_school_staff_assignments), exam scheduling (now: sch_exam_sessions + sch_exam_seatings + sch_exam_session_rooms + sch_exam_invigilator_assignments), pull-out interventions/co-teaching (now: sch_coteaching_arrangements + sch_pull_out_interventions), complex cover beyond substitute replacement (now: sch_cover_arrangements + sch_cover_arrangement_classes + sch_cover_split_students). Each expansion is documented in ADR-053. ADR-027 remains the v1 baseline reference. ADR-053 is the authoritative scope contract from v8.5 onwards.
TableDefinition
sch_bell_schedulesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, schedule_type ENUM(STANDARD,EARLY_DISMISSAL,ASSEMBLY,EXAM,CUSTOM), is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
sch_periodsid UUID PK(v7), bell_schedule_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, day_of_week SMALLINT nullable (0=Mon, 6=Sun — null for rotation-based schedules where the period belongs to a rotation day not a calendar weekday), rotation_day SMALLINT nullable (for A/B week and day-rotation schedules: the rotation day number within the cycle — e.g. 1=Day A or Day 1, 2=Day B or Day 2; resolves to a calendar date via sch_rotation_calendar; must set either day_of_week OR rotation_day, not both), start_time TIME NOT NULL, end_time TIME NOT NULL, period_type ENUM(LESSON,BREAK,LUNCH,REGISTRATION,ASSEMBLY). UNIQUE(bell_schedule_id, COALESCE(day_of_week,-1), COALESCE(rotation_day,-1), start_time). CHECK: (day_of_week IS NOT NULL) != (rotation_day IS NOT NULL) — exactly one must be set. Import: idempotent via ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE.
sch_roomsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, capacity INT, room_type ENUM(CLASSROOM,LAB,GYM,HALL,LIBRARY,OFFICE,OUTDOOR), has_projector BOOLEAN, has_av BOOLEAN, floor TEXT, building TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, fac_space_id UUID (DISPLAY-ONLY → fac_spaces.id nullable — links to the corresponding Facilities space record for the same physical room; sch_rooms is authoritative for scheduling; fac_spaces is authoritative for maintenance and inspections; both can exist for the same room). UNIQUE(school_id, name).
sch_room_change_requestsTeacher-submitted request to change the room assignment for a class period. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, timetable_slot_id UUID FK(sch_timetable_slots) NOT NULL, requested_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, current_room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) NOT NULL, requested_room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) nullable (specific room requested — null means 'any available room'), request_date DATE NOT NULL (the specific date of the change — one-off, not permanent), reason TEXT NOT NULL, status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,AUTO_APPROVED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, status, request_date). On APPROVED: Scheduling Worker creates a one-day sch_timetable_slots override for request_date. On INSERT: Task Worker creates room-change review task for scheduling admin.
sch_timetable_slotsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, class_id UUID FK(sis_classes), period_id UUID FK, teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (null for co-teaching slots where responsibility is shared — see sch_coteaching_arrangements), room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms), effective_from DATE NOT NULL, effective_to DATE, coteaching_pair_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to sch_coteaching_arrangements.id nullable — when set, teacher double-booking exclusion constraint is relaxed for the paired teacher), cross_school_staff_id UUID FK(sch_cross_school_staff_assignments) nullable (when set: the teacher for this slot is a district staff member not employed at this school — teacher_id may be null), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(class_id, period_id, effective_from). EXCLUSION CONSTRAINT 1: EXCLUDE USING gist (teacher_id WITH =, period_id WITH =, daterange(effective_from, COALESCE(effective_to,'infinity'),'[)') WITH &&) WHERE coteaching_pair_id IS NULL AND cross_school_staff_id IS NULL — teacher double-booking check skipped for co-teaching and cross-school slots. EXCLUSION CONSTRAINT 2: room double-booking exclusion unchanged. Emits sch.timetable.updated on INSERT/UPDATE — consumed by Calendar delta-update worker.
sch_room_bookingsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, room_id UUID FK, booked_by UUID FK, booking_purpose TEXT, start_at TIMESTAMPTZ, end_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM(CONFIRMED,CANCELLED). Application checks sch_timetable_slots before confirming — room cannot be double-booked against a timetable slot.
sch_substitution_timetableid UUID PK(v7), original_slot_id UUID FK(sch_timetable_slots), substitute_assignment_id UUID FK(sub_assignments), effective_date DATE NOT NULL, substitute_id UUID FK, room_id UUID FK. UNIQUE(original_slot_id, effective_date). Auto-created by Sub Marketplace Worker on sub.job.filled event — never created via timetable API.
sch_timetable_importsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, import_source ENUM(ISAMS,TIMETABLER,SCHOOLMASTER,SIMS,CSV_GENERIC) NOT NULL, imported_by UUID FK, file_s3_key TEXT, status ENUM(PENDING,VALIDATING,VALID,INVALID,APPLIED,ROLLED_BACK), validation_errors JSONB, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. All-or-nothing transactional import — validate ALL rows for conflicts before committing any. Retention: 7 years (FERPA — educational records).
sch_import_slot_metadataid UUID PK(v7), timetable_slot_id UUID FK UNIQUE, import_id UUID FK, source_format ENUM(ISAMS,TIMETABLER,SCHOOLMASTER,SIMS,CSV_GENERIC) NOT NULL, raw_source_row JSONB (NULLed on schedule activation by ImportMetadataCompactionWorker on sch.import.activated event), unmapped_fields JSONB, co_teacher_ids UUID[] (DISPLAY-ONLY), rotation_day SMALLINT, group_label TEXT, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. RANGE partition by import_date annual. INDEX(import_id) for bulk compaction. Retention: 3 years (operational data).
sch_rotation_cyclesDefines a day-rotation or A/B week cycle for a school. Replaces or supplements day_of_week scheduling when the timetable repeats on a non-7-day cycle. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, bell_schedule_id UUID FK(sch_bell_schedules) NOT NULL, cycle_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'A/B Week', '6-Day Rotation'), cycle_length INT NOT NULL (number of rotation days in one full cycle — 2 for A/B week, 6 for 6-day rotation), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, effective_from DATE NOT NULL, effective_to DATE nullable. UNIQUE(school_id, cycle_name).
sch_rotation_calendarMaps every school calendar date to a specific rotation day number within the active cycle. Created by admin at the start of year and adjusted for unexpected closures. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, calendar_date DATE NOT NULL, rotation_day SMALLINT NOT NULL (1 to cycle_length — e.g. 1=Day A, 2=Day B for A/B week), cycle_id UUID FK(sch_rotation_cycles) NOT NULL, is_school_day BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, override_reason TEXT nullable (e.g. 'Snow day — Day 3 skipped, sequence resumed' — when a school day is lost and admin decides whether to absorb or shift remaining rotation days). UNIQUE(school_id, calendar_date). INDEX(school_id, calendar_date, rotation_day).
sch_scheduling_constraintsConstraint profile for the automated schedule generator. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, constraint_name TEXT NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, max_consecutive_periods_per_teacher INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 4, min_break_between_classes_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, max_daily_periods_per_student INT nullable, respect_room_capacity BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, respect_teacher_preferences BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (attempt to honour preferred teaching times from hr_employee preferences — soft constraint), avoid_split_classes BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (same class should not have the same subject in non-consecutive periods if avoidable), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. UNIQUE(school_id, constraint_name, academic_year_id).
sch_student_subject_choicesStudent subject selection for the coming academic year — the demand input to the automated scheduler. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, course_id UUID FK(sis_courses) NOT NULL, preference_rank INT nullable (1 = first choice for optional subjects), is_mandatory BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (core curriculum subject — must be timetabled), status ENUM(SUBMITTED,CONFIRMED,REJECTED,SUBSTITUTED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SUBMITTED', substituted_course_id UUID FK(sis_courses) nullable (if REJECTED: alternative course offered). UNIQUE(student_id, academic_year_id, course_id). INDEX(school_id, academic_year_id, course_id).
sch_scheduling_requestsA request to auto-generate a timetable for an academic year. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, constraint_id UUID FK(sch_scheduling_constraints) NOT NULL, requested_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, section_count_at_submission INT NOT NULL (total timetable slots to be generated at submission time — drives algorithm selection: CP_SAT for ≤300, HEURISTIC for >300 per ADR-060), solver_algorithm ENUM(CP_SAT,HEURISTIC) NOT NULL (set by SchedulingWorker before invoking solver, recorded for audit and debugging), timeout_seconds INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 600 (solver aborted after this; partial solutions written as FAILED candidates with error_message=SOLVER_TIMEOUT), status ENUM(QUEUED,RUNNING,COMPLETED,FAILED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED', solver_job_id TEXT nullable (external reference to the constraint solver job), solutions_generated INT nullable, students_fully_scheduled INT nullable, students_with_clashes INT nullable, error_message TEXT nullable, queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, status). CONSTRAINT: max 1 RUNNING job per school enforced by SchedulingWorker before starting.
sch_scheduling_candidatesA candidate timetable solution produced by the automated scheduler — awaits admin review before activation. id UUID PK(v7), request_id UUID FK(sch_scheduling_requests) NOT NULL, solution_label TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Solution A', 'Solution B — fewer splits'), total_slots INT NOT NULL, unresolved_clashes INT NOT NULL, teacher_utilisation_pct NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL, room_utilisation_pct NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL, solver_seed TEXT nullable (HEURISTIC runs only: the random seed used by the solver — enables approximate reproduction for debugging; null for CP_SAT which is fully deterministic), review_status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,MODIFIED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, activated_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(request_id, review_status).
sch_scheduling_candidate_slotsIndividual timetable slot within a scheduling candidate — the atomic unit of the proposed schedule. id UUID PK(v7), candidate_id UUID FK(sch_scheduling_candidates) NOT NULL, class_id UUID FK(sis_classes) NOT NULL, period_id UUID FK(sch_periods) NOT NULL, teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable, room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) nullable, has_clash BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (flagged by solver when constraint could not be satisfied — admin must resolve before activation), clash_description TEXT nullable. INDEX(candidate_id, has_clash). INDEX(candidate_id, class_id).
sch_exam_sessionsA scheduled exam event — one sitting for one or more cohorts. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, exam_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Year 11 Mathematics Paper 1', 'AP Chemistry Exam'), course_id UUID FK(sis_courses) nullable, grade_level TEXT nullable, exam_date DATE NOT NULL, start_time TIME NOT NULL, end_time TIME NOT NULL, standard_duration_minutes INT NOT NULL, invigilator_count_required INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, status ENUM(SCHEDULED,IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED'. Rooms assigned via sch_exam_session_rooms junction table (replaces room_ids UUID[] array — proper relational model with per-room capacity and conflict checking). INDEX(school_id, exam_date). INDEX(course_id, exam_date).
sch_exam_session_roomsJunction table assigning rooms to an exam session. Replaces the former room_ids UUID[] array on sch_exam_sessions — enables per-room capacity management, conflict checking, and integrity enforcement. id UUID PK(v7), exam_session_id UUID FK(sch_exam_sessions) NOT NULL, room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) NOT NULL, max_students_in_room INT nullable (defaults to sch_rooms.capacity if null — allows partial use of a large room), is_main_room BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (the primary exam room — typically the largest; access arrangements rooms are non-main), notes TEXT nullable (e.g. 'Access arrangements room — extra time', 'Overflow room'). UNIQUE(exam_session_id, room_id). INDEX(exam_session_id). INDEX(room_id, exam_session_id) — room conflict check: SELECT 1 FROM sch_exam_session_rooms esr JOIN sch_exam_sessions es ON esr.exam_session_id=es.id WHERE esr.room_id=$1 AND es.exam_date=$2 AND tstzrange(es.start_time::timestamptz, (es.start_time + interval '1 minute'*es.standard_duration_minutes)::timestamptz) && tstzrange($start, $end).
sch_exam_seatingsAssignment of a student to a specific seat in a specific room for an exam. id UUID PK(v7), exam_session_id UUID FK(sch_exam_sessions) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) NOT NULL, seat_label TEXT nullable (e.g. 'Row C Seat 4'), separate_room BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (student requires a separate invigilation room per accommodation), extra_time_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (from sis_student_active_accommodations — applied at seating generation), reader_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, scribe_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, actual_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, actual_end_time TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. UNIQUE(exam_session_id, student_id). INDEX(exam_session_id, room_id).
sch_exam_invigilator_assignmentsInvigilator assigned to a room for an exam session. id UUID PK(v7), exam_session_id UUID FK(sch_exam_sessions) NOT NULL, room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) NOT NULL, invigilator_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, is_lead BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, confirmed BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. UNIQUE(exam_session_id, invigilator_id). INDEX(exam_session_id, room_id).
sch_coteaching_arrangementsTwo teachers formally assigned to the same class for the same period. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, primary_teacher_slot_id UUID FK(sch_timetable_slots) NOT NULL, secondary_teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, coteaching_model ENUM(TEAM_TEACHING,ONE_TEACH_ONE_SUPPORT,STATION_ROTATION,PARALLEL_TEACHING,ALTERNATIVE_TEACHING) NOT NULL, effective_from DATE NOT NULL, effective_to DATE nullable, notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(primary_teacher_slot_id, secondary_teacher_id, effective_from). The secondary teacher's double-booking exclusion is waived for slots referencing this arrangement via coteaching_pair_id.
sch_pull_out_interventionsA student's scheduled removal from a regular timetable slot to attend a specialist intervention session. Overrides clash detection for the named student. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, regular_slot_id UUID FK(sch_timetable_slots) NOT NULL (the lesson the student will miss), intervention_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Reading Recovery Group', 'Speech Therapy Session'), intervention_provider UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable, intervention_location TEXT nullable, start_date DATE NOT NULL, end_date DATE nullable, frequency ENUM(WEEKLY,FORTNIGHTLY,DAILY,CUSTOM) NOT NULL, days_of_week SMALLINT[] nullable (for WEEKLY/FORTNIGHTLY: which days), notes TEXT nullable. INDEX(student_id, start_date). INDEX(regular_slot_id).
sch_cross_school_staff_assignmentsA district or MAT staff member (music specialist, SEND advisory teacher, PE teacher) assigned to teach at a school where they are not employed as hr_employees. id UUID PK(v7), home_school_id UUID NOT NULL (the school where the staff member is employed), visiting_school_id UUID NOT NULL (the school where they will teach), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (their human identity — shared across schools; the iam_person record exists at platform level and is not school-scoped), home_employee_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL (their hr record at their home school), role_at_visiting_school TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Visiting Music Teacher', 'SEND Advisory'), effective_from DATE NOT NULL, effective_to DATE nullable, max_periods_per_week INT nullable, approved_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (actor — who authorised this assignment). UNIQUE(visiting_school_id, person_id, effective_from). NOTE ON FK PATTERN: this table uses both person_id FK(iam_person) AND home_employee_id FK(hr_employees) — this is correct and intentional. person_id is the ADR-055 human identity reference (platform-level, school-agnostic — the same iam_person exists at both home and visiting schools). home_employee_id is the role-context reference (category 3 of ADR-055) needed by the scheduling engine to look up hr contract terms, availability, and pay rate. Both FKs are required and serve distinct purposes.
sch_cover_arrangementsA structured cover plan for teacher absences — goes beyond simple substitute replacement to support class splits, merges, and supervised self-study. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, absence_date DATE NOT NULL, absent_teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, cover_type ENUM(SUBSTITUTE_REPLACEMENT,SPLIT_CLASS,MERGED_CLASS,SUPERVISION,SELF_DIRECTED_STUDY,EXTERNAL_COVER) NOT NULL, created_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, notes TEXT nullable, sub_assignment_id UUID FK(sub_assignments) nullable (for SUBSTITUTE_REPLACEMENT — links to Sub Marketplace assignment). INDEX(school_id, absence_date). INDEX(absent_teacher_id, absence_date).
sch_cover_arrangement_classesPer-class details within a cover arrangement — where each affected class will go and who will supervise. id UUID PK(v7), arrangement_id UUID FK(sch_cover_arrangements) NOT NULL, original_slot_id UUID FK(sch_timetable_slots) NOT NULL, cover_teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (null for SELF_DIRECTED_STUDY), cover_room_id UUID FK(sch_rooms) nullable, merged_with_slot_id UUID FK(sch_timetable_slots) nullable (for MERGED_CLASS: the slot this class merges into), is_split_group BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (for SPLIT_CLASS: this is a sub-group going to a different room — the specific students in this sub-group are in sch_cover_split_students). UNIQUE(arrangement_id, original_slot_id).
sch_cover_split_studentsJunction table listing the students assigned to a specific split-class sub-group within a cover arrangement. Replaces the former split_student_ids UUID[] array — enables integrity enforcement and per-student auditability. id UUID PK(v7), arrangement_class_id UUID FK(sch_cover_arrangement_classes) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL. UNIQUE(arrangement_class_id, student_id). INDEX(student_id, arrangement_class_id) — query: which cover arrangement is this student in today.
sch_import_slot_metadata_archiveSuperseded import metadata. Same schema as sch_import_slot_metadata EXCEPT raw_source_row omitted (NULLed before archival). INSERT here from live table when schedule superseded by a newer import for the same academic year; DELETE from live table after. RANGE partition by import_date annual. Retention: 3 years post-supersede then purged unless under platform_legal_holds. S3 T3 migration triggered at 500GB or 50,000 schools (ADR-027).
M23Healthprefix: hlth_17 tablesWave 2

All hlth_* tables use a separate HIPAA-compliant KMS key. Access restricted to roles with health_record:read permission. Classroom module never reads hlth_* tables — it reads sis_student_active_accommodations instead (ADR-030).

TableDefinition
hlth_student_health_recordsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK(sis_students) UNIQUE, school_id UUID, blood_type TEXT, allergies JSONB, emergency_medical_notes TEXT, physician_name TEXT, physician_phone TEXT. Separate HIPAA KMS key. AES-256 at rest for all PII fields.
hlth_medical_conditionsid UUID PK(v7), health_record_id UUID FK, condition_name TEXT NOT NULL, diagnosis_date DATE, is_active BOOLEAN, severity ENUM(MILD,MODERATE,SEVERE), management_plan TEXT.
hlth_immunisationsid UUID PK(v7), health_record_id UUID FK, vaccine_name TEXT NOT NULL, administered_date DATE, due_date DATE, administered_by TEXT, status ENUM(CURRENT,OVERDUE,WAIVED). INDEX(health_record_id, vaccine_name).
hlth_medicationsid UUID PK(v7), health_record_id UUID FK, medication_name TEXT NOT NULL, dosage TEXT, frequency TEXT, route ENUM(ORAL,TOPICAL,INHALER,INJECTION,OTHER), prescribing_physician TEXT, is_self_administered BOOLEAN.
hlth_medication_administrationsid UUID PK(v7), medication_id UUID FK, schedule_entry_id UUID FK(hlth_medication_schedule) nullable (the scheduled slot this administration fulfils — null for unscheduled/PRN administrations), administered_by UUID FK(hr_employees), administered_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (null when was_missed=true), dose_given TEXT, notes TEXT, parent_notified BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, was_missed BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (true when a scheduled dose was not administered — must have missed_reason if true), missed_reason ENUM(STUDENT_ABSENT,STUDENT_REFUSED,MEDICATION_UNAVAILABLE,PARENT_CANCELLED,OTHER) nullable. CHECK: NOT (was_missed = true AND administered_at IS NOT NULL). INDEX(medication_id, administered_at DESC). INDEX(schedule_entry_id, was_missed) WHERE was_missed = true — missed dose audit query.
hlth_nurse_visitsid UUID PK(v7), visited_person_id UUID NOT NULL (SOFT INTEGRITY — resolves to either sis_students.id or hr_employees.id depending on visited_person_type), visited_person_type ENUM(STUDENT,STAFF) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'STUDENT', school_id UUID, nurse_id UUID FK(hr_employees), visit_date TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM(IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'COMPLETED' (IN_PROGRESS: student/staff currently in the nurse office — set on arrival; COMPLETED: visit concluded and recorded), signed_in_at TIMESTAMPTZ, signed_out_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (set when status moves to COMPLETED — enables live nurse office roster), reason TEXT, treatment_given TEXT, parent_notified BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, sent_home BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, sent_home_at TIMESTAMPTZ, follow_up_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, follow_up_notes TEXT nullable, follow_up_date DATE nullable. INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'IN_PROGRESS' — live office roster query. INDEX(visited_person_id, visit_date DESC). PARTIAL INDEX for student visits: (student_id, visit_date DESC) WHERE visited_person_type = 'STUDENT'.
hlth_iep_plansid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK UNIQUE, plan_type ENUM(IEP,504), status ENUM(DRAFT,ACTIVE,REVIEW,EXPIRED), start_date DATE, review_date DATE, end_date DATE, case_manager_id UUID FK(hr_employees). On plan changes: emit iep.accommodation.updated Kafka event → sis_student_active_accommodations updated by SIS consumer (ADR-030).
hlth_iep_goalsid UUID PK(v7), iep_plan_id UUID FK, goal_text TEXT NOT NULL, measurement_criteria TEXT, baseline TEXT, target_value TEXT, current_value TEXT, goal_area TEXT, status ENUM(ACTIVE,MET,NOT_MET,DISCONTINUED).
hlth_iep_goal_progressid UUID PK(v7), goal_id UUID FK, recorded_by UUID FK, progress_value TEXT, observation_notes TEXT, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(goal_id, recorded_at DESC).
hlth_iep_servicesid UUID PK(v7), iep_plan_id UUID FK, service_type TEXT (speech, OT, PT, counselling...), provider_name TEXT, frequency TEXT, minutes_per_session INT, delivery_method ENUM(PULL_OUT,PUSH_IN,CONSULT).
hlth_iep_accommodationsid UUID PK(v7), iep_plan_id UUID FK, accommodation_type TEXT (EXTENDED_TIME, ALTERNATIVE_ASSESSMENT...), description TEXT, applies_to ENUM(ALL_ASSESSMENTS,ALL_ASSIGNMENTS,SPECIFIC), specific_assignment_types TEXT[], effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE. On INSERT/UPDATE: emit iep.accommodation.updated.
hlth_health_alertsid UUID PK(v7), health_record_id UUID FK, alert_type ENUM(ALLERGY,MEDICAL_CONDITION,MEDICATION,DO_NOT_CONTACT), severity ENUM(INFO,WARNING,CRITICAL), description TEXT NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. On INSERT or UPDATE where alert_type=ALLERGY: emit hlth.allergy_alert.changed Kafka event consumed by Food Service module — consumer maintains fds_student_allergen_alerts read model (ADR-030 pattern).
hlth_health_record_accessid UUID PK(v7), health_record_id UUID FK, accessed_by UUID FK, access_reason TEXT, accessed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Audit trail for HIPAA access logging.
hlth_telehealth_providersid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, provider_name TEXT NOT NULL, speciality TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN, booking_url TEXT.
hlth_telehealth_sessionsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, provider_id UUID FK, scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_minutes INT, status ENUM(SCHEDULED,IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED,NO_SHOW,CANCELLED), meeting_url TEXT, session_notes_s3_key TEXT.
hlth_telehealth_documentsid UUID PK(v7), session_id UUID FK, document_type TEXT, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, signature_request_id UUID FK(platform_signature_requests).
M24Libraryprefix: lib_20 tablesWave 3
TableDefinition
lib_locationsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
lib_catalogue_itemsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, isbn TEXT, publisher TEXT, publish_year INT, category TEXT, dewey_decimal TEXT, description TEXT, cover_image_url TEXT. INDEX(school_id, title). GIN(to_tsvector(title││' '││COALESCE(author,''))) for full-text search.
lib_catalogue_copiesid UUID PK(v7), catalogue_item_id UUID FK, location_id UUID FK, barcode TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, condition ENUM(NEW,GOOD,FAIR,POOR,LOST), is_available BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, replacement_value NUMERIC(8,2) nullable (current replacement cost — used for lost item fines and insurance/budget reporting; populated from purchase price or catalogue lookup). Note: available_copies column REMOVED — computed on demand: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE is_available=true, cached in Redis.
lib_checkout_policiesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, patron_type ENUM(STUDENT,STAFF), max_checkouts INT, loan_period_days INT, renewals_allowed INT, overdue_fine_per_day NUMERIC(4,2).
lib_checkoutsid UUID PK(v7), copy_id UUID FK, patron_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the human borrowing the book — covers students and staff uniformly; no need to distinguish by person_type here), checkout_date DATE NOT NULL, due_date DATE NOT NULL, returned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, renewal_count INT DEFAULT 0, status ENUM(ACTIVE,RETURNED,OVERDUE,LOST). RANGE partition by checkout_date annually, 7-year retention. INDEX(patron_id, checkout_date DESC). Partial index WHERE returned_at IS NULL for active checkout query.
lib_holdsid UUID PK(v7), catalogue_item_id UUID FK, patron_id UUID FK, placed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM(PENDING,READY,COLLECTED,EXPIRED,CANCELLED), notified_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(catalogue_item_id, status). INDEX(patron_id, status).
lib_finesid UUID PK(v7), checkout_id UUID FK, patron_id UUID FK, fine_type ENUM(OVERDUE,LOST,DAMAGE), amount NUMERIC(6,2), days_overdue INT, status ENUM(OUTSTANDING,PAID,WAIVED), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, invoice_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → pay_invoices.id nullable — populated when fine is surfaced to the family payment account; set by the Payments Worker on lib.fine.issued Kafka event; null for fines paid directly in cash at the library desk). INDEX(patron_id, status). INDEX(invoice_id) WHERE invoice_id IS NOT NULL. On INSERT with status=OUTSTANDING: emit lib.fine.issued Kafka event → Payments Worker creates pay_invoice_line_item on the student's family_account.
lib_reading_programmesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK, target_books INT, target_pages INT, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, is_active BOOLEAN, target_audience_type ENUM(SCHOOL_WIDE,YEAR_GROUP,CLASS,CUSTOM) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SCHOOL_WIDE' (when SCHOOL_WIDE: any student may participate; when YEAR_GROUP/CLASS: only students in the specified group are enrolled; CUSTOM: explicit student list via lib_programme_enrolments), target_id UUID nullable (SOFT INTEGRITY — resolves to sis_classes.id for CLASS or sis_academic_years year group identifier for YEAR_GROUP — application-layer resolution).
lib_reading_logsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, catalogue_item_id UUID FK, started_date DATE, completed_date DATE, pages_read INT, rating SMALLINT, review_text TEXT. INDEX(student_id, completed_date DESC).
lib_programme_progressUNIQUE(programme_id, student_id). books_read INT DEFAULT 0, pages_read INT DEFAULT 0, last_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_complete BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. On is_complete → true: emit lib.programme.completed Kafka event → Task Worker creates lib_programme_completions row and sends congratulatory notification to student.
lib_programme_completionsAward record generated when a student completes a reading programme. id UUID PK(v7), programme_id UUID FK(lib_reading_programmes) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, books_read INT NOT NULL, pages_read INT NOT NULL, certificate_s3_key TEXT nullable (generated PDF certificate — signed URL on access), awarded_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (the librarian who manually confirmed completion, if required), achievement_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → pfl_achievements.id nullable — when Portfolio module is active, completion auto-creates a pfl_achievements row of type ACADEMIC for the student's portfolio). UNIQUE(programme_id, student_id).
lib_reading_listsCurated booklist created by librarian, teacher, or curriculum team. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT nullable, list_type ENUM(CLASS,YEAR_GROUP,CURRICULUM_UNIT,GENERAL,NEW_ARRIVALS) NOT NULL, created_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, target_class_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → sis_classes.id nullable — for CLASS lists), target_grade_level TEXT nullable (for YEAR_GROUP lists), curriculum_unit_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → cur_units.id nullable — for CURRICULUM_UNIT lists; links library catalogue to M25 Curriculum), academic_year_id UUID FK nullable, is_published BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: visible to students and parents in the library portal), published_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. UNIQUE(school_id, name, academic_year_id). INDEX(school_id, list_type, is_published).
lib_reading_list_itemsIndividual books within a reading list. id UUID PK(v7), reading_list_id UUID FK(lib_reading_lists) NOT NULL, catalogue_item_id UUID FK(lib_catalogue_items) NOT NULL, item_type ENUM(REQUIRED,RECOMMENDED,EXTENSION,REFERENCE) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'RECOMMENDED', sort_order INT NOT NULL, added_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, notes TEXT nullable (librarian or teacher annotation — e.g. 'Chapters 1-4 only', 'Good for reluctant readers'). UNIQUE(reading_list_id, catalogue_item_id). INDEX(reading_list_id, sort_order).
lib_class_set_checkoutsBulk checkout of multiple copies of the same title to a teacher for class use. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, catalogue_item_id UUID FK(lib_catalogue_items) NOT NULL, teacher_patron_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the teacher who has the class set), class_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → sis_classes.id nullable), copy_count INT NOT NULL (number of copies checked out), checkout_date DATE NOT NULL, due_date DATE NOT NULL, returned_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, status ENUM(ACTIVE,PARTIALLY_RETURNED,RETURNED,OVERDUE) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', notes TEXT nullable. INDEX(school_id, status). INDEX(teacher_patron_id, checkout_date DESC). On INSERT: creates copy_count individual lib_checkouts rows linking specific barcodes to this class set checkout via class_set_checkout_id. On all copies returned: status = RETURNED.
lib_interlibrary_loansRecord of a book borrowed from or lent to another school or library within the district or network. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, loan_direction ENUM(BORROWED,LENT) NOT NULL (BORROWED: our library is borrowing from another; LENT: another library is borrowing from us), partner_institution TEXT NOT NULL (name of the other school or library), catalogue_item_id UUID FK(lib_catalogue_items) nullable (for LENT: must reference our own catalogue), title TEXT NOT NULL (denormalised — for BORROWED items not in our catalogue), isbn TEXT nullable, request_date DATE NOT NULL, received_date DATE nullable (for BORROWED), sent_date DATE nullable (for LENT), due_date DATE nullable, returned_date DATE nullable, status ENUM(REQUESTED,IN_TRANSIT,ACTIVE,RETURNED,OVERDUE,LOST) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'REQUESTED'. INDEX(school_id, status). INDEX(school_id, loan_direction, status).
lib_catalogue_import_jobsBulk catalogue import job — ISBN batch lookup, MARC record import, or CSV upload. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, import_type ENUM(ISBN_BATCH,MARC_IMPORT,CSV_UPLOAD,WORLDCAT_SYNC) NOT NULL, source_file_s3_key TEXT nullable (the uploaded file — null for API-based lookups), total_records INT nullable (populated on parse), records_imported INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, records_skipped INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (duplicates detected by ISBN match), records_failed INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, status ENUM(QUEUED,PARSING,IMPORTING,COMPLETED,FAILED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED', initiated_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, error_log_s3_key TEXT nullable (S3 key of CSV error report — row-level errors for librarian review), started_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, status). INDEX(school_id, completed_at DESC).
M40Communicationsprefix: msg_29 tablesWave 1

msg_thread_stats_checkpoint was removed in v5.0. Redis O(1) checkpoints + idempotent event payloads replaced it entirely.

TableDefinition
msg_thread_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, allowed_participant_roles TEXT[], is_system BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. UNIQUE(school_id, name). Examples: TEACHER_PARENT, CLASS_DISCUSSION, ADMIN_STAFF.
msg_threadsHASH(school_id) partition with 64 BUCKETS (ADR-047 — increased from 16; large districts with heavy messaging create hot partitions at 16 buckets). id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, thread_type_id UUID FK, subject TEXT, created_by UUID FK(platform_users), is_archived BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Partition pruning eliminates 63/64 partitions on every inbox query.
msg_messagesRANGE(created_at) monthly via pg_partman, 24-month retention. At 1M students + 500k teachers + 1M parents × 10 msgs/user/month = 250M rows/month. INDEX(thread_id, created_at DESC) per partition. school_id denormalised for (school_id, thread_id, created_at) queries without JOIN. BRIN index on created_at within each partition.
msg_thread_statsUNIQUE(thread_id). message_count INT DEFAULT 0, last_message_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_message_preview TEXT (first 100 chars). Updated by Kafka consumer on msg.message.posted using GREATEST(existing_count, thread_message_count_at_send) — idempotent by construction, safe against replay.
msg_thread_participantsHASH(school_id) 64 BUCKETS (ADR-047 — must match msg_threads partition key for efficient JOINs; increased from 16). UNIQUE(platform_user_id, thread_id, school_id). INDEX(platform_user_id, school_id, thread_id) for inbox query. is_muted BOOLEAN, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
msg_message_attachmentsid UUID PK(v7), message_id UUID FK, filename TEXT, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, file_size_bytes INT, mime_type TEXT. Signed S3 URLs with 15–60 min expiry.
msg_message_readsUNIQUE(message_id, reader_id). read_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Unread count pattern: Redis HASH key inbox:{user_id}:{thread_id} stores unread count, incremented on msg.message.posted (only when is_new=true), cleared (DEL) when user opens thread. Badge total = SUM from Redis HVALS. Never COUNT(*) on msg_messages.
msg_announcementsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, author_id UUID FK, title TEXT, body TEXT NOT NULL, publish_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, audience_type ENUM(ALL_SCHOOL,CLASS,YEAR_GROUP,ROLE,ROUTE,CUSTOM) NOT NULL (ROUTE: announcement targets all guardians of students currently assigned to a specific bus route — used by Transportation Coordinator for route-specific alerts; requires target_route_id), target_route_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → trn_routes.id nullable — required when audience_type=ROUTE; fan-out worker queries trn_student_assignments for active route riders and resolves their guardian_ids from trn_student_eta_preferences), is_recurring BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, recurrence_rule TEXT nullable (iCal RRULE string e.g. FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO — processed by Announcement Scheduler Worker which creates new msg_announcements rows on each recurrence), parent_announcement_id UUID FK(msg_announcements) nullable (for recurring instances: points to the original announcement). INDEX(school_id, publish_at DESC). INDEX(is_recurring, publish_at) WHERE is_recurring = true.
msg_announcement_audiencesPre-computed audience table. INDEX(platform_user_id, delivery_status). Eliminates real-time fan-out computation at send time. Populated by announcement worker when announcement published.
msg_announcement_readsUNIQUE(announcement_id, reader_id). read_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
msg_alert_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, severity ENUM(INFO,WARNING,URGENT,EMERGENCY), default_channels TEXT[], requires_acknowledgement BOOLEAN.
msg_emergency_alertsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, alert_type_id UUID FK, title TEXT NOT NULL, body TEXT NOT NULL, issued_by UUID FK, incident_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → inc_incidents.id), issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM(ACTIVE,RESOLVED). INDEX(school_id, issued_at DESC).
msg_emergency_alert_deliveriesUNIQUE(alert_id, recipient_id). channel ENUM(PUSH,SMS,EMAIL,APP), status ENUM(PENDING,SENT,DELIVERED,FAILED), sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
msg_notification_queueid UUID PK(v7), recipient_id UUID FK, notification_type TEXT, payload JSONB, status ENUM(PENDING,SENT,FAILED,SKIPPED), idempotency_key TEXT, scheduled_for TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Idempotency enforced via Redis SET NX on idempotency_key — no DB UNIQUE constraint (causes deadlocks during emergency alert fan-out at 50k+ concurrent inserts). Partial index (status, scheduled_for) WHERE status='PENDING'.
msg_notification_preferencesUNIQUE(platform_user_id, notification_type). channels TEXT[], is_enabled BOOLEAN, quiet_hours_start TIME, quiet_hours_end TIME.
msg_notification_logid UUID PK(v7), recipient_id UUID, notification_type TEXT, channel ENUM(PUSH,EMAIL,SMS), status ENUM(SENT,DELIVERED,FAILED), provider_ref TEXT, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ. RANGE partition by sent_at monthly.
msg_tagsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, color TEXT. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
msg_user_tagsUNIQUE(platform_user_id, tag_id).
msg_communication_groupsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, group_type ENUM(STATIC,DYNAMIC). Dynamic groups recomputed nightly.
msg_communication_group_membersUNIQUE(group_id, platform_user_id). INDEX(group_id).
msg_digest_batchesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, digest_type ENUM(DAILY,WEEKLY), scheduled_for TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM(PENDING,PROCESSING,SENT,FAILED). INDEX(school_id, scheduled_for).
msg_digest_batch_itemsUNIQUE(digest_batch_id, source_event_id). source_event_type TEXT, source_event_id UUID, summary_text TEXT.
msg_user_blocksUNIQUE(blocker_id, blocked_id). school_id UUID. INDEX(blocker_id).
msg_admin_access_logid UUID PK(v7), admin_id UUID, thread_id UUID, reason TEXT, accessed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Audit trail for admin read access to private threads (FERPA).
msg_translation_requestsid UUID PK(v7), message_id UUID FK, requested_by UUID FK, target_language TEXT, status ENUM(PENDING,COMPLETE,FAILED), translated_text TEXT.
msg_retention_policiesUNIQUE(school_id, thread_type_id). retain_months INT, archive_action ENUM(DELETE,ARCHIVE_COLD). Configures per-type retention. msg_archival_jobs tracks execution.
msg_archival_jobsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, policy_id UUID FK, status ENUM(PENDING,RUNNING,COMPLETE,FAILED), rows_archived INT, run_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
M41Meetingsprefix: mtg_13 tablesWave 2

Module 41 scope: ad-hoc meetings, video recordings, AI minutes, IEP meetings. PTC conference scheduling is in M100 Parent Engagement.

TableDefinition
mtg_conference_eventsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, title TEXT, conference_type ENUM(PARENT_TEACHER,STAFF,BOARD,IEP,TRAINING), start_date DATE, end_date DATE, status ENUM(SCHEDULED,ACTIVE,COMPLETED,CANCELLED).
mtg_meeting_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, default_duration_minutes INT, is_video BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
mtg_meetingsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, meeting_type_id UUID FK, title TEXT NOT NULL, scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_minutes INT, meeting_url TEXT, status ENUM(SCHEDULED,IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED,CANCELLED), organiser_id UUID FK. INDEX(school_id, scheduled_at DESC).
mtg_meeting_participantsUNIQUE(meeting_id, participant_id). role ENUM(HOST,PRESENTER,ATTENDEE,OBSERVER), attended BOOLEAN, join_at TIMESTAMPTZ, leave_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
mtg_meeting_slotsid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK, start_time TIMESTAMPTZ, end_time TIMESTAMPTZ, is_booked BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, booked_by UUID FK.
mtg_agenda_itemsid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK, title TEXT NOT NULL, presenter_id UUID FK, duration_minutes INT, sort_order INT, notes TEXT. INDEX(meeting_id, sort_order).
mtg_meeting_notesid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK UNIQUE, notes_text TEXT, is_approved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, approved_by UUID FK, is_parent_visible BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true and is_approved=true: parent portal shows a read-only summary of meeting notes — teacher controls visibility; individual counseling or sensitive content should never be set parent_visible=true), parent_visible_summary TEXT nullable (optional separate parent-facing summary text — if NULL and is_parent_visible=true, the full notes_text is shown; if populated, only this summary is shown to parents), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
mtg_action_itemsid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK, assignee_id UUID FK, description TEXT NOT NULL, due_date DATE, status ENUM(OPEN,IN_PROGRESS,DONE,CANCELLED), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(assignee_id, status).
mtg_recordingsid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK UNIQUE, s3_key TEXT, duration_seconds INT, file_size_bytes BIGINT, status ENUM(PROCESSING,AVAILABLE,FAILED), consent_confirmed BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. Signed S3 URLs with 15–60 min expiry.
mtg_recording_consentsUNIQUE(recording_id, participant_id). consent_given BOOLEAN, consented_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
mtg_transcription_jobsid UUID PK(v7), recording_id UUID FK UNIQUE, status ENUM(PENDING,PROCESSING,COMPLETE,FAILED), transcript_s3_key TEXT, provider ENUM(AWS_TRANSCRIBE,WHISPER), completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
mtg_ai_minutes_jobsid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK UNIQUE, status ENUM(PENDING,RUNNING,COMPLETE,FAILED), summary_text TEXT, action_items_extracted JSONB, model_version TEXT, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
mtg_iep_meeting_recordsid UUID PK(v7), meeting_id UUID FK, student_id UUID FK (SOFT INTEGRITY → sis_students.id), iep_plan_id UUID FK (SOFT INTEGRITY → hlth_iep_plans.id), attendee_roles JSONB, outcomes_summary TEXT, next_review_date DATE.
M42Publicationsprefix: pub_15 tablesWave 3

Module 42 (ADR-035). Collaborative content publishing with targeted distribution. pub_series and pub_edition are first-class entities enabling versioned, subscription-driven publication workflows.

Approval Model (ADR-035) Staff publish directly — no approval required. Students and parents/community members submit drafts that go through M2 Approval Workflows before publishing. pub_publications.approval_request_id is SOFT INTEGRITY → wsk_approval_requests.id. NULL for staff-authored publications.

### Domain 1 — Series, Editions & Publications (5 tables)

TableDefinition
pub_seriesA named recurring publication brand. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, title TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'The Weekly Eagle', 'District Digest'), description TEXT, publication_type ENUM(NEWSLETTER,BULLETIN,MAGAZINE,PROGRAM,REPORT) NOT NULL, frequency ENUM(DAILY,WEEKLY,FORTNIGHTLY,MONTHLY,TERMLY,ANNUAL,IRREGULAR) NOT NULL, series_logo_s3_key TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_by UUID FK(platform_users), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(school_id, title). A series is the container — editions are instances of it. One-off publications (announcements, single bulletins) do not require a series; they have series_id = NULL.
pub_editionA specific numbered issue of a series. id UUID PK(v7), series_id UUID FK(pub_series) NOT NULL, edition_number INT NOT NULL (auto-incremented per series), edition_label TEXT (e.g. 'Vol. 3 Issue 12' or 'Spring 2026' — display label separate from number), theme TEXT nullable, cover_image_s3_key TEXT, editorial_note TEXT, status ENUM(DRAFT,IN_REVIEW,APPROVED,PUBLISHED,ARCHIVED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT', scheduled_publish_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, editor_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (the edition editor — may differ from series creator), approval_request_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → wsk_approval_requests.id — NULL if editor is staff, ADR-035). UNIQUE(series_id, edition_number). INDEX(series_id, status, published_at DESC).
pub_publicationsA single content document — either a standalone one-off or one edition of a series. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID nullable (NULL for district-level publications), organisation_id UUID FK(organisations) nullable (NULL for school-level publications), title TEXT NOT NULL, publication_type ENUM(NEWSLETTER,BULLETIN,ANNOUNCEMENT,MAGAZINE,PROGRAM,REPORT), series_id UUID FK(pub_series) nullable (NULL for standalone publications), edition_id UUID FK(pub_edition) nullable (NULL for standalone publications; when set, publication belongs to this edition), created_by UUID FK(platform_users), status ENUM(DRAFT,IN_REVIEW,APPROVED,PUBLISHED,ARCHIVED), scheduled_publish_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, approval_request_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → wsk_approval_requests.id — NULL for staff-authored publications, ADR-035). CHECK: (school_id IS NOT NULL OR organisation_id IS NOT NULL) AND NOT (school_id IS NOT NULL AND organisation_id IS NOT NULL) — exactly one of school or district scope. CHECK: edition_id IS NULL OR series_id IS NOT NULL (cannot have an edition without a series). INDEX(school_id, status, published_at DESC) WHERE school_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(organisation_id, status, published_at DESC) WHERE organisation_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(edition_id) WHERE edition_id IS NOT NULL.
pub_publication_collaboratorsid UUID PK(v7), publication_id UUID FK, user_id UUID FK(platform_users), role ENUM(EDITOR,CONTRIBUTOR,REVIEWER,VIEWER), invited_by UUID FK, invited_at TIMESTAMPTZ, accepted_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. UNIQUE(publication_id, user_id).
pub_publication_versionsid UUID PK(v7), publication_id UUID FK, version_number INT NOT NULL, snapshot_content JSONB NOT NULL (full content snapshot at time of version), created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, version_note TEXT. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. UNIQUE(publication_id, version_number). Versions created automatically on status transitions and on explicit save checkpoints.

### Domain 2 — Sections & Content (3 tables)

TableDefinition
pub_sectionsid UUID PK(v7), publication_id UUID FK, title TEXT, content JSONB (rich text — headings, paragraphs, images, embeds), section_type ENUM(ARTICLE,ANNOUNCEMENT,PHOTO_GALLERY,EVENT_SPOTLIGHT,STUDENT_WORK,STAFF_SPOTLIGHT,AD_HOC), sort_order INT, owner_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (NULL = open to all editors), status ENUM(DRAFT,SUBMITTED,APPROVED,REJECTED). INDEX(publication_id, sort_order).
pub_section_contributorsid UUID PK(v7), section_id UUID FK, contributor_id UUID FK(platform_users), contribution_note TEXT, contributed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(section_id, contributor_id). Attribution tracking beyond the designated section owner.
pub_section_commentsid UUID PK(v7), section_id UUID FK, author_id UUID FK, body TEXT NOT NULL, is_resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, resolved_by UUID FK nullable, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, parent_comment_id UUID FK nullable (threaded replies), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(section_id, is_resolved, created_at ASC).

### Domain 3 — Distribution, Audience & Subscriptions (4 tables)

TableDefinition
pub_distribution_listsid UUID PK(v7), publication_id UUID FK, list_name TEXT NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. A publication can have multiple distribution lists combined at send time. Targeting supports: By role (all parents, all staff, all students), by grade or class, by group or club membership.
pub_distribution_rulesid UUID PK(v7), distribution_list_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM(ROLE,GRADE,CLASS,GROUP_MEMBERSHIP) NOT NULL, rule_value TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'PARENT', '3', class UUID, group UUID). Multiple rules per list are OR'd — a recipient matching any rule is included. INDEX(distribution_list_id, rule_type).
pub_distribution_recipientsid UUID PK(v7), distribution_list_id UUID FK, recipient_id UUID FK(platform_users), delivery_channel ENUM(IN_APP,EMAIL,BOTH), delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. Populated by background worker at publish time — never computed on demand (follows cls_gradebook_snapshots pattern). RANGE partition by created_at monthly. INDEX(distribution_list_id, delivered_at) WHERE delivered_at IS NULL — pending delivery queue.
pub_series_subscriptionsOpt-in subscription for a user to automatically receive new editions of a series. id UUID PK(v7), series_id UUID FK(pub_series) NOT NULL, account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, subscribed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, delivery_channel ENUM(IN_APP,EMAIL,BOTH) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'IN_APP', is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, unsubscribed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. UNIQUE(series_id, account_id). INDEX(series_id, is_active). When a new pub_edition is published for a series, the distribution worker queries pub_series_subscriptions WHERE series_id=$1 AND is_active=true and adds those recipients to pub_distribution_recipients in addition to any pub_distribution_rules. Subscriptions are additive — a user may be in both a rule-based list and a subscription list; the worker deduplicates before inserting.

### Domain 4 — Media & Assets (2 tables)

TableDefinition
pub_media_assetsid UUID PK(v7), publication_id UUID FK, uploaded_by UUID FK, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, filename TEXT, media_type ENUM(IMAGE,PDF,VIDEO_EMBED,EXTERNAL_LINK), file_size_bytes INT, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Signed S3 URLs with 60 min expiry.
pub_media_usageid UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK, section_id UUID FK, inserted_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(asset_id, section_id). Enables safe cleanup of unused assets on archive.

### Domain 5 — Templates (1 table)

TableDefinition
pub_templatesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID nullable (null = platform-seeded template), name TEXT NOT NULL, publication_type ENUM match, description TEXT, template_content JSONB (pre-populated sections with placeholder content), is_system BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_by UUID FK nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(COALESCE(school_id,'00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'), name). Platform-seeded templates use is_system=true — seeded once, available to all tenants.

### Kafka Events — M42 Publications

EventConsumersNotes
pub.publication.submitted_for_reviewApproval Workflows (M2), Communications (M40)Triggers approval request; notifies assigned approver
pub.publication.approvedTask Worker (M1)Auto-creates scheduled publish task if scheduled_publish_at is set
pub.publication.publishedCommunications (M40)Fan-out delivery to all recipients via M40 notification infrastructure
pub.publication.rejectedCommunications (M40)Notifies creator with reviewer feedback

M0 — Platform Core: IAM Subsystem (ADR-036)

IAM subsystem (ADR-036): 21 tables. All live in the platform schema as part of M0 Platform Core.

ADR-036 — IAM Subsystem Wave 1 iam_person is the source of truth for real-world human identity. platform_users becomes the account layer — its id values are stable, all existing FKs remain valid. Role assignments are always scoped (iam_scope), time-bounded (effective_from/effective_to), and provenance-labelled (source + source_ref_id). Assignments without a scope are invalid. Guardian access to student records is never implicit. Derived from iam_relationship_access_rule evaluation which produces DERIVED iam_role_assignment rows. Changing a guardian relationship triggers immediate reassessment. Authentication is delegated to the configured external IdP (Keycloak, Auth0, Cognito). CampusOS stores IdP state in iam_federated_identity but never implements authentication logic itself. The platform authorises; the IdP authenticates. Service principals never share platform_users rows with human accounts. account_type = 'SERVICE_ACCOUNT' distinguishes machine accounts from human accounts. Machine identities that require scoped access use iam_service_principal, not platform_users. iam_effective_access_cache is the sole source for hot-path permission checks. Direct JOINs across role_assignment to role to permission tables are forbidden on the request path. Cache staleness SLA: 500ms maximum for active sessions.
IAM Design Contract platform_users is the authentication account record. It holds email, credentials reference, MFA status, last_seen_at, and account_status. It carries person_id FK(iam_person) NOT NULL linking every account to its human identity. No account exists without a person. roles, permissions, role_permissions are the permission vocabulary. iam_role_assignment is the only mechanism for granting access. There is no parallel or legacy access grant path. iam_person.person_type determines which domain projections exist: STAFF → hr_employees; STUDENT → platform_students + sis_students; GUARDIAN → sis_guardians; SUBSTITUTE → platform_substitute_profiles; VOLUNTEER → platform_volunteer_profiles.

Domain 1 — Person ****&**** Account Identity (4 tables)

TableDefinition
iam_personid UUID PK(v7), first_name TEXT NOT NULL, last_name TEXT NOT NULL, preferred_name TEXT, date_of_birth DATE, national_id_hash TEXT (HMAC blind index — same pattern as platform_students), person_type ENUM(STAFF,STUDENT,GUARDIAN,VOLUNTEER,SUBSTITUTE,ALUMNI,EXTERNAL) NOT NULL (SINGLE PRIMARY CLASSIFICATION — DESIGN DECISION: person_type is intentionally a single ENUM value, not a multi-value array or junction. It represents the person's primary relationship to the platform. Dual-role cases are handled at the domain table layer, not by adding a second type to iam_person: (1) A staff member who is also a parent: person_type=STAFF; their guardian relationship is recorded in sis_guardians.person_id FK(iam_person) and sis_family_members.person_id FK(iam_person) — no identity model change required; (2) A substitute who becomes permanent staff: person_type transitions SUBSTITUTE→STAFF; historical sub assignments remain linked to the same iam_person.id; (3) A student who graduates and joins as alumni: person_type transitions STUDENT→ALUMNI. Access is always controlled by iam_role_assignment, never by person_type — a person can hold roles of any type regardless of their person_type. person_type drives UI presentation and default role seeding at provisioning only. OPEN ARCHITECTURE QUESTION: schools with significant numbers of staff-who-are-also-parents may require a sis_staff_guardian_links junction table (staff_id FK(hr_employees), guardian_id FK(sis_guardians), school_id) to make dual-role relationships explicit. This table can be added in Wave 2 without changing the identity model. Log as architecture-phase decision item.), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, merged_into_id UUID FK(iam_person) nullable (for identity merge operations — when two iam_person records are found to represent the same real person, the secondary record sets merged_into_id pointing to the primary; all domain FKs are updated to point to the primary record). PARTITION BY HASH(id) 32 BUCKETS (ADR-041). INDEX(national_id_hash). INDEX(person_type, is_active). iam_person is the authoritative source for all human identity fields (ADR-055).
platform_usersAuthentication account record. One row per login credential holder (human or service account). id UUID PK(v7), person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the human behind this account — every account has an identity; no anonymous accounts), account_status ENUM(ACTIVE,SUSPENDED,LOCKED,PENDING_VERIFICATION,DEACTIVATED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', account_type ENUM(HUMAN,SERVICE_ACCOUNT) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'HUMAN', email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, display_name TEXT, avatar_url TEXT, mfa_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(person_id). INDEX(email). CONSTRAINT: account_type=SERVICE_ACCOUNT rows must have a corresponding iam_service_principal record — enforced at application layer.
iam_identity_providerid UUID PK(v7), name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Google Workspace', 'Microsoft Entra ID', 'Keycloak'), provider_type ENUM(GOOGLE,MICROSOFT,SAML,OIDC,LOCAL,LDAP) NOT NULL, issuer_url TEXT, client_id_encrypted TEXT, metadata_url TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, trust_level ENUM(HIGH,MEDIUM,LOW) DEFAULT 'MEDIUM' (HIGH = district-managed corporate IdP; LOW = consumer social login), auto_provision_accounts BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: first login from this IdP creates a platform_users row automatically), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(issuer_url) WHERE issuer_url IS NOT NULL.
iam_federated_identityLinks a platform_users account to a specific external IdP credential. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, provider_id UUID FK(iam_identity_provider) NOT NULL, subject_id TEXT NOT NULL (the IdP stable subject identifier — sub claim in OIDC), email_at_provider TEXT, display_name_at_provider TEXT, last_sign_in_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sync_status ENUM(ACTIVE,CONFLICT,SUSPENDED,UNLINKED) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', conflict_note TEXT nullable (populated when sync_status = CONFLICT). UNIQUE(provider_id, subject_id) — one subject per provider is globally unique. INDEX(account_id). INDEX(provider_id, email_at_provider) for provisioning lookups.

Domain 2 — Scopes (2 tables)

iam_scope_membership removed per ADR-059. Scope membership is derived from active iam_role_assignment rows — an account is a member of a scope if and only if it has at least one ACTIVE assignment there. No separate membership prerequisite table exists.

TableDefinition
iam_scope_typeDefines the categories of scope that role assignments can be bound to. id UUID PK(v7), code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE (e.g. 'DISTRICT', 'SCHOOL', 'DEPARTMENT', 'CLASS', 'ACTIVITY', 'WORKFLOW', 'PLATFORM'), label TEXT NOT NULL, allows_child_scopes BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, is_system BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. Seeded at platform provisioning. Schools cannot create scope types — only platform migrations can.
iam_scopeA specific instance of a scope. id UUID PK(v7), scope_type_id UUID FK(iam_scope_type) NOT NULL, entity_id UUID NOT NULL (FK to the actual entity — e.g. a school_id, department_id, class_id), entity_table TEXT NOT NULL (DISPLAY-ONLY — the table name the entity_id refers to, for human-readable audit — ADR-028 pattern), label TEXT NOT NULL (denormalised snapshot of the entity name at scope creation time — never updated — ensures scope names are readable in audit records even if the originating entity is later archived), parent_scope_id UUID FK(iam_scope) nullable (for scope hierarchy — a CLASS scope parent is its SCHOOL scope), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(scope_type_id, entity_id). INDEX(parent_scope_id). INDEX(entity_id).

Domain 3 — Role Assignments (3 tables)

TableDefinition
iam_role_assignmentThe core access grant. Assigns a role to an account within a specific scope with full lifecycle and provenance. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, role_id UUID FK(roles) NOT NULL, scope_id UUID FK(iam_scope) NOT NULL, effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (NULL = indefinite), status ENUM(ACTIVE,SUSPENDED,EXPIRED,REVOKED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', source ENUM(MANUAL,HR_SYNC,SIS_DERIVED,GUARDIAN_RELATIONSHIP,WORKFLOW_APPROVAL,EMERGENCY) NOT NULL, source_ref_id UUID nullable (reference to the originating record — wsk_approval_requests.id for WORKFLOW_APPROVAL, iam_break_glass_grant.id for EMERGENCY), assigned_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (null for system-derived assignments), approval_request_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to wsk_approval_requests.id — nullable; populated only for assignments requiring approval), notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(account_id, status) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'. INDEX(scope_id, role_id, status). INDEX(effective_to) WHERE effective_to IS NOT NULL AND status = 'ACTIVE' — expiry sweep job. PARTIAL INDEX: (account_id, scope_id, role_id) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' AND (effective_to IS NULL OR effective_to > now()). The sole access grant mechanism in this system.
iam_role_assignment_historyImmutable audit record of every state change on a role assignment. id UUID PK(v7), assignment_id UUID FK(iam_role_assignment) NOT NULL, changed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, change_type ENUM(CREATED,ACTIVATED,SUSPENDED,REVOKED,EXPIRED,EXTENDED,SOURCE_UPDATED) NOT NULL, previous_status ENUM(ACTIVE,SUSPENDED,EXPIRED,REVOKED) nullable, new_status ENUM(ACTIVE,SUSPENDED,EXPIRED,REVOKED) nullable, previous_effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, new_effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, reason TEXT, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. INDEX(assignment_id, changed_at). RANGE partition by changed_at monthly.
iam_relationship_access_ruleGoverns how person-to-person relationships (guardian to student) translate into access grants. id UUID PK(v7), relationship_type ENUM(GUARDIAN,EMERGENCY_CONTACT,FOSTER_PARENT,PROXY_GUARDIAN,ALUMNI_MENTOR,VOLUNTEER_SUPERVISOR,CONTRACTOR_HOST,MEDICAL_RELEASE_CONTACT) NOT NULL, scope_type_id UUID FK(iam_scope_type) NOT NULL (the scope type within which access is granted — typically SCHOOL), role_id UUID FK(roles) NOT NULL (the role granted to the related person), requires_custody BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: only guardians with has_custody=true in sis_student_guardians qualify), requires_portal_access BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: only sis_student_guardians rows with portal_access=true qualify), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. UNIQUE(relationship_type, scope_type_id, role_id). Rules evaluated at provisioning time and on guardian relationship changes — they drive GUARDIAN_RELATIONSHIP source assignments in iam_role_assignment. When a guardian relationship ends, all GUARDIAN_RELATIONSHIP assignments for that pair are revoked. Access is never implicit.
How guardian access works under ADR-036 1. A guardian is linked to a student via sis_student_guardians (existing table, unchanged). 2. On INSERT or UPDATE to sis_student_guardians, a Kafka event sis.guardian.linked is emitted. 3. The IAM Access Worker consumes sis.guardian.linked, evaluates all active iam_relationship_access_rule rows for GUARDIAN type. 4. For each matching rule, the worker creates an iam_scope row for the student's school (if not already exists), then creates an iam_role_assignment row with source=GUARDIAN_RELATIONSHIP and source_ref_id pointing to the sis_student_guardians row. 5. When the guardian relationship is removed or portal_access is set to false, sis.guardian.unlinked is emitted and the corresponding iam_role_assignment rows are REVOKED. 6. The guardian now appears in iam_effective_access_cache for their school scope with the parent portal role permissions.

Domain 4 — Access Change Events (2 tables)

TableDefinition
iam_access_change_eventImmutable, IAM-specific audit log — distinct from the generic audit_log. Records every grant, revoke, suspension, scope change, IdP link, and account status change at the IAM layer. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, event_type ENUM(ACCOUNT_CREATED,ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED,ACCOUNT_REACTIVATED,ACCOUNT_LOCKED,ROLE_GRANTED,ROLE_REVOKED,ROLE_SUSPENDED,ROLE_EXPIRED,SCOPE_MEMBERSHIP_ADDED,SCOPE_MEMBERSHIP_REMOVED,FEDERATED_IDENTITY_LINKED,FEDERATED_IDENTITY_UNLINKED,GUARDIAN_ACCESS_PROVISIONED,GUARDIAN_ACCESS_REVOKED,MFA_ENROLLED,MFA_REMOVED,PASSWORD_RESET,EMERGENCY_ACCESS_GRANTED,EMERGENCY_ACCESS_REVOKED) NOT NULL, actor_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (null for system-driven events), scope_id UUID FK(iam_scope) nullable, role_id UUID FK(roles) nullable, assignment_id UUID FK(iam_role_assignment) nullable, metadata JSONB, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. RANGE partition by event_at monthly. INDEX(account_id, event_at DESC). INDEX(event_type, event_at DESC). Retention: T1 12 months to T2 7 years (FERPA/compliance minimum).
iam_auth_eventAuthentication-specific events, separate from access change events because volume, retention, and consumers differ. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (null for failed attempts with unknown account), provider_id UUID FK(iam_identity_provider) nullable, event_type ENUM(LOGIN_SUCCESS,LOGIN_FAILURE,MFA_CHALLENGE_ISSUED,MFA_CHALLENGE_PASSED,MFA_CHALLENGE_FAILED,SESSION_CREATED,SESSION_TERMINATED,TOKEN_REFRESHED,TOKEN_REVOKED,SSO_INITIATED,SSO_COMPLETED,SSO_FAILED,ACCOUNT_LOCKED_AUTO) NOT NULL, ip_address_hash TEXT (hashed — never store raw IPs), user_agent_hash TEXT, session_id UUID nullable, failure_reason TEXT nullable, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. RANGE partition by event_at monthly, 90-day T1 retention (authentication events are not educational records — shorter retention appropriate). Kafka consumer writes failed login counts to Redis for account lockout logic — never query this table on the hot path. INDEX(account_id, event_at DESC) WHERE account_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(event_type, event_at DESC).

Domain 5 — Effective Access Cache (1 table)

TableDefinition
iam_effective_access_cacheMaterialized per-account permission set for a given scope. Rebuilt by a background worker on any role assignment change within that scope. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, scope_id UUID FK(iam_scope) NOT NULL, permission_codes TEXT[] NOT NULL (array of all effective resource:action permission codes for this account in this scope, derived from all active role assignments), computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, assignment_version_hash TEXT NOT NULL (SHA-256 of all contributing assignment IDs + their statuses — used to detect staleness). UNIQUE(account_id, scope_id). INDEX(account_id) — all permission checks start here. Redis is the primary cache (5-min TTL); this table is the persistent fallback. Worker rebuilds on: iam_role_assignment INSERT/UPDATE, iam_relationship_access_rule change, role_permission change. Staleness SLA: 500ms maximum for active sessions. prewarmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (ADR-043 — set by IAMCachePrewarmWorker on LOGIN/ROLE_CHANGE events; null means cache was rebuilt reactively not proactively), prewarm_source ENUM(LOGIN,ROLE_CHANGE,MANUAL,SCHEDULED) nullable (tracks pre-warm origin for debugging cold-start issues). Pre-warm target: p95 < 200ms, cache hit rate after pre-warm > 0.99.

Domain 6 — Machine Identity (5 tables)

TableDefinition
iam_service_principalNon-human actor identity for integrations, background jobs, and API clients. id UUID PK(v7), name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, principal_type ENUM(API_CLIENT,INTEGRATION_JOB,WEBHOOK_SENDER,REPORTING_AGENT,ETL_CONNECTOR,BACKGROUND_SERVICE) NOT NULL, owner_team TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_by UUID FK(platform_users), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(name). Machine identities are platform-level — they may be granted access to specific tenant scopes via iam_service_role_assignment.
iam_client_credentialOAuth2 client credentials for service principals. id UUID PK(v7), principal_id UUID FK(iam_service_principal) NOT NULL, credential_type ENUM(CLIENT_SECRET,MTLS_CERTIFICATE,JWT_KEYPAIR) NOT NULL, client_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, credential_hash TEXT NOT NULL (bcrypt hash — never store raw secret), expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(principal_id, is_active).
iam_api_keySimpler key-based auth for lower-trust integrations (webhooks, reporting). id UUID PK(v7), principal_id UUID FK(iam_service_principal) NOT NULL, key_prefix TEXT NOT NULL (first 8 chars of key, stored plaintext for lookup), key_hash TEXT NOT NULL (SHA-256 of full key — never store raw key), scopes TEXT[] NOT NULL (permitted OAuth-style scopes — e.g. ['read:reports', 'write:attendance']), expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(key_prefix). INDEX(principal_id, is_active). Lookup pattern: SELECT WHERE key_prefix=$prefix AND is_active=true, then verify against key_hash.
iam_secret_rotation_logAudit trail for every credential rotation. id UUID PK(v7), principal_id UUID FK(iam_service_principal) NOT NULL, credential_id UUID NOT NULL (DISPLAY-ONLY reference — polymorphic: iam_client_credential.id or iam_api_key.id), credential_type ENUM(CLIENT_SECRET,MTLS_CERTIFICATE,JWT_KEYPAIR,API_KEY) NOT NULL, rotation_reason ENUM(SCHEDULED,COMPROMISED,EXPIRED,MANUAL,POLICY) NOT NULL, rotated_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (null = automated rotation), previous_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, new_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, rotated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE.
iam_service_role_assignmentGrants a service principal access within a specific scope. id UUID PK(v7), principal_id UUID FK(iam_service_principal) NOT NULL, role_id UUID FK(roles) NOT NULL, scope_id UUID FK(iam_scope) NOT NULL, effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, granted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, purpose TEXT NOT NULL (human-readable justification — mandatory for machine access), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. UNIQUE(principal_id, role_id, scope_id) WHERE is_active = true. Service principals follow the same role/permission model as humans but through a separate assignment table to keep human and machine access lifecycle separate.

Kafka Events — IAM Subsystem

EventConsumersNotes
iam.role.assignedIAM Workeriam_effective_access_cache worker, Communications (M40)
iam.role.revokedIAM Workeriam_effective_access_cache worker, Communications (M40)
iam.account.suspendedIAM WorkerAll modules via auth middleware
iam.account.lockedIAM Worker (lockout logic)Communications (M40)
iam.guardian.access.provisionedIAM Access WorkerAnalytics (M110)
sis.guardian.linkedSIS (M20)IAM Access Worker
sis.guardian.unlinkedSIS (M20)IAM Access Worker

Wave 2 IAM Additions (Scoped, Not Yet Designed)

IAM Cache Pre-warming Tables (ADR-043)

TableDefinition
iam_cache_prewarm_queueQueue for IAM cache pre-warming on login and role change events. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, trigger_event ENUM(LOGIN,ROLE_CHANGE,SCOPE_CHANGE,MANUAL) NOT NULL, priority ENUM(IMMEDIATE,NORMAL,BACKGROUND) NOT NULL (IMMEDIATE: processed within 100ms SLA — used for LOGIN events to ensure cache is ready before first API request; NORMAL: background pre-warm on role/scope changes; BACKGROUND: scheduled maintenance pre-warm), status ENUM(QUEUED,PROCESSING,COMPLETED,FAILED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED', queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, error_message TEXT nullable. INDEX(status, priority, queued_at) WHERE status = 'QUEUED' — worker polling query. Completed rows deleted after 24h by cleanup job. IMMEDIATE entries processed by dedicated high-priority worker thread pool separate from NORMAL/BACKGROUND entries.
iam_cache_prewarm_statsPre-warming performance metrics for SLA monitoring. id UUID PK(v7), stat_window_start TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, stat_window_end TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, total_prewarm_requests INT NOT NULL, completed_requests INT NOT NULL, failed_requests INT NOT NULL, avg_prewarm_time_ms INT, p95_prewarm_time_ms INT, p99_prewarm_time_ms INT, cache_hit_rate_before NUMERIC(5,4) (hit rate measured in 5-min window before pre-warm batch), cache_hit_rate_after NUMERIC(5,4) (hit rate measured in 5-min window after pre-warm batch). Target SLAs: p95 < 200ms, cache_hit_rate_after > 0.99. RANGE partition by stat_window_start monthly, 90-day retention. Written by IAMCachePrewarmWorker every 5 minutes.
Planned for Wave 2 — not in current ERD iam_delegation_policy — governs who can assign which roles in which scope with what approval requirements. iam_break_glass_grant — emergency access with mandatory justification, time limit, notification targets, and post-event review status. Referenced by iam_role_assignment.source = EMERGENCY. iam_impersonation_session — support/admin impersonation with explicit reason, limited duration, UI banner requirement, and full audit trail. iam_effective_access_cache scope inheritance — parent scope permissions cascade to child scopes (e.g. DISTRICT role grants access in all SCHOOL scopes under that district). iam_mfa_policy — per-scope MFA enforcement rules (e.g. health_record:read requires step-up MFA).

M0 — Platform Core: Data Import Subsystem (v6.6 Addition)

Platform Data Import subsystem (ADR-040): 6 tables. All live in the platform schema as part of M0 Platform Core.

ADR-040 — Platform Data Import Subsystem Purpose: provide a governed, auditable, all-or-nothing import pipeline for three distinct use cases: (1) one-time tenant onboarding migration from a legacy SIS/HRIS (PowerSchool, Skyward, iSAMS, Bromcom, SIMS, Infinite Campus), (2) self-service bulk import by school admins (CSV upload of new students, staff roster updates), (3) initial data seeding at provisioning time. NOT for ongoing directory sync — that is handled by iam_provisioning_job and iam_federated_identity (Wave 2 IAM). NOT for timetable imports — M22 Academic Scheduling owns sch_timetable_imports for its domain. This subsystem handles cross-module onboarding imports that touch multiple domains simultaneously. Design principle: every import follows the same pipeline — UPLOAD → PARSE → VALIDATE (all rows) → OPERATOR_REVIEW (if errors) → COMMIT (all-or-nothing) / ROLLBACK. No partial import state (ADR-017 principle applied platform-wide). Validation errors are surfaced to the operator in a human-readable error report before any row is committed. Target module routing: each import job declares a target_module. The platform import engine routes committed rows to the owning module's domain tables. The import engine is the sole writer during an import job — domain modules do not write directly while an import is in flight for their schema. Idempotency: import jobs are idempotent by design. A job that fails mid-commit can be retried — the engine checks for existing rows by natural key before inserting. Re-running a completed job is rejected at the application layer. Retention: platform_import_job_rows raw source data is deleted 90 days after job completion unless the job is under a platform_legal_hold. platform_import_run_log is immutable and retained 7 years (FERPA — import of educational records is itself an educational record action).

Import Subsystem Tables (6 tables)

TableDefinition
platform_import_source_registryRegistered source system formats and their parser configurations. id UUID PK(v7), source_system TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'POWERSCHOOL', 'SKYWARD', 'ISAMS', 'BROMCOM', 'SIMS', 'INFINITE_CAMPUS', 'CSV_GENERIC', 'JSON_GENERIC'), version TEXT, target_module ENUM(SIS,HR,COMMUNICATIONS,ENROLLMENT,ALL) NOT NULL, parser_class TEXT NOT NULL (the fully-qualified Java class name of the registered parser for this source), default_mapping_config JSONB (default field mapping template for this source system — operators can override per-tenant via platform_import_mappings), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, notes TEXT. UNIQUE(source_system, version, target_module). Seeded at platform provisioning. New source systems added by platform engineering only — not by tenant admins.
platform_import_jobsOne row per import attempt, from file upload through to completion or rollback. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK(schools) NOT NULL, source_registry_id UUID FK(platform_import_source_registry) NOT NULL, target_module ENUM(SIS,HR,COMMUNICATIONS,ENROLLMENT,ALL) NOT NULL, initiated_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, file_s3_key TEXT NOT NULL (the uploaded source file — retained until 90 days post-completion), file_name TEXT NOT NULL, file_size_bytes BIGINT, row_count_parsed INT, row_count_valid INT, row_count_invalid INT, row_count_committed INT, status ENUM(UPLOADED,PARSING,PARSE_FAILED,VALIDATING,VALIDATION_COMPLETE,AWAITING_OPERATOR_REVIEW,COMMITTING,COMPLETE,ROLLED_BACK,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UPLOADED', dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (dry_run=true runs full parse and validate but never commits — used for operator preview), error_summary TEXT nullable (high-level summary of validation failures for operator dashboard), committed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, status). INDEX(school_id, created_at DESC). On status → COMPLETE: emit platform.import.completed Kafka event. On status → ROLLED_BACK: emit platform.import.rolled_back.
platform_import_mappingsTenant-level field mapping overrides from source system schema to CampusOS schema.
