Operators configure these once per source system before running an import. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK(schools) NOT NULL, source_registry_id UUID FK(platform_import_source_registry) NOT NULL, field_mappings JSONB NOT NULL (array of {source_field, target_table, target_column, transform_fn nullable, default_value nullable, is_required BOOLEAN}), created_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(school_id, source_registry_id). When no tenant-level mapping exists, platform_import_source_registry.default_mapping_config is used.
platform_import_job_rowsStaging rows from the source file. One row per source record, retained through validation and used as input to the commit phase. id UUID PK(v7), job_id UUID FK(platform_import_jobs) NOT NULL, row_index INT NOT NULL (zero-based row number in source file — for error reporting), raw_source_data JSONB NOT NULL (the parsed source row before mapping — NULLed 90 days post-job-completion), mapped_data JSONB nullable (the row after field mapping is applied — populated during VALIDATING phase), row_status ENUM(PENDING,VALID,INVALID,COMMITTED,SKIPPED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', committed_record_id UUID nullable (the id of the created/updated record in the target domain table after commit), committed_table TEXT nullable (DISPLAY-ONLY — the target table name). UNIQUE(job_id, row_index). INDEX(job_id, row_status). RANGE partition by created_at monthly. Raw source data NULLed by ImportDataCompactionWorker on platform.import.completed event — same pattern as sch_import_slot_metadata.
platform_import_validation_errorsValidation failures per row, per field. Surfaced to the operator as a structured error report before any commit decision. id UUID PK(v7), job_id UUID FK(platform_import_jobs) NOT NULL, row_id UUID FK(platform_import_job_rows) NOT NULL, row_index INT NOT NULL, field_name TEXT NOT NULL (the source field that failed validation), error_code TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING', 'INVALID_DATE_FORMAT', 'DUPLICATE_NATURAL_KEY', 'REFERENCE_NOT_FOUND', 'VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE'), error_message TEXT NOT NULL (human-readable, suitable for displaying directly to operator), severity ENUM(ERROR,WARNING) NOT NULL (ERROR = blocks commit; WARNING = commit allowed with operator acknowledgement). INDEX(job_id, severity). INDEX(job_id, row_index). Errors are immutable once written — no UPDATE or DELETE. A corrected re-import creates a new job.
platform_import_run_logImmutable chronological log of every phase transition for an import job. Provides a full audit trail from file upload to commit or rollback. id UUID PK(v7), job_id UUID FK(platform_import_jobs) NOT NULL, phase ENUM(UPLOADED,PARSING,PARSE_FAILED,VALIDATING,VALIDATION_COMPLETE,AWAITING_OPERATOR_REVIEW,COMMITTING,COMPLETE,ROLLED_BACK,CANCELLED) NOT NULL, actor_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (null for system-driven transitions), rows_processed_at_phase INT nullable, duration_ms INT nullable, notes TEXT, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. INDEX(job_id, recorded_at ASC). RANGE partition by recorded_at monthly. Retention: 7 years (FERPA — import of educational records is an educational record action).

Import Pipeline Flow

StepStatusWhat happensWho acts
1UPLOADEDOperator uploads source file via admin UI. platform_import_jobs row created. File stored in S3.*Operator (school admin or platform SRE)*
2PARSINGImport engine reads file, creates one platform_import_job_rows row per source record with raw_source_data populated.*System (ImportParserWorker)*
3VALIDATINGEngine applies platform_import_mappings to each row, populates mapped_data, runs all validation rules. Writes platform_import_validation_errors for every failure.*System (ImportValidationWorker)*
4AWAITING_OPERATOR_REVIEWIf any ERROR-severity validation failures exist, status pauses here. Operator reviews error report, decides to fix source file and re-upload (new job) or proceed with WARNING-only errors.*Operator*
5COMMITTINGAll rows with status=VALID are committed to target domain tables in a single transaction. All-or-nothing: any failure rolls back the entire commit. Committed rows update platform_import_job_rows.committed_record_id.*System (ImportCommitWorker)*
6aCOMPLETEAll valid rows committed. platform.import.completed Kafka event emitted. raw_source_data scheduled for NULLing after 90 days.*System*
6bROLLED_BACKCommit failed or operator cancelled. No domain records written. platform.import.rolled_back emitted. Job may be retried.*System / Operator*

Supported Source Systems (initial registry)

Source SystemTarget ModuleFormatNotes
PowerSchoolSISCSV export (standard schema)*Dominant US K-12 SIS. Student records, attendance history, guardian contacts, discipline.*
SkywardSIS + HRCSV export*Common in US districts. Student + staff records.*
iSAMSSISCSV / XML export*Common UK independent schools. Full student and timetable data.*
BromcomSISCSV export*UK state schools. Student records, attendance, exclusions.*
SIMS (Capita)SISXML export*UK legacy SIS. Large installed base.*
Infinite CampusSISCSV export*US district SIS. Student records, guardian contacts.*
CSV_GENERICANYCSV with header row*Fallback for any source system not explicitly registered. Operator must configure platform_import_mappings manually.*
JSON_GENERICANYJSON array*For integrations that export JSON. Operator configures mappings manually.*

Kafka Events — Data Import Subsystem

EventConsumersNotes
platform.import.completedPlatform Core (M0)M110 Analytics, Communications (M40)
platform.import.rolled_backPlatform Core (M0)Communications (M40)
platform.import.validation_failedPlatform Core (M0)Communications (M40)

ADR-037 — Scope Inheritance ****&**** Long-Term FK Anchor Contract

Scope Hierarchy Rules (ADR-037) SCHOOL inherits from DISTRICT. DEPARTMENT, CLASS, and ACTIVITY inherit from SCHOOL. WORKFLOW inherits from SCHOOL or DISTRICT. PLATFORM has no parent — it is the root. Inheritance is transitive upward: a DISTRICT admin role flows down to all SCHOOL scopes under that district. A CLASS role does not flow up to SCHOOL. Inheritance is additive only. Child scopes cannot narrow permissions granted at a parent scope. If restriction is needed, do not grant the parent-scope role — grant a narrower child-scope role directly. No deny rules exist. Deny-by-default is the deny mechanism (ADR-036 Principle 1). Absence of an active assignment is the deny. Cache rebuild: when iam_effective_access_cache is rebuilt for an account in a given scope, the worker traverses parent_scope_id upward and unions all iam_role_assignment rows across all ancestor scopes. Scope inheritance is evaluated at cache-build time — no recursive SQL on the hot path.
FK Identity Contract (ADR-037, completed in ADR-055) iam_person is the canonical FK target for all domain table references to a human being. This is the design as built — there is no migration and no legacy path. Three FK categories — mutually exclusive: (1) Human identity: who is this person → FK(iam_person). Used when the row represents a person's participation, membership, ownership, or data subject status. (2) Auth/session/audit: who is acting → FK(platform_users). Used for created_by, reviewed_by, actor_id, account_id. (3) Role context: what role are they playing → domain projection ID (hr_employees.id, sis_students.id, sis_guardians.id). Every domain table that references a human uses FK(iam_person). This is enforced by ADR-055 and applies to all modules. See ADR-055 for the complete list of tables and the column naming convention. platform_users references in domain tables are only correct when the column tracks an authenticated action — never for identifying who a person is.

ADR-038 — Finance ****&**** Accounting Boundary Definition (M83)

M81 vs M83 Boundary (ADR-038) M81 Enrollment & Payments owns: the invoice, the payment event, the Stripe intent, the ledger entry, financial aid awards, lunch account balances. M81 is Accounts Receivable — families owe the school. M83 Finance & Accounting will own: what those transactions mean financially — which budget they hit, how they flow to the GL, what the fund balance is, what the board report shows. M83 is Accounts Payable — the school owes vendors. Hard boundary: M81 and M83 share no tables. GL entries are fed by M81 payment Kafka events — M81 never writes to GL tables directly. Planned M83 scope: fin_suppliers (supplier/vendor master — see below), fin_chart_of_accounts (GL account hierarchy), fin_gl_entries (double-entry ledger), fin_budgets, fin_budget_lines, fin_funds (fund accounting: general fund, special revenue, capital projects, debt service), fin_grants (award amounts, drawdown tracking, reporting periods), fin_ap_vouchers (vendor invoices — distinct from family invoices in M81), fin_ap_payments, fin_reconciliation_runs, fin_board_report_snapshots. Tables are not yet designed in full. This ADR defines the boundary and the supplier master pattern. Full schema design occurs at Wave 4 commencement.
Supplier Master Pattern (ADR-038 Updated — fin_suppliers pulled forward to Wave 2 per ADR-051) Two-table supplier model: tkt_vendors (Wave 2, operational) and fin_suppliers (Wave 2, financial — pulled forward). These are different shapes serving different purposes and must not be collapsed into one table. tkt_vendors (M60 Helpdesk — Wave 2): operational vendor registry for companies that perform repair and maintenance work. Fields: vendor_type, contact_name, contact_phone, is_preferred. Used by IT admins assigning repair tickets and facilities coordinators assigning work orders. Intentionally lean — no financial data. fin_suppliers (M81/M83 — Wave 2, pulled forward per ADR-051): the complete supplier/vendor master for all companies the school does business with. The full address book. Fields: legal_name, trading_name, tax_id (EIN/TIN, encrypted), payment_terms, preferred_payment_method, bank_account_reference (encrypted), default_gl_account_code (placeholder for Wave 4 GL), w9_s3_key, is_1099_required, supplier_category, notes, is_active, tkt_vendor_id SOFT INTEGRITY bridge. Contacts managed via fin_supplier_contacts. Bridge relationship: tkt_vendor_id on fin_suppliers is nullable and optional. A textbook publisher is a fin_supplier with no tkt_vendors record. A plumbing contractor is both. The bridge is a DB FK (both tables are now in the tenant schema at Wave 2). Wave 4 additions to fin_suppliers: default_gl_account_code will become a proper FK to fin_chart_of_accounts once the GL is designed. No other structural changes expected — the supplier master shape is locked. Activity/club finances and extracurricular budgets: M64 Extracurricular may reference fin_supplier.id via SOFT INTEGRITY for vendor payments (e.g. paying a coach or venue). M64 never owns the payment record — it links to fin_ap_vouchers in M83.
ADR-051 — fin_suppliers Pull-Forward Decision DECISION: fin_suppliers and fin_supplier_contacts are pulled forward from Wave 4 (M83) to Wave 2 (M81). Status: ACCEPTED. RATIONALE: Finance Administrators need to manage vendor relationships, W-9 records, payment terms, and contact information before the full GL is built. tkt_vendors is insufficient — it has no tax ID, payment terms, bank account reference, or compliance fields. Schools cannot wait until Wave 4 to maintain a compliant supplier address book. SCOPE OF PULL-FORWARD: fin_suppliers and fin_supplier_contacts moved to Wave 2 per ADR-051. Minimal GL backbone (fin_chart_of_accounts, fin_funds, fin_journal_batches, fin_gl_entries, fin_accounting_periods, fin_budgets, fin_budget_lines) added per ADR-058. Remaining M83 Wave 4 scope: fin_ap_vouchers, fin_ap_payments, fin_reconciliation_runs, fin_board_report_snapshots, fin_grants. INTEGRATION POINT: fin_suppliers.default_gl_account_code is a TEXT placeholder in Wave 2. At Wave 4 commencement, a migration converts this to a proper FK to fin_chart_of_accounts. This is a backward-compatible online DDL change requiring no data migration. MODULE HOME: fin_suppliers and fin_supplier_contacts live in the M81 module prefix space at Wave 2 (using fin_ prefix to signal their eventual home in M83). They migrate to M83 module documentation at Wave 4 commencement with no schema change.

ADR-052 — DPO Compliance Suite

Data Protection Officer Compliance Suite (ADR-052) DECISION: A dedicated dpo_ table namespace is introduced to support the Data Protection Officer role. 12 new tables cover the full GDPR/FERPA compliance workflow surface. Status: ACCEPTED. RATIONALE: Compliance obligations (GDPR Article 30 ROPA, Article 35 DPIAs, Article 28 DPAs, breach notification, data subject rights) require structured, auditable records that cannot be satisfied by general-purpose tables. The DPO needs a compliance dashboard that is distinct from operational school administration. TABLE NAMESPACE: All tables use the dpo_ prefix. They live in the tenant schema (school-scoped) except where noted. For multi-school organisations the DPO role is scoped at ORGANISATION level — they can query dpo_ tables across all schools in their org. RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING TABLES: dpo_subject_access_requests extends compliance_ferpa_requests (FERPA-specific) to cover GDPR data subject rights broadly. dpo_processing_activities is the Article 30 ROPA — it references dpo_retention_policies and dpo_dpias. dpo_processing_consent_records supplements platform_signature_requests (which handles document signatures) for specific processing consent. audit_log gains data_subject_id and action_category columns to enable the cross-module DPO subject access query without expensive full-table scans. AGE-18 RIGHTS TRANSFER: platform_students.data_subject_is_self is set to true by DataSubjectTransferWorker on the student's 18th birthday. This flag transfers data subject rights from parent/guardian to the student directly. All subsequent SARs, erasure requests, and consent records reference the student as the data_subject_id. Guardian portal_access is not automatically revoked — the student must explicitly grant or revoke it post-transfer. BREACH NOTIFICATION CLOCK: dpo_data_breach_records.discovery_date triggers the 72-hour GDPR supervisory authority notification window. Task Worker creates an URGENT escalating task on INSERT. If supervisory_authority_notified_at remains null after 70 hours, a PAGE-level alert fires to the DPO and platform SRE. DPIA TRIGGER RULE: Any dpo_processing_activities row with high_risk_processing=true that lacks a linked dpo_dpias record is flagged in the DPO compliance dashboard as a gap requiring immediate action. PROCESSOR REGISTER GAP DETECTION: dpo_third_party_processors rows with dpa_in_place=false are surfaced as outstanding action items. The platform itself (CampusOS) appears as the first processor in every school's register — the platform-level DPA is pre-populated on tenant provisioning.

ADR-039 — Master Data Governance

Identity Ownership & Survivorship Rules (ADR-039) Canonical human identity: iam_person. Previously split across platform_users (staff/guardians), platform_students (students), platform_substitute_profiles (substitutes). Canonical student identity: platform_students (portability across school transfers) + iam_person (universal identity root) coexist intentionally. platform_students carries portable academic identity; iam_person carries universal person identity. Guardian deduplication: one iam_person row, two domain projection rows (sis_guardians + hr_employees if also staff), one platform_users row. Merge rules: merges are one-way. Losing iam_person record gets merged_into_id set and is_active=false. All iam_role_assignment, iam_scope_membership, and domain projection FKs are updated to the surviving record. Merges require iam_break_glass_grant audit trail. Split rules: a person incorrectly merged into another requires a support-level operation with mandatory iam_break_glass_grant record and post-event review. Splits cannot be self-service. Survivorship when IdP, HR, and SIS disagree on name/email: resolution order is IdP > HR > SIS. Conflicts set iam_federated_identity.sync_status=CONFLICT and populate conflict_note. A human must resolve conflicts — no automatic silent overwrite. This is deliberate: silent survivorship is how identity merges go wrong in school systems. Employee identity vs platform user: hr_employees is a domain projection of platform_users (account) toward iam_person. An employee is a person with an employment relationship. Not all platform_users are employees and not all employees have full platform_users accounts (e.g. onboarding substitutes).

Wave 1 Pre-Flight Checklist

The Foundation phase has two sub-phases. Platform Core schema provisioning (M0 tables deployed) is necessary but not sufficient. The following operational validators must pass before any Wave 1 domain module is considered shippable to production.

#ValidatorWhy it mattersBlocking?
1IAM effective access rebuild under concurrent load — iam_effective_access_cache rebuild tested across simultaneous role assignment changes for multiple tenants. Cache staleness verified ≤ 500ms SLA.*Incorrect permissions on first login or after role change cause silent access failures or over-permission bugs that are hard to trace after the fact.*BLOCKING
2Tenant freeze/rebalance behavior (ADR-031) — is_frozen write-gate validated across all module workers, API endpoints, Kafka consumers, and scheduled jobs for a test tenant migration.*A production rebalance that doesn't pause all writers risks cross-schema data corruption. Impossible to retrofit safely after data exists.*BLOCKING
3platform_reference_health nightly jobs operational (ADR-028) — SoftIntegrityVerificationWorker running, alerting on < 0.99 resolution rate, results visible in platform_reference_health.*SOFT INTEGRITY references are the primary cross-module FK pattern. Without health monitoring, silent reference breakage goes undetected in production.*BLOCKING
4Audit/event retention partition management live — audit_log, iam_access_change_event, and iam_auth_event pg_partman jobs running, partition creation verified, retention_drift_days within ±2 days.*FERPA requires audit log retention from day one. A retroactive partition structure change after data exists is operationally painful and risks data loss.*BLOCKING
5At least one service principal pattern proven — Task Worker (M1), IAM Access Worker, and Cache Rebuild Worker each have scoped iam_service_principal + iam_service_role_assignment records and operate via iam_api_key authentication.*Internal workers need machine identities. Using hardcoded secrets or human account credentials for workers is a security and operational debt that compounds.*BLOCKING
6iam_break_glass_grant operational (Wave 1.5) — emergency access grant flow tested end-to-end: justification captured, temporary iam_role_assignment created with source=EMERGENCY, iam_access_change_event recorded, time expiry enforced.*Without governed emergency access, operators either can't respond to crises or respond by granting permanent access — both are unacceptable for a platform holding health and HIPAA data.*BLOCKING
7iam_delegation_policy seeded for school-level admin role assignment (Wave 1.5) — school principals can assign lunch monitor, club sponsor, and substitute coverage roles without requiring platform admin intervention.*Without delegation, every role change requires platform admin involvement. This is operationally infeasible at scale and blocks school self-service from day one.*BLOCKING
8Dead letter queue (platform_dlq_messages) monitored — DLQ dashboard showing consumer group lag, retry counts, and alert thresholds per Kafka topic configured and tested.*Unmonitored DLQ means silent Kafka consumer failures that look like missing data rather than infrastructure errors.*WARNING
9Redis memory and eviction policy verified — maxmemory-policy=volatile-lru, provisioned at 60% utilization, alerts at 75% WARNING and 85% PAGE configured per ADR-006.*Permission cache and notification fan-out idempotency keys both depend on Redis. An eviction under memory pressure without the right policy silently breaks both.*WARNING

M0 — Platform Core: IAM Wave 1.5 Additions

2 tables promoted from Wave 2 to Wave 1.5 (required before broad Wave 1 rollout). from 18 to 20 tables. Total M0: 52 → 54 tables.

iam_delegation_policy

TableDefinition
iam_delegation_policyGoverns who can assign which roles in which scope context with what approval requirements and duration limits. id UUID PK(v7), delegator_role_id UUID FK(roles) NOT NULL (the role that grants delegation authority — e.g. SCHOOL_PRINCIPAL), target_role_id UUID FK(roles) NOT NULL (the role the delegator is allowed to assign), scope_type_constraint ENUM(SCHOOL,DEPARTMENT,CLASS,ACTIVITY,WORKFLOW) NOT NULL (delegation is only valid within this scope type — a principal cannot delegate district-level roles), requires_approval BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: delegation attempts create a wsk_approval_request before taking effect), max_duration_days INT nullable (null = indefinite; non-null = delegated assignments automatically expire after this many days), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_by UUID FK(platform_users), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(delegator_role_id, target_role_id, scope_type_constraint). Seeded at provisioning with safe defaults: principals can assign LUNCH_MONITOR, CLUB_SPONSOR, SUBSTITUTE_COVERAGE, SUBSTITUTE_COORDINATOR without approval. Assigning HEALTH_RECORDS_ADMIN or DISTRICT_FINANCE_ADMIN requires approval and is not delegatable by school-level roles.

iam_break_glass_grant

TableDefinition
iam_break_glass_grantEmergency access grant with mandatory justification, time limit, notification, and post-event review. id UUID PK(v7), account_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (the account receiving emergency access), role_id UUID FK(roles) NOT NULL (the emergency role granted), scope_id UUID FK(iam_scope) NOT NULL (the scope within which emergency access is granted), granted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (the platform or school admin authorizing the grant), justification TEXT NOT NULL (minimum 20 characters — empty or trivial justifications rejected at application layer), auto_trigger_rule TEXT nullable (the system rule that triggered automatic grant, if applicable — e.g. 'NURSE_CRISIS_ACCESS_RULE'), effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL (mandatory — no indefinite emergency access), notification_sent_to UUID[] (array of account IDs notified of this grant — always includes the school's security officer and data privacy officer), post_event_review_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, post_event_review_completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, post_event_review_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, review_notes TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(account_id, expires_at). INDEX(expires_at) WHERE post_event_review_completed_at IS NULL — overdue review sweep. On INSERT: (1) create iam_role_assignment with source=EMERGENCY and source_ref_id=this.id; (2) emit iam_access_change_event EMERGENCY_ACCESS_GRANTED; (3) write iam_auth_event. On expiry: the expiry sweep job sets the linked iam_role_assignment status=EXPIRED and emits EMERGENCY_ACCESS_REVOKED.
M27Student Servicesprefix: svc_25 tablesWave 3

Module 27. svc_ prefix. Tenant schema. 20 tables across 5 domains. Core Academic group. Wave 3.

M27 Scope Boundary IN SCOPE: counseling caseloads and session notes, formal referral workflows (teacher to counselor to external), MTSS/RTI tier assignments and intervention progress monitoring, behavior intervention plans (BIPs), student wellbeing check-ins. OUT OF SCOPE: IEP/504 plans and accommodations (owned by M23 Health), nurse visits and medical records (M23 Health), campus-wide emergency response (M91 Incident & Emergency), discipline incidents (M20 SIS Core). Student Services coordinates with Health and SIS but does not own their data. Cross-module links: SOFT INTEGRITY references to sis_students, sis_discipline_incidents, hlth_iep_plans, cls_gradebook_snapshots (at-risk read model). Emits svc.referral.created and svc.tier.changed Kafka events consumed by M110 Analytics. Data sensitivity: svc_session_notes are counseling records protected under FERPA with restricted access. Separate permission code required: student_counseling_record:read. School counselors, school psychologists, and administrators with explicit grants may access. Teachers may not access session notes.

### Domain 1 — Caseloads & Referrals (4 tables)

TableDefinition
svc_caseloadsCounselor-to-student assignment within a school. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, counselor_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, primary_concern ENUM(ACADEMIC,BEHAVIORAL,SOCIAL_EMOTIONAL,ATTENDANCE,CRISIS,TRANSITION,GENERAL) NOT NULL, is_primary_counselor BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (false for co-counselors — a student may have one primary and one secondary counselor simultaneously; the UNIQUE constraint below only restricts one PRIMARY per student per year), status ENUM(ACTIVE,CLOSED,TRANSFERRED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', opened_at DATE NOT NULL, closed_at DATE nullable, closure_reason TEXT nullable, notes TEXT. UNIQUE(student_id, academic_year_id) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' AND is_primary_counselor = true — one primary counselor per student per year. UNIQUE(counselor_id, student_id, academic_year_id) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' — one record per counselor per student per year. INDEX(counselor_id, status). INDEX(student_id, academic_year_id).
svc_referral_typesSchool-configured referral categories. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, referral_category ENUM(INTERNAL,EXTERNAL,CRISIS) NOT NULL (INTERNAL = to school counselor; EXTERNAL = to outside provider; CRISIS = immediate response protocol), default_priority ENUM(LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,URGENT) DEFAULT 'MEDIUM', requires_parent_notification BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
svc_referralsFormal referral workflow from source (teacher, admin, self) to counselor or external provider. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, referral_type_id UUID FK(svc_referral_types) NOT NULL, referred_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, assigned_to_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (the counselor assigned to handle this referral), priority ENUM(LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,URGENT) NOT NULL, concern_description TEXT NOT NULL, status ENUM(PENDING,ACCEPTED,IN_PROGRESS,RESOLVED,CLOSED,DECLINED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', source_incident_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to sis_discipline_incidents.id — nullable; populated when referral originates from a discipline event), parent_notified BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, parent_notified_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(student_id, status). INDEX(assigned_to_id, status) WHERE status NOT IN ('RESOLVED','CLOSED','DECLINED'). On INSERT: emit svc.referral.created Kafka event.
svc_referral_activityImmutable activity log per referral. id UUID PK(v7), referral_id UUID FK NOT NULL, actor_id UUID FK NOT NULL, activity_type ENUM(STATUS_CHANGE,ASSIGNMENT_CHANGE,NOTE_ADDED,PARENT_NOTIFIED,ESCALATED,EXTERNAL_CONTACT_MADE) NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE. INDEX(referral_id, created_at ASC).

### Domain 2 — Sessions & Notes (2 tables)

TableDefinition
svc_sessionsA counseling session record. id UUID PK(v7), counselor_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, school_id UUID NOT NULL, session_date DATE NOT NULL, duration_minutes INT, session_type ENUM(INDIVIDUAL,GROUP,CRISIS,CHECK_IN,PARENT_MEETING,CONSULTATION) NOT NULL, primary_caseload_id UUID FK(svc_caseloads) nullable (the primary caseload this session is billed to — null for group sessions and parent-only meetings), referral_id UUID FK(svc_referrals) nullable (the referral that prompted this session, if any), status ENUM(SCHEDULED,COMPLETED,NO_SHOW,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(counselor_id, session_date DESC). INDEX(primary_caseload_id, session_date DESC) WHERE primary_caseload_id IS NOT NULL. NOTE: For GROUP sessions, participants are recorded in svc_session_participants. For INDIVIDUAL sessions, primary_caseload_id identifies the student.
svc_session_participantsLinks students to a counseling session — mandatory for GROUP sessions, optional for INDIVIDUAL (can be omitted when primary_caseload_id is set). id UUID PK(v7), session_id UUID FK(svc_sessions) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, caseload_id UUID FK(svc_caseloads) nullable (the student's active caseload at time of session), attendance_status ENUM(ATTENDED,NO_SHOW,LATE) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ATTENDED'. UNIQUE(session_id, student_id). INDEX(student_id, session_id).
svc_session_notesFERPA-protected counseling session notes. One row per student per session — enabling group sessions to have individual notes per participant. id UUID PK(v7), session_id UUID FK(svc_sessions) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL (the specific student this note concerns), notes_text TEXT NOT NULL, goals_addressed TEXT[], follow_up_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, follow_up_notes TEXT, is_locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (once locked, notes are immutable), locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, locked_by UUID FK nullable. UNIQUE(session_id, student_id) — one note per student per session; FERPA requirement — each student's record contains only information about them. SEPARATE HIPAA-EQUIVALENT ENCRYPTION KEY from hlth_* tables. Access restricted to roles with student_counseling_record:read permission. audit_log records every access.

### Domain 3 — MTSS / RTI (3 tables)

TableDefinition
svc_mtss_tiersMTSS/RTI tier assignment for a student within an academic year. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, tier ENUM(TIER_1,TIER_2,TIER_3) NOT NULL (TIER_1 = universal supports, all students; TIER_2 = targeted supplemental; TIER_3 = intensive individualized), domain ENUM(ACADEMIC,BEHAVIORAL,SOCIAL_EMOTIONAL,ATTENDANCE) NOT NULL, assigned_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, assigned_at DATE NOT NULL, review_date DATE NOT NULL, exit_date DATE nullable, exit_reason TEXT nullable, status ENUM(ACTIVE,EXITED,PROMOTED,DEMOTED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE'. UNIQUE(student_id, academic_year_id, domain) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'. INDEX(school_id, tier, status). On INSERT or UPDATE to tier: emit svc.tier.changed Kafka event.
svc_interventionsA specific intervention applied to a student, linked to their MTSS tier. id UUID PK(v7), tier_id UUID FK(svc_mtss_tiers) NOT NULL, intervention_name TEXT NOT NULL, intervention_type ENUM(ACADEMIC_SUPPORT,BEHAVIORAL_SUPPORT,SOCIAL_EMOTIONAL_LEARNING,ATTENDANCE_SUPPORT,COUNSELING,EXTERNAL_SERVICE) NOT NULL, frequency TEXT (e.g. '3x per week, 30 minutes'), start_date DATE NOT NULL, end_date DATE nullable, provider_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (the staff member delivering the intervention), status ENUM(ACTIVE,COMPLETED,DISCONTINUED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(tier_id, status).
svc_intervention_progressProgress monitoring data points for a specific intervention. id UUID PK(v7), intervention_id UUID FK(svc_interventions) NOT NULL, recorded_by UUID FK NOT NULL, recorded_date DATE NOT NULL, measure_type TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Reading Fluency WPM', 'Office Referrals per Week', 'Attendance Rate'), score NUMERIC(8,2), benchmark NUMERIC(8,2) nullable, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(intervention_id, recorded_date DESC).

### Domain 4 — Behavior Plans (2 tables)

TableDefinition
svc_mtss_team_meetingsLinks a formal MTSS team meeting to the students being reviewed. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, meeting_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to mtg_meetings.id NOT NULL — the scheduled team meeting), academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, facilitated_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, meeting_date DATE NOT NULL, notes TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, meeting_date DESC).
svc_mtss_team_meeting_studentsStudents discussed at a specific MTSS team meeting and the tier assignments reviewed. id UUID PK(v7), team_meeting_id UUID FK(svc_mtss_team_meetings) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, tier_id UUID FK(svc_mtss_tiers) nullable (the active tier at the time of the meeting), outcome ENUM(NO_CHANGE,TIER_UP,TIER_DOWN,EXIT,CONTINUE_WITH_ADJUSTMENT) nullable, outcome_notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(team_meeting_id, student_id).
svc_behavior_plansBehavior Intervention Plan (BIP) for a student. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, caseload_id UUID FK(svc_caseloads) nullable, plan_type ENUM(BIP,BSP,SAFETY_PLAN) NOT NULL, status ENUM(DRAFT,ACTIVE,REVIEW,EXPIRED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT', created_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, review_date DATE NOT NULL, review_meeting_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to mtg_meetings.id nullable — the meeting scheduled to formally review this plan; set when review is booked), target_behaviors TEXT[] NOT NULL, replacement_behaviors TEXT[], reinforcement_strategies TEXT[], plan_document_s3_key TEXT, source_incident_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to sis_discipline_incidents.id nullable). UNIQUE(student_id, plan_type) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'. INDEX(student_id, status).
svc_behavior_plan_goalsSpecific measurable goals within a behavior plan. id UUID PK(v7), plan_id UUID FK(svc_behavior_plans) NOT NULL, goal_text TEXT NOT NULL, baseline_frequency TEXT, target_frequency TEXT, measurement_method TEXT, progress ENUM(NOT_STARTED,IN_PROGRESS,MET,NOT_MET) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'NOT_STARTED', last_assessed_at DATE nullable. INDEX(plan_id).
svc_bip_teacher_feedbackStructured teacher input on BIP strategy effectiveness in the classroom. Counselor requests; teacher submits. id UUID PK(v7), plan_id UUID FK(svc_behavior_plans) NOT NULL, teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, requested_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL (the counselor requesting feedback), requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, strategies_observed TEXT[] nullable (which plan strategies the teacher has observed being used), overall_effectiveness ENUM(NOT_EFFECTIVE,SOMEWHAT_EFFECTIVE,EFFECTIVE,VERY_EFFECTIVE) nullable, classroom_observations TEXT nullable, recommended_adjustments TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(plan_id, teacher_id) WHERE submitted_at IS NULL — one pending request per teacher per plan. INDEX(plan_id, submitted_at).

### Domain 5 — Wellbeing Check-ins (4 tables)

TableDefinition
svc_wellbeing_survey_templatesSchool-configured wellbeing check-in surveys. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, frequency_recommendation ENUM(DAILY,WEEKLY,MONTHLY,AS_NEEDED) NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_by UUID FK NOT NULL. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
svc_wellbeing_questionsIndividual questions within a wellbeing survey template. id UUID PK(v7), template_id UUID FK NOT NULL, question_text TEXT NOT NULL, question_type ENUM(SCALE_1_5,SCALE_1_10,YES_NO,FREE_TEXT,EMOJI_SCALE) NOT NULL, domain ENUM(ACADEMIC,SOCIAL,EMOTIONAL,PHYSICAL,SAFETY) NOT NULL, sort_order INT. INDEX(template_id, sort_order).
svc_wellbeing_deploymentsA counselor deploying a wellbeing survey to a targeted group of students. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, template_id UUID FK(svc_wellbeing_survey_templates) NOT NULL, deployed_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, deploy_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, target_type ENUM(CASELOAD,CLASS,YEAR_GROUP,SCHOOL,CUSTOM_LIST) NOT NULL, target_ids UUID[] nullable (class IDs, year group IDs, or specific student IDs depending on target_type — null for CASELOAD which auto-targets deploying counselor's active caseload), status ENUM(SCHEDULED,ACTIVE,COMPLETED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED', total_targeted INT nullable, total_completed INT nullable. INDEX(school_id, status). On status = ACTIVE: platform creates svc_wellbeing_checkins rows for all targeted students with status PENDING.
svc_wellbeing_checkinsA student completing a wellbeing survey instance. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, template_id UUID FK(svc_wellbeing_survey_templates) NOT NULL, deployment_id UUID FK(svc_wellbeing_deployments) nullable (the deployment that triggered this check-in — null for ad-hoc check-ins), completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (null = not yet completed), flagged_for_follow_up BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (set by application logic when responses indicate concern), assigned_counselor_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable. INDEX(student_id, completed_at DESC). INDEX(flagged_for_follow_up, completed_at) WHERE flagged_for_follow_up = true. INDEX(deployment_id, completed_at) WHERE deployment_id IS NOT NULL.
svc_wellbeing_responsesIndividual question responses within a check-in. id UUID PK(v7), checkin_id UUID FK NOT NULL, question_id UUID FK NOT NULL, numeric_response SMALLINT nullable, text_response TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(checkin_id, question_id). NOTE: wellbeing responses are student-generated self-report data. Access restricted to the counseling team and school administration. Students may view their own responses only. Individual response data is never surfaced in class-level or public dashboards — only aggregated anonymized school-level trends in M110 Analytics.

### Kafka Events — M27 Student Services

EventConsumersNotes
svc.referral.createdStudent Services (M27)Task Worker (M1), Communications (M40), Analytics (M110)
svc.referral.escalatedStudent Services (M27)Communications (M40), Analytics (M110)
svc.tier.changedStudent Services (M27)Analytics (M110), Communications (M40)
svc.wellbeing.flaggedStudent Services (M27)Task Worker (M1), Communications (M40)

IAM — Authorization Evaluation Pipeline

Defines the precise order in which the authorization engine evaluates access for any incoming request. All modules must follow this order. Short-circuit at the earliest denial.

StepStageLogicWhen
1Account status gateIf platform_users.account_status is SUSPENDED or LOCKED, deny all access immediately. No bypass, no exceptions. Returns 401 ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED or 401 ACCOUNT_LOCKED.ALWAYS
2Break-glass overrideIf an active iam_break_glass_grant exists for this account in this scope with expires_at > now(), add the emergency role's permissions to the base permission set. Break-glass is ADDITIVE — it never removes other restrictions. All requests under break-glass are flagged in audit_log and iam_access_change_event with EMERGENCY_ACCESS_ACTIVE metadata.IF ACTIVE
3Impersonation context (Wave 2)If an active iam_impersonation_session exists, substitute the impersonated account's permission set for the remainder of evaluation. Restrict to the session's declared scope. Flag all audit events with both impersonated_account_id and impersonator_account_id.IF ACTIVE
4Effective access cache readRead iam_effective_access_cache WHERE account_id=$1 AND scope_id=$2. This is the primary path for all normal requests. If a cache entry exists and computed_at is within the 500ms staleness SLA, return the permission_codes array.PRIMARY PATH
5Cache miss — compute and rebuildIf no cache entry exists or entry is stale: JOIN iam_role_assignment → roles → role_permissions → permissions WHERE account_id=$1 AND scope_id=$2 AND status='ACTIVE' AND (effective_to IS NULL OR effective_to > now()). Traverse parent_scope_id chain upward and union ancestor role assignments. Write result to iam_effective_access_cache and Redis. Return computed permission_codes.ON MISS
6Permission checkCheck whether the required permission code is present in the effective permission set. If present: allow. If absent: deny with 403 FORBIDDEN.FINAL
7No deny rulesEvaluation ends at step 6. There are no explicit deny rules. Deny-by-default means the absence of a permission is the denial. This eliminates deny-override complexity and makes the permission model tractable.DESIGN PRINCIPLE

Master Data Ownership Register (ADR-039 Appendix)

Defines the canonical owning table for every major entity type. Modules must not claim ownership of entities listed here as belonging to another module. Cross-module access is via events, SOFT INTEGRITY references, or read models — never direct table writes.

EntityOwner ModuleCanonical TableNotes
Person identityIAMiam_person*Universal root for all human identity. One row per real-world person.*
User account / credentialIAMplatform_users*Credentialed digital identity. Auth, session, and audit FKs point here.*
Student recordSISsis_students + platform_students*platform_students for portability; sis_students for school context. Both required.*
Employee recordHRhr_employees*Domain projection of platform_users toward iam_person for employment context.*
Guardian relationshipsSISsis_guardians + sis_student_guardians*Triggers IAM access derivation via iam_relationship_access_rule on change.*
Volunteer profilePlatform Coreplatform_volunteer_profiles*ADR-032. Parallel to substitute profiles. Never in tenant schema.*
Substitute profilePlatform Coreplatform_substitute_profiles*ADR-014. Cross-school visibility via direct platform schema read.*
Service / machine identityIAMiam_service_principal*Non-human actors. Never share platform_users rows with humans.*
Facilities assetsM65 Facilitiesfac_assets*Physical equipment, HVAC, infrastructure. Distinct from tech assets.*
Technology assetsM62 Technologytech_assets*Computing devices, software, MDM-managed equipment. Distinct from facilities.*
Scheduling roomsM22 Academic Schedulingsch_rooms*Rooms as schedulable units. DISPLAY-ONLY link to fac_spaces where overlap exists.*
Facility spacesM65 Facilitiesfac_spaces*Operational spaces (bathrooms, corridors, grounds). DISPLAY-ONLY link to sch_rooms.*
Communication recipientsM40 Communicationsmsg_thread_participants, msg_announcement_audiences*Distribution targeting computed at send time by Communications worker.*
Publication recipientsM42 Publicationspub_distribution_recipients*Distinct from Communications. Computed at publish time by Publications worker.*
Financial accounts (future)M83 Financefin_chart_of_accounts (planned)*M81 owns transaction records. M83 owns account structure and GL interpretation.*
Supplier master / address bookM81/M83 Financefin_suppliers (Wave 2 — pulled forward per ADR-051)*Canonical address book for all vendor relationships — legal name, tax ID, payment terms, contacts, W-9. fin_supplier_contacts for multiple POCs per supplier. Migrates to M83 documentation at Wave 4 commencement.*
Operational vendor registryM60 Helpdesktkt_vendors*Lean operational record for repair/maintenance dispatch. Bridges to fin_suppliers via tkt_vendor_id SOFT INTEGRITY when same company is also a financial supplier.*
Canonical physical space (future)Platform Coreplatform_physical_spaces (not yet created)*Evolution trigger: introduce when any two modules need FK-level space coordination.*

M110 Analytics — Event Replay Procedure

The rpt_* read models in M110 are built entirely from Kafka events. This section defines which events feed which tables, and the procedure for rebuilding a corrupt or stale read model.

Read ModelSource TopicsProducerRebuild Procedure
rpt_daily_attendance_summaryattendance.confirmedSIS (M20)*Rewind consumer group offset to beginning of relevant date range partition. Replay events. Worker is idempotent: uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE on UNIQUE(school_id, class_id, summary_date).*
rpt_student_academic_summarygrade.posted, attendance.confirmedClassroom (M21), SIS (M20)*Rewind both consumer groups. at_risk_flags recomputed on each event. Full replay rebuilds correct state.*
rpt_class_performance_summarygrade.postedClassroom (M21)*Rewind grade.posted consumer. Idempotent upsert on UNIQUE(class_id, term_id).*
rpt_staff_summaryleave.approved, grade.postedHR (M80), Classroom (M21)*Rewind both topics for affected academic_year_id range.*
rpt_school_summary, rpt_district_summarystudent.enrolled, attendance.confirmedSIS (M20)*Rewind from beginning of academic year. Rebuild school then district summaries in order.*
rpt_daily_attendance_summary — full tenant rebuildattendance.confirmedSIS (M20)*1. SET consumer group offset to T1 retention boundary (3 years back). 2. Truncate affected rpt_* partition. 3. Resume consumer — worker replays and rebuilds. 4. Alert if rebuild takes > 30 min.*
Analytics Layer Clarification M110 Analytics is the OPERATIONAL READ LAYER only. The rpt_* tables are optimized for school-day queries (attendance dashboards, at-risk alerts, gradebook snapshots) — not multi-year aggregations. Data older than rpt_* retention limits (2–3 years) lives in T2 or T3 archive tiers, queried via Archive API or Athena respectively. The platform uses a federated query model — T1 operational reads via rpt_*, T2 compliance queries via Archive API, T3 longitudinal/research via Athena-over-Parquet. There is no separate OLAP warehouse: Athena serves that role for T3 without requiring additional infrastructure. Migration to Redshift or BigQuery is an infrastructure swap inside the archive layer if Athena costs or latency become limiting — not an ERD change.

M1 — Student ****&**** Parent Task Model (v6.7 Addition)

Documents how tsk_tasks, tsk_acknowledgements, and cal_personal_calendar_items work together to deliver the student/parent To-Do screen, planner integration, and acknowledgement screen.

To-Do Screen Query

How the To-Do screen is built SELECT tsk_tasks WHERE owner_id = $account_id AND status IN ('TODO','IN_PROGRESS') ORDER BY due_at ASC NULLS LAST, priority DESC. Grouped by task_category on the UI: ACADEMIC (assignment submissions, grade reviews), ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (sign-offs required), ADMINISTRATIVE (forms, library returns), PERSONAL (student/parent-created). ACKNOWLEDGEMENT tasks JOIN to tsk_acknowledgements to retrieve the prompt text, document link, dispute option, and expiry. The task is marked DONE when the acknowledgement is actioned — they are always in sync. Tasks with no due_at appear at the bottom of their group. Tasks past their due_at are surfaced at the top with an OVERDUE indicator.

Planner / Schedule Integration

How tasks appear on the student or parent planner cal_personal_calendar_items.source_type = TASK is created by the Task Calendar Worker when a tsk_tasks row with due_at is written for a student or parent account. The calendar item title mirrors tsk_tasks.title. The start_at and end_at are both set to due_at (point-in-time marker, not a duration block). When tsk_tasks.status moves to DONE or CANCELLED, the Task Calendar Worker removes the corresponding cal_personal_calendar_items row via the delta-update pattern (ADR-013). This means the student's planner shows timetable slots, lesson resources, school events, assignment due dates, AND outstanding tasks — all in one unified timeline without any separate query.

Auto-Task Rules for Students and Parents

Trigger EventTarget AccountCategoryAcknowledgement?Example task title
ASSIGNMENT_POSTEDStudentACADEMICNo*Complete: Reading Response — due Fri 3pm*
ASSIGNMENT_OVERDUEStudentACADEMICNo*Overdue: Submit Reading Response*
GRADE_RETURNEDStudentACADEMICNo*Review feedback: Reading Response*
ANNOUNCEMENT_REQUIRES_ACKNOWLEDGEMENTStudent or ParentACKNOWLEDGEMENTYes — no dispute option*Acknowledge: Classroom conduct policy*
DISCIPLINE_PENDING_STUDENT_SIGN_OFFStudent + ParentACKNOWLEDGEMENTYes — dispute option enabled*Review and sign: Behaviour incident 12 March*
CONSENT_REQUESTEDParent (guardian account)ACKNOWLEDGEMENTYes — parent signs on behalf of student*Give consent: Science Museum trip 20 March*
CHECKOUT_OVERDUEStudentADMINISTRATIVENo*Return overdue book: Charlotte's Web*
INVOICE_DUEParentADMINISTRATIVENo*Payment due: Spring term fees — 31 March*

Acknowledgement Screen Flow

How the acknowledgement screen works Query: SELECT tsk_acknowledgements WHERE subject_id = $account_id AND status = 'PENDING' ORDER BY expires_at ASC NULLS LAST. Each item shows: the acknowledgement title (what they are certifying), optional document link (body_s3_key — must read before acknowledging), expiry date if set, and action buttons. If requires_dispute_option = false: one button — 'Acknowledge'. Sets status = ACKNOWLEDGED, acknowledged_at = now(). The linked tsk_tasks row is marked DONE by the same transaction. If requires_dispute_option = true: two buttons — 'Acknowledge' and 'Acknowledge with dispute'. Dispute requires dispute_reason text before submitting. Sets status = ACKNOWLEDGED_WITH_DISPUTE. On either outcome: student.acknowledgement.completed Kafka event emitted with source_type and source_ref_id. The originating module consumes this — e.g. SIS marks the discipline incident as student-acknowledged, Communications marks the announcement as read. Expired acknowledgements (expires_at < now() and still PENDING): status set to EXPIRED by a nightly sweep. A new task is auto-created for the teacher flagging the non-acknowledgement. The student's task is marked CANCELLED.

Kafka Events — Task ****&**** Acknowledgement Model

EventConsumersNotes
task.createdTask Worker (M1)Calendar delta-update worker (M3)
task.completedTask Worker (M1)Calendar delta-update worker (M3)
student.acknowledgement.completedTask Worker (M1)SIS (M20), Communications (M40), M64 Extracurricular, M27 Student Services
announcement.acknowledgement.requiredCommunications (M40)Task Worker (M1)
discipline.student.sign_off.requiredSIS (M20)Task Worker (M1)
consent.requestedM64 ExtracurricularTask Worker (M1)

M23 — Health: Parent Update Requests (v6.8 Addition)

Parents can submit requests to update their child's health information. All updates require nurse review before being applied — health records are safety-critical and cannot be self-edited directly.

TableDefinition
hlth_parent_update_requestsParent-submitted request to update a student health record. Reviewed and applied by the school nurse. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, submitted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL (guardian account), update_category ENUM(ALLERGY,MEDICAL_CONDITION,MEDICATION,EMERGENCY_CONTACT_MEDICAL,GP_DETAILS,OTHER) NOT NULL, current_value JSONB nullable (snapshot of the current value for the relevant field at the time of request — stored for nurse comparison), proposed_value JSONB NOT NULL (the parent-proposed new value — structured per update_category: for ALLERGY: {allergen, severity, reaction, action_plan}; for MEDICATION: {name, dose, frequency, prescriber, storage_notes}; for MEDICAL_CONDITION: {condition, diagnosis_date, management_plan}), additional_notes TEXT nullable, supporting_document_s3_key TEXT nullable (e.g. updated doctor letter, new prescription), status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,SUPERSEDED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING' (SUPERSEDED: a newer request for the same category was submitted before this one was reviewed — old request auto-closed), reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (nurse or health admin who reviewed), reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, reviewer_notes TEXT nullable, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (populated when nurse confirms the change was applied to the relevant hlth_* table). INDEX(student_id, status). INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'PENDING' — nurse review queue. On INSERT: Task Worker creates INFO_UPDATE_REQUESTED task for school nurse. On APPROVED: nurse manually updates the relevant hlth_* table (allergy row, medication row, etc.) and marks applied_at. The request record is the immutable audit trail for what the parent asked for and what the nurse actioned. FERPA/HIPAA: this table is health-record-adjacent — same encryption key as hlth_student_health_records, same access restriction.

ADR-053 — Advanced Scheduling Expansion (M22)

M24 Advanced Scheduling — Scope Expansion (ADR-053) DECISION: Module 22 Academic Scheduling is expanded to cover the six items explicitly excluded from ADR-027 v1 scope. These items are now formalised as a Wave 2 expansion completing the scheduling subsystem. Status: ACCEPTED. ROTATION CALENDARS: sch_rotation_cycles defines a cycle (A/B week = length 2, 6-day rotation = length 6). sch_rotation_calendar maps every school date to a rotation day number, maintained by admin and adjusted for closures. sch_periods gains a rotation_day column — a period belongs to either a weekday OR a rotation day, enforced by CHECK constraint. sch_timetable_slots gains coteaching_pair_id to identify co-teaching relationships. AUTOMATED SCHEDULE GENERATION: sch_scheduling_constraints captures the constraint profile. sch_student_subject_choices captures demand (student subject elections). sch_scheduling_requests triggers the solver job. sch_scheduling_candidates holds the generated solution proposals awaiting admin review. sch_scheduling_candidate_slots is the atomic slot unit within each candidate. On approval, candidate slots are promoted to sch_timetable_slots. The solver is an external constraint programming service (e.g. Google OR-Tools) — the ERD models inputs, outputs, and state; the algorithm is application-layer. EXAM SCHEDULING: sch_exam_sessions schedules the sitting. sch_exam_session_rooms is a junction table assigning rooms to sessions — replacing the former room_ids UUID[] array — with per-room capacity and is_main_room flag. sch_exam_seatings assigns students to rooms and seats with accommodation fields (extra time, separate room, reader, scribe) drawn from sis_student_active_accommodations. sch_exam_invigilator_assignments assigns staff. Room conflict detection uses the sch_exam_session_rooms junction table to check tstzrange overlaps against sch_timetable_slots. CO-TEACHING: sch_coteaching_arrangements links two teachers to a slot with a teaching model enum. The secondary teacher's double-booking exclusion constraint is relaxed for slots where coteaching_pair_id is set. Five co-teaching models supported: TEAM_TEACHING, ONE_TEACH_ONE_SUPPORT, STATION_ROTATION, PARALLEL_TEACHING, ALTERNATIVE_TEACHING. PULL-OUT INTERVENTIONS: sch_pull_out_interventions records a student's scheduled absence from a regular slot to attend a specialist session. The regular slot's attendance record is pre-marked as PULL_OUT rather than absent. Clash detection is waived for named students with an active pull-out intervention during that period. CROSS-SCHOOL STAFF: sch_cross_school_staff_assignments links a person_id FK(iam_person) to a visiting school's timetable. home_employee_id FK(hr_employees) provides the role-context reference needed for contract terms and pay rate at the home school (ADR-055 category 3 — both FKs are correct and serve distinct purposes). sch_timetable_slots.cross_school_staff_id references this. The teacher double-booking EXCLUSION CONSTRAINT uses person_id to prevent the same human being double-booked across schools. COMPLEX COVER: sch_cover_arrangements extends cover beyond substitute replacement. sch_cover_arrangement_classes maps each affected class to its arrangement — where it will go, who will supervise, or whether it merges with another class. SPLIT_CLASS arrangements use sch_cover_split_students, a junction table (arrangement_class_id, student_id) replacing the former split_student_ids UUID[] array — enabling integrity enforcement, indexed lookup, and per-student audit. TOUR BOOKING: enr_tour_slots, enr_tour_bookings, and enr_tour_booking_guests are added to M81 Enrollment. Group tour capacity controlled by max_bookings on the slot. Bookings are linked to enr_applications when a family subsequently applies, closing the inquiry-to-application funnel loop.

ADR-054 — Wave 5: Procurement ****&**** Purchasing Module (Roadmap)

Wave 5 — Procurement & Purchasing (ADR-054) DECISION: A Wave 5 Procurement & Purchasing module is added to the product roadmap. Wave 5 commences after Wave 4 Finance & Accounting (M83) is fully implemented and stable. No tables are designed at this time. Status: ACCEPTED (roadmap only). WAVE DEPENDENCY: Wave 5 has hard dependencies on Wave 4: fin_chart_of_accounts (for GL coding of purchases), fin_ap_vouchers (for three-way matching), fin_budgets (for commitment tracking), and fin_suppliers (already pulled forward to Wave 2 per ADR-051). PLANNED SCOPE: purchase requisitions (staff-initiated requests to buy), multi-level approval workflow (integrates with M17 wsk_approval_requests), purchase orders (formal PO issued to supplier), goods receipt (confirmation of delivery), three-way matching (PO vs receipt vs supplier invoice), budget commitment tracking (encumber budget at PO creation, release on invoice), blanket purchase orders (standing orders for recurring purchases), catalogue-based purchasing (pre-approved items at pre-negotiated prices), and procurement reporting (spend by supplier, by category, by department). MODULE PREFIX: prc_ (reserved — not yet used). Module number: M84 (follows M83 Finance & Accounting in Wave 4). INTEGRATION POINTS: prc_purchase_orders → fin_ap_vouchers (three-way match trigger). prc_requisitions → wsk_approval_requests (approval workflow). prc_purchase_orders.supplier_id → fin_suppliers (supplier master). prc_budget_commitments → fin_budgets (encumbrance). prc_catalogue_items may overlap with fds_inventory_items (food procurement) and tech_asset_categories (device procurement) — a shared procurement catalogue is a Wave 5 design decision. RATIONALE FOR DEFERRAL: Procurement without a functioning GL and budget system would produce orphaned financial data with no reconciliation path. Wave 4 must be complete before Wave 5 is designed. The procurement workflow is also deeply dependent on how the GL chart of accounts is structured, which is a Wave 4 design decision.

ADR-055 — Universal Human Identity FK Contract

ADR-055 — iam_person as Universal Human Identity FK Target DECISION: iam_person.id is the canonical FK target for all domain table references to a human being. platform_users.id is reserved exclusively for authentication, session management, and audit trail (who acted). This contract is locked from v9.2 onwards. Status: ACCEPTED. THREE FK CATEGORIES — these are mutually exclusive and must not be mixed: (1) Human identity: 'who is this person' → FK(iam_person). Used when the row represents a person's participation, membership, ownership, or identity. (2) Auth/session/audit: 'who is acting right now' → FK(platform_users). Used for created_by, reviewed_by, authorised_by, actor_id, account_id — i.e. who performed an action in an authenticated session. (3) Role context: 'what role are they playing' → domain projection ID (hr_employees.id, sis_students.id, etc.). Used when the business context requires the person's role-scoped record, not their universal identity. RATIONALE: The previous mixed model (some domain tables used platform_users for human identity, others used iam_person) created ambiguity for engineers, inconsistent joins, and a hidden assumption that every person has a login account. Under iam_person: a guardian with no portal access, a volunteer without a platform account, or a student too young to log in all have an iam_person record and can be correctly referenced. Under platform_users: these cases require a nullable FK or a workaround. RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN iam_person AND platform_users: Every platform_users account has a person_id FK(iam_person) (per ADR-036). A person can exist without an account (person but no login). An account always has a person (accounts without identity are service principals, which use iam_service_principal not platform_users). The join iam_person → platform_users is always LEFT JOIN: some people have no account. RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN iam_person AND platform_students: platform_students retains its own table for student portability across school transfers (ADR-014 equivalent for students). It gains person_id FK(iam_person) NOT NULL UNIQUE in v9.2, making platform_students a projection of iam_person for the student domain. PII fields are denormalised in platform_students for zero-latency lookup without joins. The authoritative name/DOB lives in iam_person. DOMAIN PROJECTIONS: hr_employees, sis_students, sis_guardians, sis_staff — these are role-scoped projections of iam_person within a school context. They use person_id FK(iam_person) for identity and account_id FK(platform_users) for auth access. The pattern is: one iam_person → zero or one platform_users account → zero or more domain projections in different schools/roles. ENFORCEMENT: New tables must use FK(iam_person) for human identity references. A code review check should flag any new FK(platform_users) in a domain table that is not clearly an auth/audit reference. The distinction: if you would call the column 'person_id', 'patron_id', 'data_subject_id', 'member_id', 'recipient_id' — use iam_person. If you would call it 'created_by', 'reviewed_by', 'actor_id', 'account_id' — use platform_users. TABLES UPDATED IN v9.2: platform_students (gains person_id), platform_substitute_profiles (platform_user_id → person_id + account_id), platform_volunteer_profiles (same), sis_guardians (platform_user_id → person_id + account_id), sis_family_members (person_id FK target → iam_person), sis_family_member_relationships (same), sis_staff (same), hr_employees (same), grp_members (same), grp_announcements (author_id), grp_announcement_reads (reader_id), grp_events (created_by), trn_adhoc_trip_passengers (person_id), ext_equipment_assignments (assigned_to_id), fac_space_bookings (booked_by), lib_checkouts (patron_id), lib_class_set_checkouts (teacher_patron_id), fds_meal_transactions (patron_id), inc_accountability_records (person_id), eng_ptc_bookings (parent_id), alm_alumni_profiles (platform_student_id → person_id), dpo_ data_subject_id and requestor_id, compliance_ferpa_requests requestor_id, pay_family_accounts account_holder_id, pay_family_account_access authorised_person_id, pay_payment_authorisation_requests requested_by, sch_cross_school_staff_assignments (platform_user_id → person_id), enr_tour_bookings (booked_by).

ADR-056 — Scheduling Engine Contract (M22)

Scheduling Engine Contract (ADR-056) DECISION: The scheduling engine is an external constraint solver invoked by a background worker. It operates as a stateless service boundary — it reads inputs from CampusOS tables, produces candidate solutions written back to CampusOS tables, and has no runtime connection to the application layer. CampusOS owns all state; the solver owns only computation. Status: ACCEPTED. ARCHITECTURE: The SchedulingWorker reads sch_scheduling_requests (status=QUEUED), assembles the full input payload (students, courses, teachers, rooms, periods, constraints), submits to the solver service (Google OR-Tools or equivalent constraint programming engine), receives the candidate solution set, writes sch_scheduling_candidates and sch_scheduling_candidate_slots, then sets the request status=COMPLETED and notifies the admin. The solver service is stateless — it receives a complete problem description in one call and returns a complete solution set. No partial state exists in the solver. INPUT CONTRACT: The solver receives a JSON payload derived from: (1) sch_student_subject_choices — demand matrix (which students need which courses); (2) sis_classes — class definitions with max_enrollment; (3) hr_employees + sch_cross_school_staff_assignments — teacher availability; (4) sch_rooms — room inventory with capacity; (5) sch_periods — the period grid (day/rotation structure); (6) sch_scheduling_constraints — the active constraint profile; (7) sch_rotation_calendar — the date-to-rotation-day mapping for the academic year. The SchedulingWorker assembles this payload entirely from existing tables before invoking the solver. No solver-specific tables exist — input state is the application's state. CONSTRAINT MODEL — HARD vs SOFT: Hard constraints cannot be violated: (H1) a teacher cannot be in two places simultaneously (EXCLUSION CONSTRAINT on sch_timetable_slots); (H2) a room cannot be double-booked (same constraint); (H3) a student cannot be assigned to two concurrent classes (evaluated at candidate_slot generation); (H4) class size cannot exceed room capacity; (H5) cross-school staff cannot exceed their max_periods_per_week. Soft constraints are scored and minimised: (S1) avoid consecutive periods for same teacher; (S2) honour teacher period preferences from HR preferences; (S3) minimise student free periods; (S4) avoid same-subject on consecutive days; (S5) distribute co-teaching arrangements evenly. The solver reports constraint_violations JSONB on each candidate when any soft constraint was relaxed and sets all_constraints_satisfied=false if any hard constraint required relaxation (meaning no fully satisfying solution was found). PROMOTION LIFECYCLE: Four stages, each gated by an explicit state change: (1) DRAFT — solver output written to sch_scheduling_candidates with review_status=PENDING and all candidate slots in sch_scheduling_candidate_slots; (2) REVIEWED — admin reviews candidates via the scheduling dashboard; has_clash=true slots are highlighted for resolution; (3) APPROVED — admin selects one candidate and sets review_status=APPROVED; the SchedulingActivationWorker begins promotion; (4) ACTIVE — for each sch_scheduling_candidate_slots row, the worker creates or updates a sch_timetable_slots row with effective_from set to the academic year start date. On activation, sch.timetable.updated Kafka events fire for all affected slots, triggering the Calendar delta-update worker. A candidate can only be activated if all its has_clash=true slots have been resolved (either removed or manually overridden with a documented justification). REBUILD TRIGGERS: A schedule becomes stale and must be regenerated when: (R1) a new enrolment adds a student with subject choices not covered by the active schedule; (R2) a teacher is added, removed, or changes availability; (R3) a room is added, closed (via fac_space_closures), or changes capacity; (R4) sch_scheduling_constraints is updated for the current academic year; (R5) a class is added or removed. Rebuild does not automatically invalidate the active schedule — it creates a new sch_scheduling_requests entry for admin-initiated regeneration. Emergency mid-year changes are handled via sch_cover_arrangements (not full regeneration). CONFLICT RESOLUTION RULES: When two candidate slots share a teacher or room at the same period, has_clash=true is set on both. The admin dashboard presents the conflict with three options: (C1) remove one slot and leave the student group unscheduled (with a tsk_task created for follow-up); (C2) reassign to a different room or teacher if alternatives exist; (C3) override and accept the conflict with a documented justification stored in sch_scheduling_candidate_slots.clash_description. Option C3 is only permitted for room conflicts, never teacher conflicts (a teacher physically cannot be in two places). SOLVER ALGORITHM (ADR-060): Hybrid approach. CP-SAT (OR-Tools deterministic constraint programming) for schools with ≤300 sections — fully reproducible, same inputs always produce same candidate set, auditability is straightforward. Heuristic solver (genetic algorithm or simulated annealing) for schools with >300 sections — faster for large problems where CP-SAT may not find a solution within the 600s SLA. sch_scheduling_requests.section_count_at_submission INT NOT NULL records the count at job start (used for algorithm selection and performance baselining). sch_scheduling_requests.solver_algorithm ENUM(CP_SAT, HEURISTIC) NOT NULL records which algorithm was used — critical for debugging and audit. For heuristic runs, the same candidate may not be reproducible from the same inputs alone; the solver seed value is stored in sch_scheduling_candidates.solver_seed nullable. Performance SLA: both algorithms must produce at least one candidate within 600 seconds for their respective section count ranges. TENANCY: Each sch_scheduling_requests is school-scoped. The solver treats each school's scheduling problem as fully independent. Cross-school staff availability (sch_cross_school_staff_assignments) is included in both schools' input payloads with the max_periods_per_week constraint propagated. The solver does not have visibility across school boundaries — cross-school constraint coordination is the SchedulingWorker's responsibility before submitting each school's payload.

ADR-057 — Platform Event Model

Platform Event Model (ADR-057) DECISION: All Kafka events produced by CampusOS modules must conform to a standard event envelope. Event schemas are versioned. Consumers must be idempotent. Topics follow a deterministic naming convention. This contract is enforced by a platform event registry and validated at CI build time. Status: ACCEPTED. EVENT ENVELOPE (required fields on every event): { event_id: UUID (v7 — monotonically increasing, globally unique, used as idempotency key by all consumers), event_type: STRING (e.g. 'sis.student.enrolled', 'pay.invoice.created' — dot-separated: domain.entity.verb), event_version: STRING (semver — e.g. '1.0.0'; minor version increments are backward-compatible field additions; major version increments require a new topic), occurred_at: TIMESTAMPTZ (when the domain event occurred — not when it was published; used for event replay ordering), published_at: TIMESTAMPTZ (when the message was written to Kafka — used for lag monitoring), tenant_id: UUID (the school_id — all consumers filter on this; cross-tenant fan-out is forbidden), source_module: STRING (the module prefix — e.g. 'sis', 'pay', 'hlth' — for observability and DLQ routing), correlation_id: UUID nullable (the originating request ID — enables distributed tracing across async boundaries), payload: JSONB (event-specific data — typed per event_type and event_version) }. TOPIC NAMING CONVENTION: {environment}.{domain}.{entity}.{verb} — e.g. 'prod.sis.student.enrolled', 'prod.pay.invoice.created', 'prod.hlth.allergy_alert.changed'. Environment prefix (prod/staging/dev) prevents accidental cross-environment consumption. Each topic belongs to exactly one source module — no module publishes to another module's topic. Topic creation requires a platform event registry entry (see below). VERSIONING RULES: (V1) Field additions to payload are backward-compatible — increment minor version; consumers must ignore unknown fields. (V2) Field removals, renames, or type changes are breaking — increment major version; create a new topic suffix e.g. 'prod.sis.student.enrolled.v2'; run both topics in parallel during a transition window defined by the platform release process. (V3) A consumer must declare which event_version(s) it supports; the platform event registry validates this at service startup. (V4) Events older than the consumer's declared minimum version are routed to the DLQ, not silently dropped. IDEMPOTENCY CONTRACT: Every consumer must be idempotent with respect to event_id. The standard pattern uses platform_event_consumer_idempotency (M0 platform schema, ADR-059): (1) INSERT (consumer_name, event_id) — UNIQUE constraint is the gate; (2) if INSERT succeeds: process the event; (3) if INSERT fails (duplicate): skip and acknowledge. Replay safety: resetting consumer group offset is safe because the idempotency table prevents reprocessing. Clear the relevant (consumer_name, event_id) rows before a supervised replay window. REPLAY SEMANTICS: Events are replayed by resetting the consumer group offset to the desired position in the topic. Since occurred_at is the domain timestamp and published_at is the Kafka timestamp, consumers that build read models (rpt_ tables, iam_effective_access_cache) must use occurred_at for ordering, not published_at. During replay, the idempotency table must be cleared for the replayed event range first — this is a supervised operational procedure, not an automated one. DEAD LETTER QUEUE: Every consumer has a corresponding DLQ topic: {original_topic}.dlq. On three consecutive processing failures for the same event_id, the message is moved to the DLQ with an envelope augmented by: failure_count, last_error, last_failed_at, consumer_name. The DLQ is monitored; SRE is alerted on any DLQ message older than 15 minutes. DLQ messages are replayed manually after root cause is resolved. EVENT OWNERSHIP: Each event type is owned by exactly one module. The owning module is the sole producer. Other modules are consumers only. The platform event registry (a configuration file in the platform repository, not a database table) lists every event type with its owner, topic, current version, consumer list, and payload schema reference. Changes to event schemas require a PR to the event registry with review from the owning module team and any affected consumer teams.

ADR-058 — M83 Finance ****&**** Accounting Minimal Backbone

M83 Finance — Minimal GL Backbone (ADR-058) DECISION: A minimal General Ledger backbone is defined as part of Wave 4. This covers the structural tables required to validate revenue recognition, reporting, fund accounting, and the M81→M83 event integration. Full AP, procurement, and grant management remain Wave 4 scope. The minimal backbone is sufficient for audit trail validation and must be designed before Wave 4 build commences. Status: ACCEPTED. M81 TO M83 INTEGRATION: M81 (Enrollment & Payments) emits Kafka events for all financial transactions: pay.invoice.created, pay.payment.received, pay.refund.issued, pay.credit_note.issued, pay.debt.written_off. M83 consumes these events and posts double-entry GL entries. M81 never writes to fin_* tables directly. The integration is event-driven and asynchronous — M83 may lag M81 by seconds but is eventually consistent. fin_journal_entries.status=PENDING covers the lag window; status=POSTED means the entry is in the permanent ledger. ACCOUNTS PAYABLE INTEGRATION (Wave 4): fin_ap_vouchers will link to fin_suppliers (already in Wave 2 per ADR-051). On fin_ap_vouchers.status=APPROVED, a fin_journal_batch is created debiting the expense account and crediting accounts payable. On payment (fin_ap_payments), a second batch debits accounts payable and credits cash. This three-way match pattern (PO → receipt → invoice) is Wave 5 procurement scope — Wave 4 covers the voucher-to-GL posting only.

Platform Invariants Specification

Cross-Module Invariants (referenced by ADR-028 SOFT INTEGRITY contract) These invariants define the minimum valid state of the system. Each is classified by enforcement mechanism: DB = database constraint (FK, CHECK, EXCLUSION); SVC = service layer validation before write; WORKER = background worker reconciliation (detects and alerts on violations, does not prevent them). Invariants enforced only at SVC or WORKER level require compensating logic in all write paths. ENROLLMENT & STUDENT: (I-01) A sis_students record requires a valid platform_students and iam_person record — DB FK. (I-02) A student cannot be ENROLLED at two schools simultaneously under the same organisation — SVC check on enrolment confirmation. (I-03) An enr_offers record cannot be ACCEPTED if enr_intake_capacities.places_remaining = 0 — SVC. (I-04) A withdrawal (enr_withdrawal_requests) must complete all MANDATORY enr_withdrawal_exit_tasks before sis_students.enrollment_status can be set to WITHDRAWN — SVC. ACADEMIC: (I-05) A sis_class requires a valid sis_course and sis_academic_year — DB FK. (I-06) A cls_grade requires a cls_submission which requires a cls_assignment which requires a sis_class — DB FK chain. (I-07) A sis_attendance_record requires the student to be ENROLLED at the school on the attendance date — SVC (checked at pre-population time). (I-08) A sch_timetable_slots teacher exclusion constraint prevents double-booking — DB EXCLUSION CONSTRAINT. (I-09) A student cannot be added to sch_exam_seatings without a valid sis_student_active_accommodations read model being current — WORKER (reconciliation alert). PAYMENTS & FINANCE: (I-10) A pay_payment requires a non-CANCELLED pay_invoice — SVC. (I-11) A pay_invoice total must equal the sum of its pay_invoice_line_items — DB CHECK (computed column or trigger). (I-12) A pay_ledger_entries row is IMMUTABLE — no UPDATE or DELETE — DB (append-only table, no UPDATE permission granted to application role). (I-13) A fin_journal_batch must balance to zero (sum of all debit amounts equals sum of all credit amounts) — DB CHECK on batch close. (I-14) A fin_gl_entries row is IMMUTABLE once fin_journal_batch.status=POSTED — DB trigger sets posted_at and blocks further writes. IDENTITY & ACCESS: (I-15) Every iam_role_assignment requires a valid iam_scope — DB FK. (I-16) An iam_role_assignment cannot be created for an account with account_status=SUSPENDED or DEACTIVATED — SVC. (I-17) A guardian's iam_relationship_access_rule is reassessed within 60 seconds of any change to sis_student_guardians — WORKER SLA (iam_effective_access_cache staleness SLA from ADR-036). (I-18) An iam_break_glass_grant requires a non-empty justification of at least 20 characters — DB CHECK. HEALTH & SAFEGUARDING: (I-19) An hlth_iep_plan cannot transition to ACTIVE without a valid platform_signature_requests.status=SIGNED for the guardian consent — SVC. (I-20) A trn_uncollected_child_events record triggers an immediate notification to the safeguarding officer — WORKER (on INSERT, zero-latency Kafka event). (I-21) An fds_meal_transactions row with allergen_override_required=true requires a non-null supervisor_override_id — DB CHECK. DATA INTEGRITY PATTERNS: The following patterns are system-wide invariants, not module-specific: (P-01) All append-only/immutable tables (audit_log, pay_ledger_entries, trn_dispatch_events, inc_incident_timeline, iam_role_assignment_history, trn_route_change_log) have no UPDATE or DELETE grants on the application database role — enforced by PostgreSQL GRANT at provisioning. (P-02) All RANGE-partitioned tables have pg_partman retention policies configured before the first row is inserted — enforced by provisioning scripts. (P-03) All cross-module SOFT INTEGRITY references are registered in platform_reference_health per ADR-028 — enforced by a CI check that validates every SOFT INTEGRITY comment has a corresponding registry entry.

ADR-059 — Pre-Build Architectural Fixes

ADR-059 — Eight Architectural Fixes Applied Before Wave 1 Build DECISION: Eight architectural issues identified during pre-build review are resolved in v9.2 before Wave 1 build commences. None of these are design philosophy changes — they are correctness fixes to schema definitions, missing mechanisms, and redundant structures that would have caused build failures or implementation ambiguity. Status: ACCEPTED. FIX 1 — fin_gl_entries partition key (Issue: RANGE partition by period_id UUID would fail in PostgreSQL): Added period_start DATE NOT NULL to fin_gl_entries, denormalised from fin_accounting_periods.period_start and set at INSERT time. RANGE partition by period_start ANNUAL. This aligns partitions with fiscal years — the natural audit and reporting boundary. FIX 2 — fin_journal_batches.is_balanced removed (Issue: GENERATED ALWAYS AS column checked application-maintained header totals, not actual entry rows — false confidence): Removed total_debits, total_credits, and is_balanced from fin_journal_batches. Balance is now validated by validate_batch_balance(batch_id) DB procedure that aggregates fin_gl_entries at post time. PostingService calls this inside the posting transaction — failure rolls back the status update. FIX 3 — platform_event_consumer_idempotency added to M0 (Issue: ADR-057 required a per-consumer idempotency table but none was defined in the schema): Added platform_event_consumer_idempotency to M0 platform schema.
