Schema: (consumer_name TEXT, event_id UUID, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). UNIQUE(consumer_name, event_id). RANGE partition monthly, 90-day retention. Shared across all consumers — event_id is globally unique (UUIDv7) so no tenant_id needed. FIX 4 — iam_scope_membership removed (Issue: redundant prerequisite layer — iam_role_assignment already implies scope membership): iam_scope_membership table removed. Scope membership is now derived from active iam_role_assignment rows. An account is a member of a scope if and only if it has at least one ACTIVE assignment in that scope. iam_effective_access_cache is rebuilt on role assignment changes and is the sole hot-path source. FIX 5 — GDPR erasure strategy for immutable tables (Issue: no pseudonymisation path for PII in immutable rows): Hybrid approach. audit_log.metadata (highest-risk free-form PII) receives field-level pseudonymisation via PseudonymisationWorker on approved dpo_erasure_requests. Operations logged in dpo_pseudonymisation_log (new table, 13th dpo_ table). fin_gl_entries.description and pay_ledger_entries are retained on legal obligation grounds (FERPA 7-year minimum, financial record retention); denial basis documented in dpo_erasure_requests.denied_categories. FIX 6 — person.identity.updated Kafka event for projection sync (Issue: no mechanism to keep platform_students and platform_substitute_profiles in sync with iam_person name/DOB updates): Added person.identity.updated event emitted on iam_person UPDATE to name or DOB fields. IdentityProjectionSyncWorker consumes it and updates denormalised snapshots in platform_students, platform_substitute_profiles, and platform_volunteer_profiles. Idempotent and safe to replay. FIX 7 — fac.space.created / fac.space.deactivated Kafka events (Issue: no protocol for keeping sch_rooms and fac_spaces in sync — a room added in Facilities could be invisible to scheduling): Added fac.space.created event consumed by SchedulingWorker which creates a draft sch_rooms entry (is_active=false, requires_scheduling_setup=true) for Scheduling Admin review. Added fac.space.deactivated event that sets sch_rooms.is_active=false. sch_rooms gains requires_scheduling_setup BOOLEAN for the draft state. DistrictAnalyticsWorker: nightly worker that aggregates school-level rpt_ snapshots into rpt_district_summary and rpt_district_school_comparison for all organisations. Runs after school-level nightly analytics jobs complete. Replica-routed reads, primary writes to the two district tables. Triggered by a scheduled job at 03:00 UTC after all school nightly jobs complete. FIX 8 — platform_families and platform_family_members added to M0 (Issue: cross-school sibling detection impossible with tenant-scoped sis_families — tenant schemas cannot JOIN): Added platform_families (HASH-partitioned, 16 buckets) and platform_family_members (person_id FK(iam_person), member_role, is_primary_contact) to M0 platform schema. sis_families gains platform_family_id FK(platform_families) nullable. Cross-school sibling query is now a platform-schema JOIN. FamilyMemberSyncWorker keeps platform_family_members in sync with sis_family_members INSERT/UPDATE events. TABLE COUNT (at v9.2): 658 tables, 32 modules, 60 ADRs. Updated to 803/36/70 in v10 (59 after ADR-059; +1 ADR-060 district analytics). M0 Platform Core: 74 tables (iam_ count is 20 — iam_scope_membership removed; +3 new: platform_event_consumer_idempotency, platform_families, platform_family_members). DPO Compliance Suite: 13 tables (+dpo_pseudonymisation_log).

ADRs 041-050 — Scalability Series

10 new ADRs addressing infrastructure gaps identified during million-user scale review. All prior ADRs carry forward unchanged.

ADRDecisionStatus
041Platform schema partitioning: iam_person partitioned HASH(id) 32 buckets. platform_students partitioned HASH(id) 32 buckets. platform_users increases from 16 to 32 HASH(email_domain) buckets. Online migration via pg_partman 5-step procedure: enable extension, create 32 new partitions with _new suffix, online data migration in 10k-row batches, atomic partition swap via DETACH/ATTACH in single transaction, drop old partitions after 24h verification.ACCEPTED
042Tenant routing cache: platform_tenant_routing cached in Redis with 500ms TTL (key: tenant:{subdomain}). TenantRoutingDataSource reads Redis first, falls back to DB on miss. Cache invalidation via tenant.routing.changed Kafka event consumed by TenantRoutingCacheWorker. Prevents per-request DB hits for tenant resolution at scale.ACCEPTED
043IAM effective access pre-warming: on login event, IAMCachePrewarmWorker rebuilds iam_effective_access_cache for all scopes the account has assignments in before the first API request is served. Pre-warm queue has IMMEDIATE/NORMAL/BACKGROUND priority tiers. IMMEDIATE processed by dedicated worker thread pool with 100ms SLA. Target: p95 prewarm time < 200ms, cache hit rate after prewarm > 0.99. Tracked in iam_cache_prewarm_queue and iam_cache_prewarm_stats.ACCEPTED
044Per-tenant connection pooling: platform_tenant_routing gains max_connections_pool INT column (default 10, ENTERPRISE up to 50). PgBouncer config generated from this table by ConfigSyncWorker on value change. Exceeding pool limit returns 503 POOL_EXHAUSTED. Prevents single large tenant from exhausting cluster connection capacity.ACCEPTED
045Tenant usage metrics and noisy-neighbor detection: platform_tenant_usage_metrics tracks real-time RPS, connections, Kafka lag, storage, AI tokens per 1-hour windows. NoisyNeighborDetector compares against 7-day median. WARNING at 2x median (log only). THROTTLE at 3x sustained 5 min (50% quota, consumer deprioritized). ISOLATE at 5x sustained 10 min (dedicated consumer groups, isolated connection pool, SRE page). MIGRATE on repeated ISOLATE events (cluster rebalance per ADR-031). Quota breaches immutably logged in platform_quota_breach_events.ACCEPTED
046Kafka consumer isolation by tenant tier: ENTERPRISE and LARGE tenants get dedicated consumer groups for high-volume topics (attendance.confirmed, grade.posted, msg.message.posted). SMALL and MEDIUM tenants share pooled consumer groups. Consumer group format: {topic}.dedicated.{tenant_id} or {topic}.shared.{pool_id}. KafkaConsumerRebalanceWorker manages assignments in platform_kafka_consumer_assignments. Prevents a single large tenant backlog from blocking all others on shared groups. Dedicated group lag thresholds: WARNING 10k, CRITICAL 50k. Shared group thresholds: WARNING 1k, CRITICAL 5k.ACCEPTED
047Messaging partition scale-up: msg_threads and msg_thread_participants increase from 16 to 64 HASH(school_id) buckets. Redis unread counter changes from simple String counter to ZSET (member: thread_id, score: last_message_timestamp) with 90-day TTL. Eliminates unbounded Redis key growth and enables efficient inbox sorting without DB queries. ZSET pattern: ZADD inbox:unread:{user_id} {timestamp} {thread_id}; count via ZCARD; sorted inbox via ZREVRANGE.ACCEPTED
048Financial ledger archival: pay_ledger_entries gains RANGE partition by created_at ANNUAL via pg_partman. Entries older than 7 years migrate to archive table (S3 Parquet via Athena). Compliance queries via Archive API using registered templates. Prevents unbounded ledger growth while maintaining FERPA-compliant 7-year retention accessible via compliance endpoint. Online partition migration via pg_partman batch procedure.ACCEPTED
049Analytics incremental snapshots: rpt_rebuild_snapshots stores weekly full-state snapshots of each rpt_* read model per school. Rebuild procedure: load latest snapshot (< 7 days old), apply events from kafka_offset_at_snapshot to current offset. Reduces rebuild time from hours (full replay) to minutes (snapshot + delta). FROM_SNAPSHOT target: < 10 minutes. Full replay fallback when no valid snapshot exists. Tracked in rpt_rebuild_jobs.ACCEPTED
050Read replica routing: platform_read_replica_routing defines query patterns that MUST route to read replicas. Mandatory replica patterns: all rpt_* queries, archive queries, aggregations over more than 7 days, analytics dashboard queries, report generation. Primary reserved exclusively for writes. ReplicaRoutingInterceptor evaluates rules at query parse time using table_pattern regex + query_pattern type. Replica removed from pool if replication_lag_seconds > 30 or consecutive_failures > 3. Tracked in platform_read_replica_health.ACCEPTED

M0 — Platform Core: Scalability Infrastructure

Scalability infrastructure: 12 tables (ADR-041–050). The 2 IAM prewarm tables (iam_cache_prewarm_queue, iam_cache_prewarm_stats) are documented in the IAM subsystem section. All tables live in the platform schema as part of M0 Platform Core.

Domain 1 — Tenant Quotas ****&**** Usage Metrics (4 tables)

TableDefinition
platform_tenant_quotasPer-tenant resource limits governing platform capacity allocation. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK(schools) NOT NULL UNIQUE, plan_tier ENUM(SMALL,MEDIUM,LARGE,ENTERPRISE) NOT NULL, max_api_rps INT NOT NULL (100 for SMALL, 500 MEDIUM, 2000 LARGE, 10000 ENTERPRISE), max_connections_pool INT NOT NULL (must match platform_tenant_routing.max_connections_pool — kept in sync by ConfigSyncWorker), max_storage_gb INT NOT NULL (50 SMALL, 200 MEDIUM, 1000 LARGE, 5000 ENTERPRISE), max_ai_tokens_daily INT NOT NULL, max_message_fanout INT NOT NULL (max recipients for a single announcement before async batching kicks in), max_concurrent_imports INT NOT NULL (maximum simultaneous platform_import_jobs in COMMITTING state), kafka_consumer_isolation ENUM(SHARED,DEDICATED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SHARED' (ADR-046 — DEDICATED for LARGE and ENTERPRISE), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Seeded at provisioning from plan_tier defaults. Overrides possible for individual tenants by platform admin.
platform_tenant_usage_metricsReal-time usage tracking for noisy-neighbor detection (ADR-045). id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK NOT NULL, metric_window_start TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, metric_window_end TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL (1-hour windows), api_requests_count INT NOT NULL, peak_api_rps INT NOT NULL, active_db_connections INT NOT NULL, kafka_consumer_lag_total BIGINT NOT NULL, storage_bytes_delta BIGINT NOT NULL (change in storage bytes during this window), ai_tokens_consumed INT NOT NULL, is_flagged_noisy BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (set by NoisyNeighborDetector when any metric exceeds 2x rolling median). RANGE partition by metric_window_start HOURLY via pg_partman, 7-day retention. INDEX(school_id, metric_window_start DESC). INDEX(is_flagged_noisy, metric_window_start) WHERE is_flagged_noisy = true — detector query.
platform_tenant_usage_dailyDaily rollup of usage metrics for billing, capacity planning, and plan tier review. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK NOT NULL, usage_date DATE NOT NULL, total_api_requests BIGINT NOT NULL, peak_api_rps INT NOT NULL, avg_api_rps NUMERIC(8,2) NOT NULL, total_storage_gb NUMERIC(10,3) NOT NULL, total_ai_tokens INT NOT NULL, noisy_neighbor_flags_count INT NOT NULL, throttle_events_count INT NOT NULL, isolate_events_count INT NOT NULL. UNIQUE(school_id, usage_date). RANGE partition by usage_date MONTHLY, 2-year retention. Written nightly by UsageDailyRollupWorker from platform_tenant_usage_metrics.
platform_quota_breach_eventsImmutable log of every quota breach and enforcement action. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK NOT NULL, quota_type ENUM(API_RPS,CONNECTIONS,STORAGE,AI_TOKENS,MESSAGE_FANOUT,CONCURRENT_IMPORTS) NOT NULL, limit_value BIGINT NOT NULL, actual_value BIGINT NOT NULL, breach_action ENUM(THROTTLED,WARNED,BLOCKED,ISOLATED) NOT NULL, breach_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. IMMUTABLE. RANGE partition by breach_at MONTHLY, 1-year retention. On INSERT: emit platform.quota.breached Kafka event consumed by alerting and analytics workers. INDEX(school_id, breach_at DESC).

Domain 2 — Connection Pool Governance (2 tables)

TableDefinition
platform_connection_pool_configPgBouncer connection pool configuration per database cluster. id UUID PK(v7), cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, pool_mode ENUM(SESSION,TRANSACTION,STATEMENT) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'TRANSACTION' (TRANSACTION mode required for schema-per-tenant isolation), default_pool_size INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 20, max_client_connections INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1000, reserve_pool_size INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 10, reserve_pool_timeout_seconds INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 5, query_timeout_seconds INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 30, idle_transaction_timeout_seconds INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 60, config_version INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 (incremented on every change — ConfigSyncWorker regenerates pgbouncer.ini and sends SIGHUP to PgBouncer process when version changes). Updated by platform admin only.
platform_connection_pool_healthReal-time connection pool metrics per cluster for monitoring and alerting. id UUID PK(v7), cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, active_client_connections INT NOT NULL, waiting_client_connections INT NOT NULL, server_connections_active INT NOT NULL, server_connections_idle INT NOT NULL, pools_exhausted_count INT NOT NULL (number of tenant pools currently at max_connections_pool limit), avg_query_time_ms INT NOT NULL, max_query_time_ms INT NOT NULL. RANGE partition by recorded_at DAILY, 30-day retention. Alert thresholds: waiting_client_connections > 50 = WARNING; pools_exhausted_count > 0 = PAGE.

Domain 3 — Kafka Consumer Isolation (2 tables)

TableDefinition
platform_kafka_consumer_assignmentsMaps tenants to specific Kafka consumer groups per topic, implementing ADR-046 tenant isolation. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK NOT NULL, topic TEXT NOT NULL, consumer_group TEXT NOT NULL (format: {topic}.dedicated.{tenant_id} for DEDICATED or {topic}.shared.{pool_id} for SHARED), isolation_type ENUM(DEDICATED,SHARED) NOT NULL, pool_id TEXT nullable (pool identifier for SHARED consumers — multiple tenants share one consumer group), assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, last_rebalanced_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. UNIQUE(school_id, topic). INDEX(consumer_group) — lookup by consumer group for lag monitoring. KafkaConsumerRebalanceWorker updates this table when tenants are promoted from SHARED to DEDICATED (triggered when kafka_consumer_isolation changes in platform_tenant_quotas).
platform_kafka_consumer_lag_alertsKafka consumer lag monitoring with tenant-aware alert thresholds (ADR-046). id UUID PK(v7), consumer_group TEXT NOT NULL, topic TEXT NOT NULL, partition_num INT NOT NULL, current_lag BIGINT NOT NULL, alert_level ENUM(OK,WARNING,CRITICAL,PAGE) NOT NULL, alert_triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, alert_resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, isolation_type ENUM(DEDICATED,SHARED) NOT NULL. UNIQUE(consumer_group, topic, partition_num). Alert thresholds: DEDICATED groups — WARNING 10k, CRITICAL 50k; SHARED groups — WARNING 1k, CRITICAL 5k (tighter because shared lag affects all tenants in the pool). On CRITICAL or PAGE: emit alert via Communications module to SRE on-call.

Domain 4 — Read Replica Routing (2 tables)

TableDefinition
platform_read_replica_routingDefines query patterns that must be routed to read replicas rather than the primary database (ADR-050). id UUID PK(v7), routing_rule_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, table_pattern TEXT NOT NULL (regex matching table names — e.g. '^rpt_' for all analytics tables), query_pattern ENUM(SELECT_AGGREGATE,SELECT_RANGE_SCAN,SELECT_REPORT,SELECT_EXPORT,SELECT_ANY) NOT NULL, min_date_range_days INT nullable (for range scan rules: only route to replica if date range exceeds this threshold), is_mandatory BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true (mandatory rules always route to replica; non-mandatory route to replica if available), priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (higher priority rules evaluated first when multiple patterns match). ReplicaRoutingInterceptor evaluates rules at query parse time before execution. Seeded at provisioning with standard rules for all rpt_* tables and archive queries.
platform_read_replica_healthReal-time replica health monitoring for routing decisions. id UUID PK(v7), cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL, replica_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, replica_endpoint TEXT NOT NULL, replication_lag_seconds INT NOT NULL, is_healthy BOOLEAN NOT NULL, consecutive_failures INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_health_check_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, removed_from_pool_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. Replica removed from routing pool if replication_lag_seconds > 30 OR consecutive_failures > 3. Re-admitted after 3 consecutive healthy checks. ReplicaHealthChecker polls every 10 seconds. On health change: emit replica.health.changed Kafka event consumed by ReplicaRouter.

Domain 5 — Analytics Rebuild Infrastructure (2 tables)

TableDefinition
rpt_rebuild_snapshotsPeriodic full-state snapshots of rpt_* read models enabling fast incremental rebuilds (ADR-049). id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, read_model_table TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'rpt_daily_attendance_summary', 'rpt_student_academic_summary'), snapshot_s3_key TEXT NOT NULL (S3 URI of the Parquet snapshot file), kafka_offset_at_snapshot JSONB NOT NULL (map of {topic: {partition: offset}} capturing the Kafka state at snapshot time — used as the starting point for delta replay), row_count INT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL (snapshots expire after 28 days — rebuild falls back to full replay if no valid snapshot). RANGE partition by created_at WEEKLY. INDEX(school_id, read_model_table, created_at DESC). Written by AnalyticsSnapshotWorker every Sunday at 02:00 UTC per school timezone.
rpt_rebuild_jobsTracks analytics read model rebuild operations for monitoring and debugging. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, read_model_table TEXT NOT NULL, rebuild_type ENUM(FULL_REPLAY,FROM_SNAPSHOT,INCREMENTAL) NOT NULL (FULL_REPLAY: replay all Kafka events from beginning; FROM_SNAPSHOT: load snapshot then replay delta; INCREMENTAL: apply only new events since last rebuild), snapshot_id UUID FK(rpt_rebuild_snapshots) nullable (populated for FROM_SNAPSHOT rebuilds), triggered_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (null for automated rebuilds), trigger_reason TEXT nullable, status ENUM(QUEUED,RUNNING,COMPLETED,FAILED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED', events_processed BIGINT nullable, rows_written INT nullable, duration_seconds INT nullable, error_message TEXT nullable, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. INDEX(school_id, read_model_table, created_at DESC). SLA target for FROM_SNAPSHOT: < 10 minutes.

Operational Procedures Summary

Partition Rebalancing (ADR-041) — 5-step online procedure Step 1: Enable pg_partman (CREATE EXTENSION pg_partman; SELECT partman.create_parent(...)). < 1 min. Step 2: Create 32 new hash partitions with _new suffix via pg_partman. < 5 min. Step 3: Online data migration via partition_data_proc() in batches of 10,000 rows. Duration varies by table size. Step 4: Atomic partition swap — ALTER TABLE DETACH/ATTACH old and new partitions in single transaction. < 1 min. Step 5: Drop old partitions after 24-hour verification period. < 1 min.
Noisy Neighbor Response (ADR-045) WARNING (2x median): Log to platform_tenant_usage_metrics.is_flagged_noisy. No operational action. Include in daily capacity report. THROTTLE (3x median sustained 5 min): API rate limit to 50% of quota. Kafka consumer deprioritized. Alert sent to tenant admin via Communications module. ISOLATE (5x median sustained 10 min): Route to dedicated consumer groups. Connection pool isolated. SRE paged. MIGRATE (repeated ISOLATE events): Tenant flagged for cluster rebalance per ADR-031. platform_tenant_routing.is_frozen set.

Kafka Events — Scalability Series

EventConsumersNotes
tenant.routing.changedPlatform CoreTenantRoutingCacheWorker
platform.quota.breachedQuotaEnforcerAlerting worker, Analytics
iam.cache.prewarmAuth ServiceIAMCachePrewarmWorker
analytics.rebuild.requestedPlatform AdminAnalyticsRebuildWorker
replica.health.changedReplicaHealthCheckerReplicaRouter
M60Service Ticketsprefix: tkt_13 tablesWave 1

Scope: M60 is the shared reactive ticketing engine for IT, facilities, and HR support requests. It is not a facilities management system. M65 Facilities Management handles proactive operational work; M60 handles reactive requests submitted by staff. A facilities work order in M65 may link to a Helpdesk ticket via DISPLAY-ONLY reference when a reactive ticket escalates into a formal work order.

TableDefinition
tkt_vendorsExternal vendor registry — IT repair companies, facilities contractors, cleaning firms, and other service providers. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, vendor_name TEXT NOT NULL, vendor_type ENUM(IT_REPAIR,FACILITIES_MAINTENANCE,CLEANING,ELECTRICAL,PLUMBING,HVAC,SECURITY,GROUNDS,OTHER) NOT NULL, contact_name TEXT, contact_email TEXT, contact_phone TEXT, website TEXT, is_preferred BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (preferred vendors are suggested first in the assignment UI), notes TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(school_id, vendor_name). INDEX(school_id, vendor_type, is_active).
tkt_categoriesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, parent_category_id UUID FK(tkt_categories), icon TEXT. UNIQUE(school_id, name). Supports IT, facilities, maintenance, HR support.
tkt_subcategoriesid UUID PK(v7), category_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, default_assignee_id UUID FK(hr_employees), auto_assign_to_role TEXT.
tkt_sla_policiesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, category_id UUID FK, priority ENUM(LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL), response_hours INT, resolution_hours INT. UNIQUE(school_id, category_id, priority).
tkt_ticketsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, category_id UUID FK, subcategory_id UUID FK, requester_id UUID FK(platform_users), assignee_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (internal staff assignee — null when assigned to external vendor), vendor_id UUID FK(tkt_vendors) nullable (external vendor assignee — null when assigned internally), vendor_reference TEXT nullable (the vendor's own job or work order reference number), vendor_assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, priority ENUM(LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL) DEFAULT 'MEDIUM', status ENUM(OPEN,IN_PROGRESS,VENDOR_ASSIGNED,PENDING_REQUESTER,RESOLVED,CLOSED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'OPEN' (VENDOR_ASSIGNED: ticket has been sent to an external vendor — SLA clock pauses while awaiting vendor), sla_policy_id UUID FK, first_response_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. CHECK: NOT (assignee_id IS NOT NULL AND vendor_id IS NOT NULL) — a ticket is assigned to either an internal staff member or an external vendor, not both simultaneously. INDEX(school_id, status, created_at DESC). INDEX(assignee_id, status) WHERE assignee_id IS NOT NULL. INDEX(vendor_id, status) WHERE vendor_id IS NOT NULL.
tkt_ticket_attachmentsid UUID PK(v7), ticket_id UUID FK, s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, filename TEXT, uploaded_by UUID FK, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Signed S3 URLs with 15–60 min expiry.
tkt_ticket_tagsUNIQUE(ticket_id, tag). tag TEXT NOT NULL.
tkt_problemsA Problem record linking multiple related tickets to a single root cause (e.g. a failed network switch causing 20 connectivity tickets). id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT NOT NULL, category_id UUID FK(tkt_categories) NOT NULL, status ENUM(OPEN,INVESTIGATING,KNOWN_ERROR,RESOLVED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'OPEN', root_cause TEXT nullable (populated when status=KNOWN_ERROR or RESOLVED), resolution TEXT nullable, workaround TEXT nullable (interim guidance for affected users while problem is unresolved), assigned_to_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable, vendor_id UUID FK(tkt_vendors) nullable, created_by UUID FK NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, status). On RESOLVED: all linked tkt_problem_tickets tickets that are still OPEN or IN_PROGRESS are updated to RESOLVED in a batch operation.
tkt_problem_ticketsLinks individual tickets to a Problem record. id UUID PK(v7), problem_id UUID FK(tkt_problems) NOT NULL, ticket_id UUID FK(tkt_tickets) NOT NULL. UNIQUE(problem_id, ticket_id). INDEX(ticket_id) — so a ticket can quickly show which problem it belongs to.
tkt_ticket_asset_linksLinks a helpdesk ticket to one or more tech_assets records. Enables IT admins to associate a support request with the specific device it concerns. id UUID PK(v7), ticket_id UUID FK(tkt_tickets) NOT NULL, asset_id UUID FK(tech_assets) NOT NULL. UNIQUE(ticket_id, asset_id). INDEX(asset_id) — so an asset record can show its full ticket history.
tkt_ticket_commentsid UUID PK(v7), ticket_id UUID FK, author_id UUID FK, body TEXT NOT NULL, is_internal BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(ticket_id, created_at ASC).
tkt_ticket_activityid UUID PK(v7), ticket_id UUID FK, actor_id UUID, activity_type ENUM(STATUS_CHANGE,REASSIGNMENT,COMMENT,ATTACHMENT,ESCALATION), metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(ticket_id, created_at ASC). Immutable activity log.
M61Transportationprefix: trn_38 tablesWave 2
TableDefinition
trn_vehiclesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, registration TEXT NOT NULL, make TEXT, model TEXT, year INT, capacity INT, vehicle_type ENUM(BUS,MINIBUS,VAN), status ENUM(ACTIVE,MAINTENANCE,RETIRED). UNIQUE(school_id, registration).
trn_vehicle_documentsid UUID PK(v7), vehicle_id UUID FK, document_type ENUM(INSURANCE,REGISTRATION,MOT,INSPECTION), s3_key TEXT, expiry_date DATE. Alert job at 60/30/7 days before expiry.
trn_vehicle_maintenanceid UUID PK(v7), vehicle_id UUID FK, maintenance_type TEXT, scheduled_date DATE, completed_date DATE, cost NUMERIC(8,2), notes TEXT, status ENUM(SCHEDULED,COMPLETED,CANCELLED).
trn_routesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, direction ENUM(AM,PM,BOTH), route_type ENUM(SCHEDULED,ADHOC,CONTRACTED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED' (SCHEDULED: regular daily route generated by the route optimiser or manually; ADHOC: one-off trip not recurring; CONTRACTED: operated by a third-party contractor), contractor_id UUID FK(tkt_vendors) nullable (the external transport contractor — set when route_type=CONTRACTED), required_arrival_by TIME nullable (the latest arrival time at the destination school — used as the hard deadline constraint for route generation), destination_building_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → fac_buildings.id nullable — the school building this route serves; drives arrival constraint in optimiser), status ENUM(ACTIVE,INACTIVE,GENERATED_PENDING_APPROVAL) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE' (GENERATED_PENDING_APPROVAL: produced by optimiser, awaiting TC review before activation), generation_request_id UUID FK(trn_generation_requests) nullable (the generation request that produced this route — null for manually created routes). UNIQUE(school_id, name).
trn_stopsid UUID PK(v7), route_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, latitude NUMERIC(9,6), longitude NUMERIC(9,6), sequence_order INT, scheduled_time TIME. INDEX(route_id, sequence_order).
trn_student_assignmentsUNIQUE(student_id, academic_year_id). id UUID PK(v7), route_id UUID FK, stop_id UUID FK, direction ENUM(AM,PM,BOTH), effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE.
trn_ridership_recordsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, route_id UUID FK, scan_direction ENUM(BOARDING,ALIGHTING), scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, stop_id UUID FK. INDEX(student_id, scanned_at DESC). RANGE partition by scanned_at monthly. Note: the Transportation No-Show Worker compares expected ridership (trn_student_assignments) against actual scans each morning — students expected on a route but not scanned within a configurable window trigger a trn_no_show_alerts record and parent notification.
trn_bus_passesid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, academic_year_id UUID FK, pass_type ENUM(ANNUAL,TERM,DAILY), qr_code_token TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, is_active BOOLEAN, valid_from DATE, valid_to DATE.
trn_vehicle_positionsid UUID PK(v7), vehicle_id UUID FK, latitude NUMERIC(9,6), longitude NUMERIC(9,6), speed NUMERIC(5,1), heading NUMERIC(5,1), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ. RANGE partition by recorded_at DAY via pg_partman. 90-day retention. BRIN index on recorded_at. Monitored in partition_mgmt_health.
trn_route_run_logsid UUID PK(v7), route_id UUID FK, vehicle_id UUID FK, driver_id UUID FK(hr_employees), run_date DATE, direction ENUM(AM,PM), status ENUM(SCHEDULED,IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED,CANCELLED,DELAYED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED', departure_at TIMESTAMPTZ, arrival_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delay_reason TEXT nullable (populated when status=DELAYED — e.g. 'Vehicle breakdown', 'Heavy traffic', 'Adverse weather'), estimated_delay_minutes INT nullable (estimated additional minutes — used by ETA notification worker to push updated ETAs to parents), manifest_id UUID FK(trn_run_manifests) nullable (the live manifest generated at dispatch time for this run — null until driver checks in via driver app). INDEX(route_id, run_date DESC). INDEX(manifest_id) WHERE manifest_id IS NOT NULL.
trn_eta_notificationsid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, route_run_id UUID FK, stop_id UUID FK, eta_minutes INT, notification_sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(student_id, notification_sent_at DESC).
trn_driver_qualificationsid UUID PK(v7), employee_id UUID FK UNIQUE, licence_number TEXT, licence_class TEXT, licence_expiry DATE, dbs_check_date DATE, dbs_ref TEXT, is_approved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. Alert: licence and DBS expiry.
trn_student_eta_preferencesUNIQUE(student_id). notification_channel ENUM(PUSH,SMS), notify_at_stop_sequence INT (stops before arrival), guardian_ids UUID[].
trn_no_show_alertsSafeguarding alert when a student expected on a bus route does not scan within the configured window. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, route_id UUID FK NOT NULL, expected_stop_id UUID FK NOT NULL, expected_direction ENUM(AM,PM) NOT NULL, expected_date DATE NOT NULL, scan_window_closes_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, alert_status ENUM(OPEN,RESOLVED,FALSE_ALARM) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'OPEN', resolved_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, resolution_notes TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, alert_status, expected_date). INDEX(student_id, expected_date). On INSERT: push notification to guardian_ids from trn_student_eta_preferences AND to school reception/safeguarding officer. On RESOLVED or FALSE_ALARM: emit trn.no_show.resolved Kafka event. Note: a student arriving via other means (parent drop-off) should be manually resolved as FALSE_ALARM by reception.
trn_transport_eligibilityDefines which students are entitled to bus transport and records their pickup/dropoff coordinates. The primary input to the route generation optimiser. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL UNIQUE, home_latitude NUMERIC(9,6) NOT NULL, home_longitude NUMERIC(9,6) NOT NULL, home_address TEXT NOT NULL (human-readable — for driver reference and parent confirmation), eligibility_type ENUM(STANDARD,SPECIAL_NEEDS,TEMPORARY,CONTRACTED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'STANDARD', max_ride_time_minutes INT nullable (student-specific override for maximum acceptable ride duration — overrides school default from trn_route_constraints when set), am_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (needs morning route), pm_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (needs afternoon route), effective_from DATE NOT NULL, effective_to DATE nullable (null = ongoing), notes TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, am_required, pm_required, effective_from).
trn_route_constraintsSchool-level constraint profile governing route generation. One active profile per school at any time. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID FK NOT NULL, constraint_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. '2026-27 Standard Profile'), max_ride_time_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 45 (maximum allowed journey time for any student on the route), max_route_mileage NUMERIC(7,2) nullable (maximum route length in miles — null = unconstrained), max_students_per_vehicle INT nullable (override school-wide vehicle capacity with a tighter planning cap), required_arrival_buffer_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 10 (how many minutes before school start the bus must arrive), max_stops_per_route INT nullable (upper bound on stops — helps avoid overly complex routes), walkable_radius_metres INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 400 (students within this radius of an existing stop are grouped to that stop rather than getting a new one), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. UNIQUE(school_id, constraint_name).
trn_generation_requestsA TC or system-initiated request to auto-generate routes for a given period. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, requested_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, request_type ENUM(FULL_YEAR,TERM,DATE_RANGE,SINGLE_DATE) NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK nullable, term_id UUID FK nullable, date_from DATE nullable, date_to DATE nullable, constraint_id UUID FK(trn_route_constraints) NOT NULL (the constraint profile to use for this generation run), directions ENUM(AM_ONLY,PM_ONLY,BOTH) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BOTH', status ENUM(QUEUED,RUNNING,COMPLETED,FAILED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED', optimiser_run_id TEXT nullable (external reference to the route optimisation job — e.g. a Google OR-Tools job ID or an internal worker job UUID), routes_generated INT nullable (count of candidate routes produced), students_covered INT nullable (count of eligible students accommodated), students_uncovered INT nullable (count of eligible students the optimiser could not route), error_message TEXT nullable, queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, status). On COMPLETED: TC receives notification to review generated candidates.
trn_generation_candidatesA candidate route produced by the route optimiser. Awaits TC review before becoming an active trn_routes record. id UUID PK(v7), request_id UUID FK(trn_generation_requests) NOT NULL, candidate_name TEXT NOT NULL (suggested route name — TC can rename before approval), direction ENUM(AM,PM) NOT NULL, vehicle_type_required ENUM(BUS,MINIBUS,VAN) NOT NULL (the smallest vehicle type that satisfies capacity — optimiser selects from trn_vehicles), total_students INT NOT NULL, total_stops INT NOT NULL, estimated_route_mileage NUMERIC(7,2) NOT NULL, estimated_duration_minutes INT NOT NULL (worst-case total route time including all stops), max_student_ride_time_minutes INT NOT NULL (the longest any single student on this route will ride), all_constraints_satisfied BOOLEAN NOT NULL (false if any constraint had to be relaxed to find a solution), constraint_violations JSONB nullable (array of {constraint, violation_description} for relaxed constraints), review_status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED,MODIFIED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, approved_route_id UUID FK(trn_routes) nullable (populated when APPROVED — the live route created from this candidate). INDEX(request_id, review_status).
trn_generation_candidate_stopsThe ordered stops within a route generation candidate. id UUID PK(v7), candidate_id UUID FK(trn_generation_candidates) NOT NULL, stop_name TEXT NOT NULL (suggested stop name — may be an address or intersection), latitude NUMERIC(9,6) NOT NULL, longitude NUMERIC(9,6) NOT NULL, sequence_order INT NOT NULL, scheduled_time TIME NOT NULL (estimated arrival time at this stop given departure from school), student_count INT NOT NULL (number of students the optimiser assigns to this stop), student_ids UUID[] NOT NULL (array of sis_students.id assigned to this stop — used when TC approves to create trn_stops and trn_student_assignments). UNIQUE(candidate_id, sequence_order). INDEX(candidate_id, sequence_order).
trn_adhoc_tripsA one-off transport event not on a recurring schedule — field trips, sports fixtures, extracurricular events. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, trip_name TEXT NOT NULL, trip_type ENUM(FIELD_TRIP,SPORTS_FIXTURE,EXTRACURRICULAR,STAFF_TRANSPORT,OTHER) NOT NULL, departure_location_description TEXT NOT NULL, destination_description TEXT NOT NULL, destination_latitude NUMERIC(9,6) nullable, destination_longitude NUMERIC(9,6) nullable, departure_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, return_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, vehicle_id UUID FK(trn_vehicles) nullable (assigned vehicle — null until TC assigns), driver_id UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (assigned driver — null until TC assigns), status ENUM(REQUESTED,APPROVED,VEHICLE_ASSIGNED,IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'REQUESTED', linked_approval_id UUID FK(wsk_approval_requests) nullable (field trips may require principal approval), linked_ext_trip_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY → ext_field_trips.id nullable — links to Extracurricular module when trip is school-organised), organised_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, departure_at). INDEX(status, departure_at) WHERE status IN ('APPROVED','VEHICLE_ASSIGNED').
trn_adhoc_trip_passengersStudents and staff travelling on a specific ad-hoc trip. id UUID PK(v7), trip_id UUID FK(trn_adhoc_trips) NOT NULL, person_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the human travelling on the trip — iam_person covers both students and staff uniformly), person_type ENUM(STUDENT,STAFF) NOT NULL, boarding_location TEXT nullable (if different from trip departure_location), has_boarded BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, has_returned BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. UNIQUE(trip_id, person_id). INDEX(trip_id, person_type).
trn_driver_sessionsActive driver app session for a route run. Created when the driver logs in to the app and selects their run; closed when the run is marked COMPLETED. id UUID PK(v7), run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) NOT NULL UNIQUE (one active session per run), driver_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, device_id TEXT NOT NULL (the driver's device identifier — used for push delivery), session_token TEXT NOT NULL (short-lived session token for driver app WebSocket authentication), session_started_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, last_heartbeat_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL (updated every 30s by driver app — used to detect lost connection), session_ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. INDEX(run_id, is_active). On session_started_at: trn_route_run_logs.status set to IN_PROGRESS and manifest generated if not already present.
trn_run_manifestsSnapshot of the planned stop and student sequence for a route run, generated at dispatch time. Immutable once created — actual execution is tracked in trn_dispatch_events. id UUID PK(v7), run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) NOT NULL UNIQUE, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, total_stops INT NOT NULL, total_students INT NOT NULL. INDEX(run_id).
trn_manifest_stopsPer-stop entry within a run manifest. Captures the planned state at dispatch time. id UUID PK(v7), manifest_id UUID FK(trn_run_manifests) NOT NULL, stop_id UUID FK(trn_stops) NOT NULL, sequence_order INT NOT NULL, scheduled_arrival TIME NOT NULL, student_count_planned INT NOT NULL, is_skipped BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (set during the run via dispatch event when all students at this stop are marked no-show — driver does not need to visit). UNIQUE(manifest_id, sequence_order). INDEX(manifest_id, sequence_order).
trn_manifest_studentsPer-student entry within a manifest stop. Tracks planned vs actual boarding. id UUID PK(v7), manifest_stop_id UUID FK(trn_manifest_stops) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, planned_action ENUM(PICKUP,DROPOFF) NOT NULL, actual_status ENUM(PENDING,BOARDED,DROPPED_OFF,NO_SHOW,REMOVED_PRE_RUN) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING' (REMOVED_PRE_RUN: student removed from manifest before run started due to absence notification received before dispatch), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(manifest_stop_id, student_id). INDEX(student_id, manifest_stop_id) — enables parent to query their child's current status.
trn_dispatch_eventsImmutable real-time event log during a route run. Every state change — stop reached, student boarded, no-show flagged, delay reported, re-route triggered, message received — is appended here. Powers the live driver app display, parent tracking, and post-run audit. id UUID PK(v7), run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) NOT NULL, event_type ENUM(RUN_STARTED,STOP_REACHED,STUDENT_BOARDED,STUDENT_NO_SHOW,STUDENT_DROPPED_OFF,STOP_SKIPPED,DELAY_REPORTED,VEHICLE_SUBSTITUTED,ROUTE_DEVIATED,ABSENCE_NOTIFICATION_RECEIVED,MESSAGE_RECEIVED,RUN_COMPLETED) NOT NULL, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, actor_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (the person who triggered the event — driver, TC, or parent; null for system events), stop_id UUID FK(trn_stops) nullable, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) nullable, metadata JSONB nullable (event-specific payload — e.g. for DELAY_REPORTED: {delay_minutes, reason}; for STUDENT_NO_SHOW: {notification_source: PARENT/SCHOOL}; for STOP_SKIPPED: {reason}). IMMUTABLE. RANGE partition by event_at MONTHLY, 3-year retention. INDEX(run_id, event_at ASC). INDEX(student_id, event_at DESC) WHERE student_id IS NOT NULL — parent tracking query.
trn_run_messagesOperational in-run messages sent to or from the driver during an active route run. Separate from msg_threads — these are dispatch communications, not personal conversations. id UUID PK(v7), run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) NOT NULL, sender_id UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, sender_type ENUM(DRIVER,SCHOOL_OFFICIAL,PARENT,SYSTEM) NOT NULL, message_text TEXT NOT NULL, related_student_id UUID FK(sis_students) nullable (the student this message concerns — e.g. parent notifying of absence, school notifying of pickup change), message_type ENUM(STUDENT_ABSENCE,STOP_CHANGE,EMERGENCY,GENERAL_INFO,DRIVER_REPLY) NOT NULL, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, delivered_to_driver_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (set when WebSocket delivery confirmed), read_by_driver_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(run_id, sent_at ASC). INDEX(run_id, delivered_to_driver_at) WHERE delivered_to_driver_at IS NULL — undelivered message queue. On INSERT with sender_type IN (SCHOOL_OFFICIAL,PARENT): push to driver via WebSocket on trn_driver_sessions.session_token; also append ABSENCE_NOTIFICATION_RECEIVED or similar trn_dispatch_events row. On student_absence message: trn_manifest_students.actual_status updated to NO_SHOW for that student; if student was only rider at their stop, trn_manifest_stops.is_skipped set to true and STOP_SKIPPED dispatch event emitted.
trn_student_transport_needsSpecial transport requirements for students with medical, physical, or care needs. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL UNIQUE, wheelchair_accessible_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, car_seat_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, escort_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (trained escort must be present on the vehicle), medical_equipment_onboard TEXT nullable (e.g. 'Epipen', 'Oxygen tank' — driver must verify present before departure), max_journey_time_override_minutes INT nullable (hard limit for this student — overrides route constraint), boarding_assistance_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, additional_notes TEXT nullable, reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, reviewed_at DATE NOT NULL. When wheelchair_accessible_required=true: route optimiser only assigns this student to vehicles where trn_vehicles.vehicle_type supports accessibility (application-layer check). INDEX(school_id, wheelchair_accessible_required).
trn_uncollected_child_eventsSafeguarding record for a student not collected from their bus stop. Immutable. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) NOT NULL, stop_id UUID FK(trn_stops) NOT NULL, driver_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, action_taken ENUM(RETURNED_TO_SCHOOL,WAITED_COLLECTED,CONTACTED_PARENT,CONTACTED_EMERGENCY_SERVICES,SCHOOL_OFFICIAL_ATTENDED) NOT NULL, action_notes TEXT NOT NULL, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, resolved_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable. IMMUTABLE. INDEX(school_id, event_at DESC). INDEX(student_id, event_at DESC). On INSERT: immediate push notification to school safeguarding officer and admin.
trn_vehicle_incidentsVehicle accident, breakdown, or near-miss record. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, vehicle_id UUID FK(trn_vehicles) NOT NULL, run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) nullable (null if incident occurred outside a run), driver_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, incident_type ENUM(ACCIDENT,BREAKDOWN,NEAR_MISS,THEFT,VANDALISM,MEDICAL_EMERGENCY_ON_BOARD,OTHER) NOT NULL, incident_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, location_description TEXT NOT NULL, latitude NUMERIC(9,6) nullable, longitude NUMERIC(9,6) nullable, students_on_board INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, injuries_reported BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, injury_description TEXT nullable, third_parties_involved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, police_report_reference TEXT nullable, insurance_claim_reference TEXT nullable, description TEXT NOT NULL, follow_up_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, linked_maintenance_id UUID FK(trn_vehicle_maintenance) nullable (for BREAKDOWN: links to resulting repair), report_s3_key TEXT nullable. INDEX(school_id, incident_at DESC). INDEX(vehicle_id, incident_at DESC).
trn_vehicle_fuel_logsVehicle fuel consumption records. id UUID PK(v7), vehicle_id UUID FK(trn_vehicles) NOT NULL, logged_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, log_date DATE NOT NULL, odometer_reading NUMERIC(9,1) NOT NULL (km or miles — school configures unit in trn_route_constraints), fuel_quantity NUMERIC(7,2) NOT NULL (litres or gallons), fuel_cost NUMERIC(8,2) nullable, fuel_type ENUM(DIESEL,PETROL,ELECTRIC,HYBRID,LPG) NOT NULL, refuel_location TEXT nullable. INDEX(vehicle_id, log_date DESC).
trn_driver_hours_logsCumulative driver hours tracking for regulatory compliance (EU Working Time Directive, US Federal HOS rules). id UUID PK(v7), driver_id UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, run_id UUID FK(trn_route_run_logs) nullable (null for non-run driving duties), log_date DATE NOT NULL, duty_start_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, duty_end_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, driving_minutes INT nullable (computed on duty_end_at — minutes spent actually driving vs other duties), break_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, cumulative_weekly_minutes INT nullable (computed on duty_end_at — running total for the ISO week; compared against regulatory limit). INDEX(driver_id, log_date DESC). Alert: when cumulative_weekly_minutes approaches jurisdiction-specific limit (configurable per school).
trn_route_change_logImmutable audit trail of every change to a route definition — stop added, removed, reordered, scheduled time changed. id UUID PK(v7), route_id UUID FK(trn_routes) NOT NULL, changed_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, change_type ENUM(STOP_ADDED,STOP_REMOVED,STOP_REORDERED,STOP_TIME_CHANGED,STUDENT_ADDED,STUDENT_REMOVED,ROUTE_ACTIVATED,ROUTE_DEACTIVATED) NOT NULL, stop_id UUID FK(trn_stops) nullable, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) nullable, old_value JSONB nullable (snapshot of changed field before the change), new_value JSONB nullable (snapshot after the change), reason TEXT nullable. IMMUTABLE. RANGE partition by changed_at MONTHLY, 7-year retention (FERPA safeguarding record). INDEX(route_id, changed_at DESC).
rpt_fleet_statusMaterialised fleet condition summary for TC dashboard. One row per vehicle, updated nightly. id UUID PK(v7), vehicle_id UUID FK(trn_vehicles) NOT NULL UNIQUE, school_id UUID NOT NULL, vehicle_registration TEXT NOT NULL, vehicle_status TEXT NOT NULL, days_until_insurance_expiry INT nullable, days_until_registration_expiry INT nullable, days_until_mot_expiry INT nullable, days_until_licence_expiry INT nullable (for the assigned primary driver), maintenance_overdue BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, last_incident_date DATE nullable, total_incidents_this_year INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, current_route_assignment TEXT nullable (name of the route this vehicle is currently assigned to if any). INDEX(school_id, maintenance_overdue). INDEX(school_id, days_until_insurance_expiry).
trn_route_change_requestsParent-submitted request for a temporary change to child's bus assignment (e.g. going to a different stop, or not taking the bus on a specific day). id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, submitted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, change_date DATE NOT NULL, change_type ENUM(DIFFERENT_STOP,NO_BUS,DIFFERENT_ROUTE) NOT NULL, requested_stop_id UUID FK nullable (the requested alternative stop — null for NO_BUS), requested_route_id UUID FK nullable (alternative route if DIFFERENT_ROUTE), reason TEXT nullable, status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(student_id, change_date). INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'PENDING'. On APPROVED: Transportation Worker creates a one-day override in trn_student_assignments effective for change_date only. On change_date morning: no-show alert logic uses the approved request to adjust expected ridership.
M62IT Infrastructureprefix: tech_26 tablesWave 2
TableDefinition
tech_asset_categoriesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, depreciation_years INT, maintenance_interval_months INT. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
tech_assetsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, category_id UUID FK, asset_tag TEXT NOT NULL, serial_number TEXT, make TEXT, model TEXT, purchase_date DATE, purchase_cost NUMERIC(10,2), status ENUM(AVAILABLE,ASSIGNED,REPAIR,RETIRED,LOST), warranty_expiry DATE. UNIQUE(school_id, asset_tag). INDEX(school_id, status).
tech_asset_documentsid UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK, document_type ENUM(INVOICE,WARRANTY,INSURANCE,MANUAL), s3_key TEXT, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
tech_asset_assignmentsid UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK, assigned_to_id UUID FK(platform_users), assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, returned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, condition_at_assign ENUM(EXCELLENT,GOOD,FAIR,DAMAGED), condition_at_return ENUM(EXCELLENT,GOOD,FAIR,DAMAGED), notes TEXT, signature_request_id UUID FK(platform_signature_requests). INDEX(assigned_to_id) WHERE returned_at IS NULL for active assignments.
tech_damage_reportsid UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK, reported_by UUID FK, description TEXT NOT NULL, severity ENUM(MINOR,MODERATE,MAJOR,WRITE_OFF), photo_s3_keys TEXT[], reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, repair_record_id UUID FK(tech_repair_records).
tech_repair_recordsid UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK NOT NULL, damage_report_id UUID FK nullable, vendor_id UUID FK(tkt_vendors) nullable (registered external vendor — preferred; set tech_assets.status=VENDOR_REPAIR on INSERT), vendor_text TEXT nullable (free-text vendor for one-off vendors not in tkt_vendors registry), repair_type ENUM(INTERNAL,EXTERNAL_VENDOR,WARRANTY_CLAIM) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'EXTERNAL_VENDOR', cost NUMERIC(8,2), estimated_return_date DATE nullable, sent_for_repair_at TIMESTAMPTZ, returned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_notes TEXT, status ENUM(PENDING,IN_REPAIR,COMPLETE,WRITTEN_OFF) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING'. CHECK: vendor_id IS NOT NULL OR vendor_text IS NOT NULL OR repair_type = 'INTERNAL'. On INSERT with vendor_id or vendor_text: automatically set tech_assets.status = VENDOR_REPAIR. On returned_at set: set tech_assets.status = AVAILABLE (or ASSIGNED if it was assigned before).
tech_mdm_sync_logsid UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK, mdm_provider ENUM(JAMF,INTUNE,MOSYLE,GOOGLE), sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ, device_name TEXT, os_version TEXT, last_check_in TIMESTAMPTZ, is_compliant BOOLEAN, compliance_details JSONB. INDEX(asset_id, sync_at DESC).
tech_device_usage_summariesUNIQUE(asset_id, summary_date). screen_time_minutes INT, apps_used TEXT[], flagged_activity BOOLEAN, summary_source TEXT. Populated from MDM provider data.
tech_software_licencesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, software_name TEXT NOT NULL, vendor TEXT, licence_type ENUM(PER_SEAT,SITE,SUBSCRIPTION), total_seats INT, used_seats INT, expiry_date DATE, annual_cost NUMERIC(10,2). Alert: 80% seat usage, 30 days before expiry.
tech_software_assignmentsUNIQUE(licence_id, assignee_id). assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
tech_remote_actionsImmutable log of remote MDM actions issued to a device (lock, wipe, restart, locate, unenroll). id UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK(tech_assets) NOT NULL, action_type ENUM(LOCK,WIPE,RESTART,LOCATE,UNENROLL,ENABLE_LOST_MODE,DISABLE_LOST_MODE) NOT NULL, initiated_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, justification TEXT NOT NULL (mandatory — audit requirement; minimum 20 characters), mdm_command_ref TEXT nullable (the MDM provider's command ID for tracking), status ENUM(PENDING,SENT,COMPLETED,FAILED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, failure_reason TEXT nullable. IMMUTABLE. INDEX(asset_id, initiated_at DESC). On WIPE action: tech_assets.status automatically set to AVAILABLE once completed (device reset; previous assignment ended).
tech_inventory_auditsRecords of formal physical device inventory audits. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, audit_name TEXT NOT NULL, conducted_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, audit_date DATE NOT NULL, total_assets_expected INT NOT NULL, total_assets_found INT NOT NULL, total_assets_missing INT NOT NULL, total_assets_unrecorded INT NOT NULL (assets found physically but not in the system), audit_notes TEXT, status ENUM(IN_PROGRESS,COMPLETED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'IN_PROGRESS', completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, audit_date DESC).
tech_inventory_audit_itemsIndividual asset verification within an inventory audit. id UUID PK(v7), audit_id UUID FK(tech_inventory_audits) NOT NULL, asset_id UUID FK(tech_assets) nullable (null for unrecorded assets found during audit), asset_tag TEXT NOT NULL (scanned or entered asset tag), found BOOLEAN NOT NULL, condition_observed ENUM(EXCELLENT,GOOD,FAIR,DAMAGED) nullable, location_observed TEXT nullable, discrepancy_notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(audit_id, asset_tag). INDEX(audit_id, found).
tech_mdm_alertsActive MDM alerts for policy violations and stale device check-ins. id UUID PK(v7), asset_id UUID FK(tech_assets) NOT NULL, alert_type ENUM(STALE_CHECKIN,POLICY_VIOLATION,JAILBREAK_DETECTED,ENCRYPTION_DISABLED,UNAUTHORISED_APP,LOW_STORAGE) NOT NULL, alert_detail TEXT, first_detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, last_detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, is_resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, resolved_by UUID FK nullable, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(asset_id, is_resolved) WHERE is_resolved = false. INDEX(school_id, alert_type, is_resolved) WHERE is_resolved = false — IT admin dashboard.
tech_procurement_ordersDevice purchasing records and procurement tracking. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, order_title TEXT NOT NULL, vendor_id UUID FK(tkt_vendors) nullable, vendor_text TEXT nullable, purchase_order_number TEXT, ordered_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, order_date DATE NOT NULL, expected_delivery_date DATE nullable, total_cost NUMERIC(10,2), status ENUM(DRAFT,SUBMITTED,APPROVED,ORDERED,DELIVERED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT', linked_approval_id UUID FK(wsk_approval_requests) nullable (high-value orders may require approval), delivery_notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(school_id, status). On DELIVERED: IT admin creates tech_assets records for each delivered device and links via procurement_order_id.
tech_licence_renewalsSoftware licence renewal history. id UUID PK(v7), licence_id UUID FK(tech_software_licences) NOT NULL, previous_expiry_date DATE NOT NULL, new_expiry_date DATE NOT NULL, renewal_cost NUMERIC(10,2), renewed_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, renewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, notes TEXT. INDEX(licence_id, renewed_at DESC). On INSERT: tech_software_licences.expiry_date updated to new_expiry_date.
tech_infrastructure_itemsNon-assignable infrastructure assets such as switches, access points, servers, and printers not managed as user devices. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, item_name TEXT NOT NULL, item_type ENUM(SWITCH,ACCESS_POINT,SERVER,ROUTER,FIREWALL,PRINTER,NAS,UPS,OTHER) NOT NULL, location TEXT NOT NULL (building and room), ip_address TEXT nullable, mac_address TEXT nullable, make TEXT, model TEXT, serial_number TEXT, purchase_date DATE, warranty_expiry DATE nullable, status ENUM(ACTIVE,MAINTENANCE,DECOMMISSIONED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', last_checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, notes TEXT. UNIQUE(school_id, serial_number) WHERE serial_number IS NOT NULL. INDEX(school_id, item_type, status).
M63Food Serviceprefix: fds_31 tablesWave 3
TableDefinition
fds_menu_cyclesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, cycle_length_days INT DEFAULT 5, is_active BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, name).
fds_menu_itemsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, category ENUM(MAIN,SIDE,DESSERT,DRINK,SNACK), unit_cost NUMERIC(5,2), calories INT, allergens TEXT[] (free-text array for display), allergen_codes TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' (structured allergen identifiers using standard codes — e.g. MILK, EGG, FISH, SHELLFISH, TREE_NUTS, PEANUTS, WHEAT, SOYBEANS, SESAME — used for machine-readable POS cross-check against fds_student_allergen_alerts.allergen_code), is_vegetarian BOOLEAN, is_vegan BOOLEAN, is_gluten_free BOOLEAN, is_preorderable BOOLEAN DEFAULT true (when false: item cannot appear in pre-order windows — for daily specials, salad bars, or items with unpredictable availability). INDEX(school_id, category). INDEX(school_id, allergen_codes) using GIN — allergen cross-reference query.
fds_daily_menusid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, menu_date DATE NOT NULL, cycle_id UUID FK, meal_type ENUM(BREAKFAST,LUNCH,DINNER,SNACK). UNIQUE(school_id, menu_date, meal_type).
fds_daily_menu_itemsUNIQUE(daily_menu_id, menu_item_id). quantity_prepared INT, quantity_served INT nullable (updated at session close — total units served; required for USDA waste tracking), quantity_wasted INT nullable (computed: quantity_prepared - quantity_served; or directly entered if over-prepared), is_available BOOLEAN DEFAULT true.
fds_pos_devicesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, device_name TEXT NOT NULL, location TEXT, device_type ENUM(CASHIER_STAFFED,SELF_SERVICE_KIOSK,MOBILE_CART) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CASHIER_STAFFED' (SELF_SERVICE_KIOSK: student PIN entry and touchscreen item selection — allergen warnings displayed to student directly; MOBILE_CART: for concessions or outdoor events), is_active BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(school_id, device_name).
fds_meal_service_sessionsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, service_date DATE, meal_type ENUM(BREAKFAST,LUNCH,DINNER,SNACK), opened_by UUID FK, opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
fds_meal_transactionsid UUID PK(v7), patron_id UUID FK(iam_person) NOT NULL (the human being served — iam_person covers students and staff uniformly per ADR-055; patron_type disambiguates for business rules), patron_type ENUM(STUDENT,STAFF) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'STUDENT', session_id UUID FK, pos_device_id UUID FK, items JSONB, total NUMERIC(6,2), payment_method ENUM(LUNCH_ACCOUNT,INVOICE,CASH,FREE_MEAL,STAFF_ACCOUNT), allergen_override_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (set to true when the transaction was processed despite a CRITICAL allergen match — requires supervisor_override_id), supervisor_override_id UUID FK(platform_users) nullable (required when allergen_override_required=true — the supervisor who authorised serving despite allergen alert), served_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(patron_id, served_at DESC). INDEX(session_id, served_at).
fds_student_dietary_profilesid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK UNIQUE, school_id UUID, dietary_restrictions TEXT[], allergens TEXT[] (preference-based dietary notes — for display only), free_meal_eligible BOOLEAN, meal_plan_type ENUM(STANDARD,VEGETARIAN,VEGAN,HALAL,KOSHER,OTHER). Checked at POS terminal on student scan. NOTE: allergy data for POS safety enforcement comes from fds_student_allergen_alerts (read model from Health module) — not this table. fds_student_dietary_profiles.allergens is a preference/display field only.
fds_usda_reimbursement_claimsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, academic_year_id UUID FK, month_year DATE, free_meals_count INT, reduced_meals_count INT, paid_meals_count INT, reimbursement_amount NUMERIC(10,2), status ENUM(DRAFT,SUBMITTED,APPROVED,REJECTED), submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
fds_preorder_windowsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, opens_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closes_at TIMESTAMPTZ, service_date DATE, meal_type ENUM(BREAKFAST,LUNCH,DINNER,SNACK). UNIQUE(school_id, service_date, meal_type).
fds_meal_preordersid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, preorder_window_id UUID FK, status ENUM(PENDING,CONFIRMED,CANCELLED). UNIQUE(student_id, preorder_window_id).
fds_meal_preorder_itemsid UUID PK(v7), preorder_id UUID FK, menu_item_id UUID FK, quantity INT DEFAULT 1.
fds_preorder_production_reportsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, service_date DATE, meal_type ENUM(BREAKFAST,LUNCH,DINNER,SNACK), generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, report_data JSONB (item totals, dietary breakdown, staffing estimate).
fds_student_allergen_alertsHealth-to-Food-Service read model. Structured allergen records for POS and pre-order allergy enforcement. Populated and maintained by the Food Service module's consumer of hlth.allergy_alert.changed Kafka events (ADR-030 pattern — Food Service never reads hlth_* tables directly). id UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, school_id UUID NOT NULL, allergen_code TEXT NOT NULL (standardised allergen identifier matching fds_menu_items.allergen_codes — e.g. PEANUTS, TREE_NUTS, MILK, EGG, WHEAT, SHELLFISH, FISH, SOYBEANS, SESAME), allergen_display_name TEXT NOT NULL (human-readable name for POS display), severity ENUM(INFO,WARNING,CRITICAL) NOT NULL (CRITICAL = anaphylactic/life-threatening — POS blocks transaction and requires supervisor override; WARNING = serious but not life-threatening — POS alerts but allows with acknowledgement; INFO = preference/intolerance — POS displays only), source_health_alert_id UUID NOT NULL (SOFT INTEGRITY → hlth_health_alerts.id — the originating health alert; used to detect staleness), is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. UNIQUE(student_id, allergen_code) WHERE is_active = true. INDEX(student_id, is_active). INDEX(school_id, allergen_code) WHERE is_active = true — bulk allergen cross-reference. POS check: when student scans in, SELECT allergen_codes FROM fds_student_allergen_alerts WHERE student_id=$1 AND is_active=true; cross-reference with allergen_codes[] of items in the transaction; on match emit warning or block per severity.
fds_dietary_update_requestsParent-submitted request to update their child's dietary profile. Reviewed by food service manager. Follows hlth_parent_update_requests pattern. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, submitted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, change_type ENUM(ADD_RESTRICTION,REMOVE_RESTRICTION,ADD_ALLERGEN,REMOVE_ALLERGEN,CHANGE_MEAL_PLAN,UPDATE_ELIGIBILITY) NOT NULL, proposed_value TEXT NOT NULL (the new restriction, allergen, or meal plan type being requested), reason TEXT nullable, status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,REJECTED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'PENDING'.
fds_inventory_groupsNamed inventory pool — a distinct stock area with its own accountability. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Main Lunch', 'Breakfast Programme', 'Concessions', 'Athletics Snack Bar', 'Catering'), group_type ENUM(LUNCH,BREAKFAST,SNACK,CONCESSIONS,CATERING,OTHER) NOT NULL, location TEXT nullable (physical location of this stock — e.g. 'Main Kitchen', 'Gymnasium Concession Stand'), is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, managed_by UUID FK(hr_employees) nullable (the staff member responsible for this group's inventory). UNIQUE(school_id, name). INDEX(school_id, is_active).
fds_inventory_itemsFood ingredient and product master — what can be stocked, regardless of which group holds it. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, unit TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'kg', 'litres', 'units', 'cases'), category ENUM(PROTEIN,DAIRY,GRAIN,VEGETABLE,FRUIT,CONDIMENT,BEVERAGE,PACKAGING,OTHER) NOT NULL, allergen_codes TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' (same codes as fds_menu_items.allergen_codes), reorder_threshold NUMERIC(8,2) nullable (when quantity_on_hand in any group falls below this, emit fds.inventory.low Kafka event → Task Worker creates procurement task), preferred_vendor_id UUID FK(tkt_vendors) nullable, preferred_vendor_text TEXT nullable, unit_cost NUMERIC(8,4) nullable (approximate cost per unit — for food cost reporting). UNIQUE(school_id, name). INDEX(school_id, category).
fds_inventory_levelsCurrent quantity of an inventory item within a specific group. id UUID PK(v7), group_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_groups) NOT NULL, item_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_items) NOT NULL, quantity_on_hand NUMERIC(10,3) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_counted_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable (last physical count date — for identifying stale records). CHECK(quantity_on_hand >= 0). UNIQUE(group_id, item_id). INDEX(group_id, quantity_on_hand) WHERE quantity_on_hand = 0. When quantity_on_hand falls below fds_inventory_items.reorder_threshold: emit fds.inventory.low Kafka event.
fds_inventory_transactionsImmutable append-only log of every inventory stock movement. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, group_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_groups) NOT NULL, item_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_items) NOT NULL, transaction_type ENUM(RECEIPT,USAGE,WASTE,ADJUSTMENT,TRANSFER_IN,TRANSFER_OUT,STOCKTAKE) NOT NULL, quantity_delta NUMERIC(10,3) NOT NULL (positive for IN movements: RECEIPT,TRANSFER_IN; negative for OUT movements: USAGE,WASTE,TRANSFER_OUT), performed_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, transaction_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, transfer_reference_id UUID nullable (links paired TRANSFER_OUT and TRANSFER_IN rows — both rows share the same transfer_reference_id for atomic traceability), related_session_id UUID FK(fds_meal_service_sessions) nullable (for USAGE: the service session that consumed these items), notes TEXT nullable. IMMUTABLE. RANGE partition by transaction_at MONTHLY, 3-year retention. INDEX(group_id, item_id, transaction_at DESC). INDEX(transfer_reference_id) WHERE transfer_reference_id IS NOT NULL.
fds_inventory_transfer_requestsRequest to transfer stock from one inventory group to another. Low-value transfers can be auto-approved; high-value or cross-location transfers may require manager approval. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, from_group_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_groups) NOT NULL, to_group_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_groups) NOT NULL, item_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_items) NOT NULL, quantity NUMERIC(10,3) NOT NULL, reason TEXT nullable, status ENUM(PENDING,APPROVED,COMPLETED,REJECTED,CANCELLED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', requested_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, reviewed_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, transfer_reference_id UUID nullable (set on COMPLETED — links to the paired fds_inventory_transactions rows). INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED'). CHECK: from_group_id != to_group_id.
fds_negative_balance_policySchool-level configuration for how to handle a student attempting to purchase a meal with a negative or zero lunch account balance. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE, policy_type ENUM(SERVE_STANDARD_ALWAYS,SERVE_ALTERNATE_MEAL,DENY_SERVICE,NOTIFY_ONLY) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SERVE_STANDARD_ALWAYS' (SERVE_STANDARD_ALWAYS: always serve regardless of balance — most US districts use this to avoid stigma; SERVE_ALTERNATE_MEAL: serve a designated alternate meal; DENY_SERVICE: do not serve — not recommended for student welfare reasons; NOTIFY_ONLY: serve but send immediate notification to parent), alternate_meal_description TEXT nullable (for SERVE_ALTERNATE_MEAL: what to serve, e.g. 'Cheese sandwich and milk'), negative_balance_notification_threshold NUMERIC(8,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (send notification when balance falls below this amount — e.g. set to 5.00 to notify 5 days before running out), parent_notification_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true.
fds_waste_logsSession-level food waste record. id UUID PK(v7), session_id UUID FK(fds_meal_service_sessions) NOT NULL, logged_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, total_plate_waste_kg NUMERIC(6,2) nullable (estimated total student plate waste — weighed at tray return), notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(session_id). INDEX(session_id).
fds_temperature_logsFood safety temperature compliance log. Required by health authorities. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, check_location ENUM(DELIVERY,REFRIGERATOR,FREEZER,SERVING_LINE,HOT_HOLD,COLD_HOLD,COOK_TEMP) NOT NULL, location_name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Walk-in Fridge 1', 'Hot Hold Counter A'), temperature_celsius NUMERIC(4,1) NOT NULL, safe_range_min NUMERIC(4,1) NOT NULL, safe_range_max NUMERIC(4,1) NOT NULL, is_compliant BOOLEAN NOT NULL (application layer: temperature_celsius BETWEEN safe_range_min AND safe_range_max), corrective_action TEXT nullable (required when is_compliant=false), logged_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. INDEX(school_id, is_compliant, logged_at DESC) WHERE is_compliant = false — non-compliance review. INDEX(school_id, logged_at DESC). RANGE partition by logged_at MONTHLY, 3-year retention.
fds_cash_drawer_reconciliationCash drawer balance at session open and close for reconciliation. id UUID PK(v7), session_id UUID FK(fds_meal_service_sessions) NOT NULL, pos_device_id UUID FK(fds_pos_devices) NOT NULL, opening_balance NUMERIC(8,2) NOT NULL, expected_closing_balance NUMERIC(8,2) NOT NULL (computed: opening_balance + SUM of CASH transactions in session), actual_closing_balance NUMERIC(8,2) nullable (physically counted by staff at session close), variance NUMERIC(8,2) nullable (actual - expected — negative = short, positive = over), reconciled_by UUID FK(platform_users) nullable, reconciled_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, status ENUM(OPEN,RECONCILED,VARIANCE_FLAGGED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'OPEN'. UNIQUE(session_id, pos_device_id). INDEX(school_id, status) WHERE status = 'VARIANCE_FLAGGED'.
fds_eligibility_applicationsNSLP free/reduced price meal eligibility application submitted by a family. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, submitted_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, household_size INT NOT NULL, annual_household_income NUMERIC(10,2) nullable (for income-based applications — encrypted at rest), snap_benefit_case_number TEXT nullable (for categorical eligibility via SNAP/TANF/FDPIR — reference number only, not benefit details), application_type ENUM(INCOME_BASED,CATEGORICAL,DIRECT_CERTIFICATION) NOT NULL, status ENUM(SUBMITTED,UNDER_REVIEW,APPROVED,DENIED,WITHDRAWN) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SUBMITTED', submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL. INDEX(school_id, academic_year_id, status). INDEX(student_id, academic_year_id).
fds_eligibility_determinationsEligibility determination outcome for an application. id UUID PK(v7), application_id UUID FK(fds_eligibility_applications) NOT NULL UNIQUE, determined_by UUID FK(platform_users) NOT NULL, determined_at DATE NOT NULL, eligibility_category ENUM(FREE,REDUCED,PAID,DENIED) NOT NULL, effective_from DATE NOT NULL, effective_to DATE NOT NULL (typically end of academic year — annual re-certification required), determination_basis TEXT nullable (notes on how eligibility was determined), notification_sent BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, notification_sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. On APPROVED with FREE or REDUCED: update fds_student_dietary_profiles.free_meal_eligible accordingly.
fds_recipesRecipe definition for a menu item — maps a finished product back to raw ingredients for procurement planning and cost calculation. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, menu_item_id UUID FK(fds_menu_items) NOT NULL UNIQUE, yield_servings INT NOT NULL (how many servings this recipe produces per batch), prep_time_minutes INT nullable, cook_time_minutes INT nullable, instructions TEXT nullable, version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '1.0', is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true. INDEX(school_id, menu_item_id).
fds_recipe_ingredientsIngredients and quantities required for a recipe. id UUID PK(v7), recipe_id UUID FK(fds_recipes) NOT NULL, inventory_item_id UUID FK(fds_inventory_items) NOT NULL, quantity_per_batch NUMERIC(10,4) NOT NULL (quantity of this ingredient needed per recipe yield_servings batch), notes TEXT nullable (e.g. 'diced', 'room temperature'). UNIQUE(recipe_id, inventory_item_id). INDEX(recipe_id).
M64Clubs & Student Lifeprefix: ext_22 tablesWave 3
TableDefinition
ext_activity_typesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, name TEXT NOT NULL, category ENUM(SPORT,ARTS,ACADEMIC,LEADERSHIP,COMMUNITY,OTHER). UNIQUE(school_id, name).
ext_activitiesid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, activity_type_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK, coach_id UUID FK(hr_employees), max_participants INT, status ENUM(ACTIVE,INACTIVE,COMPLETED), group_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to grp_groups.id nullable). INDEX(school_id, academic_year_id). INDEX(group_id) WHERE group_id IS NOT NULL.
ext_activity_membersUNIQUE(activity_id, student_id). joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM(ACTIVE,WITHDRAWN,SUSPENDED), role ENUM(PARTICIPANT,CAPTAIN,VICE_CAPTAIN).
ext_eligibility_rulesid UUID PK(v7), activity_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM(MIN_GPA,MAX_ABSENCES,GRADE_LEVEL,NO_ACTIVE_SUSPENSION), threshold NUMERIC(5,2), is_blocking BOOLEAN.
ext_eligibility_checksUNIQUE(activity_id, student_id, checked_at). result ENUM(ELIGIBLE,INELIGIBLE,PROVISIONAL), reasons JSONB, checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
ext_field_tripsid UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID, activity_id UUID FK, destination TEXT NOT NULL, trip_date DATE, return_date DATE, cost NUMERIC(8,2), status ENUM(PLANNING,APPROVED,COMPLETED,CANCELLED), lead_teacher_id UUID FK(hr_employees). INDEX(school_id, trip_date).
ext_consent_recordsUNIQUE(field_trip_id, student_id). guardian_id UUID FK, consented BOOLEAN, consented_at TIMESTAMPTZ, signature_request_id UUID FK(platform_signature_requests).
ext_medical_consentParent-granted medical consent for school trips and activities — separate from general consent. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, field_trip_id UUID FK(ext_field_trips) NOT NULL, guardian_id UUID FK(sis_guardians) NOT NULL, consent_granted BOOLEAN NOT NULL, allows_emergency_treatment BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (parent authorises school to seek emergency medical treatment if unable to reach guardian), allows_medication_administration BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (parent authorises nurse or designated staff to administer listed medications on the trip), medications_to_administer JSONB nullable (array of {medication_name, dose, frequency, condition} — only populated when allows_medication_administration=true), additional_notes TEXT nullable, consented_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, signature_request_id UUID FK(platform_signature_requests) nullable. UNIQUE(field_trip_id, student_id). INDEX(field_trip_id, guardian_id).
ext_chaperone_assignmentsid UUID PK(v7), field_trip_id UUID FK, employee_id UUID FK, role ENUM(LEAD,SUPPORT,DRIVER), confirmed BOOLEAN. UNIQUE(field_trip_id, employee_id).
ext_activity_schedulesid UUID PK(v7), activity_id UUID FK, day_of_week SMALLINT, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, location TEXT, recurring BOOLEAN. INDEX(activity_id).
ext_activity_resultsid UUID PK(v7), activity_id UUID FK, result_date DATE, opponent TEXT, result_type ENUM(WIN,LOSS,DRAW,COMPLETION), score TEXT, notes TEXT.
ext_individual_resultsid UUID PK(v7), activity_result_id UUID FK, student_id UUID FK, performance_notes TEXT, award ENUM(NONE,COMMENDATION,MVP,FIRST,SECOND,THIRD).
ext_stat_definitionsid UUID PK(v7), activity_type_id UUID FK, stat_name TEXT NOT NULL, stat_unit TEXT, description TEXT. UNIQUE(activity_type_id, stat_name).
ext_stat_valuesid UUID PK(v7), student_id UUID FK, activity_id UUID FK, stat_definition_id UUID FK, value NUMERIC(10,3), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(student_id, activity_id, stat_definition_id).
ext_practice_logsCoach-recorded practice or training session. id UUID PK(v7), activity_id UUID FK(ext_activities) NOT NULL, session_date DATE NOT NULL, location TEXT nullable, duration_minutes INT, session_notes TEXT nullable, recorded_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. INDEX(activity_id, session_date DESC).
ext_practice_attendancePer-student attendance within a practice session. id UUID PK(v7), practice_log_id UUID FK(ext_practice_logs) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, status ENUM(PRESENT,ABSENT,LATE,EXCUSED) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PRESENT', notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(practice_log_id, student_id). INDEX(student_id, practice_log_id).
ext_season_plansCoach-authored season or programme plan. id UUID PK(v7), activity_id UUID FK(ext_activities) NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, goals TEXT nullable, competition_schedule TEXT nullable, training_focus TEXT nullable, created_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. UNIQUE(activity_id).
ext_awardsAward or recognition given to a student. id UUID PK(v7), activity_id UUID FK(ext_activities) NOT NULL, student_id UUID FK(sis_students) NOT NULL, award_name TEXT NOT NULL, award_type ENUM(MVP,MOST_IMPROVED,SPORTSMANSHIP,ACADEMIC_ATHLETE,LEADERSHIP,PARTICIPATION,CUSTOM) NOT NULL, academic_year_id UUID FK NOT NULL, awarded_by UUID FK(hr_employees) NOT NULL, awarded_at DATE NOT NULL, description TEXT nullable, achievement_id UUID (SOFT INTEGRITY to pfl_achievements.id nullable). INDEX(activity_id, academic_year_id). INDEX(student_id, academic_year_id).
ext_equipment_typesAthletic equipment item master — defines the types of equipment managed across activities. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Jersey', 'Football', 'Shin Guards', 'Goalkeeping Gloves'), category ENUM(CLOTHING,FOOTWEAR,PROTECTIVE,BALL,TRAINING_EQUIPMENT,OTHER) NOT NULL, requires_size BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (when true: each physical item must have a size assigned from size_options), size_options TEXT[] nullable (e.g. {'XS','S','M','L','XL'} or {'Youth 8','Youth 10','Adult 6'}), replacement_cost NUMERIC(8,2) NOT NULL (used when item is lost or unreturned — this amount is charged to the family account), normal_wear_policy TEXT nullable (description of what constitutes acceptable wear vs chargeable damage — displayed to assignee at checkout), is_consumable BOOLEAN DEFAULT false (consumables like tape are not expected to be returned). UNIQUE(school_id, name). INDEX(school_id, category).
ext_equipment_itemsIndividual physical equipment item. Each row is one real-world object with its own barcode/tag, condition, and assignment history. id UUID PK(v7), school_id UUID NOT NULL, equipment_type_id UUID FK(ext_equipment_types) NOT NULL, activity_id UUID FK(ext_activities) nullable (the activity this item primarily belongs to — null for shared pool items), barcode TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE (barcode or QR tag for scan-based check-out; generated by platform if not provided), size TEXT nullable (required when ext_equipment_types.requires_size=true), number_label TEXT nullable (for jerseys: the number on the back — e.g. '23'), condition ENUM(NEW,GOOD,FAIR,POOR,DAMAGED,WRITTEN_OFF) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'NEW', status ENUM(AVAILABLE,ASSIGNED,IN_LAUNDRY,UNDER_REPAIR,WRITTEN_OFF,LOST) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'AVAILABLE', acquisition_date DATE nullable, notes TEXT nullable. UNIQUE(school_id, barcode). INDEX(activity_id, status). INDEX(equipment_type_id, status, size) WHERE status = 'AVAILABLE' — kit assignment availability query.
